./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:5:- [fact] Read primary repository contract files outside `docs/`: `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CUSTOM-CIRCUITS.md`, `CIRCUITS.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `CLAUDE.md`; the README defines Circuit as structured, resumable, multi-phase workflow orchestration inside Claude Code. `README.md:8`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:6:- [fact] Read shipped workflow manifests for `build`, `explore`, `migrate`, `repair`, `run`, and `sweep`; generated surface docs enumerate those workflows plus utility commands. `CIRCUITS.md:11`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:7:- [fact] Read JSON schemas for manifests, events, job results, state, continuity records/indexes, and surface manifests; the manifest schema requires `schema_version` and `circuit` and rejects additional top-level properties. `schemas/circuit-manifest.schema.json:6`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:8:- [fact] Read shell relay wrappers and prompt composer; the `circuit-engine.sh` and `dispatch.sh` wrappers delegate to bundled Node CLIs. `scripts/relay/circuit-engine.sh:1`, `scripts/relay/dispatch.sh:1`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:9:- [fact] Read runtime TypeScript source for bootstrap, state derivation, command support, dispatch, checkpoint, synthesis, resume, continuity, config, catalog extraction, custom circuits, Codex isolation, and batch mutation; the runtime package declares build, typecheck, and test scripts. `scripts/runtime/engine/package.json:23`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:10:- [fact] Read architectural ratchet tests and type packages; ratchets ban direct runtime reads of `state.json` as canonical input. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/architecture-ratchets.test.ts:239`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:15:- [invariant] A circuit is not just code or prose; its identity is the pairing of `circuit.yaml` topology with `SKILL.md` execution contract, and the architecture says quality checks can silently skip if those disagree. `ARCHITECTURE.md:27`, `ARCHITECTURE.md:40`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:16:- [invariant] Durable state is event-led: runtime reads the manifest snapshot and append-only event log, derives `state.json`, and treats ledger state as stronger than artifact presence. `ARCHITECTURE.md:91`, `scripts/runtime/engine/src/derive-state.ts:1`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:25:- [invariant] A dispatch result claiming `complete` must have written every declared artifact path before the runtime will advance. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/dispatch-step.ts:453`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:26:- [invariant] Checkpoint request/resolve is file-backed: request requires a checkpoint request artifact, resolve requires a response artifact, validates selection, then routes. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/checkpoint-step.ts:158`, `scripts/runtime/engine/src/checkpoint-step.ts:234`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:28:- [invariant] Terminal states clear the indexed current run; active nonterminal states upsert it. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/command-support.ts:402`, `scripts/runtime/engine/src/continuity-control-plane.ts:445`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:32:- [invariant] Custom circuits are validated by bootstrapping a detached run and requiring manifest/events/state/active-run artifacts before publish. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/custom-circuits.ts:351`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:39:- [seam] Worker exchange is a multi-file seam: prompt/request file, receipt JSON, result JSON, and declared artifacts all need to align before a dispatch step can route.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:40:- [seam] Prompt composition is a separate contract layer: header, selected skill files, template, protocol sentinel, placeholders, and adapter hints are assembled before any model sees the task.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:42:- [seam] Continuity has two authorities with precedence rules: pending continuity records can override current-run banners and active-run resume context.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:51:- [failure-mode] Contract drift between `SKILL.md` and `circuit.yaml` can silently skip intended quality checks because the runtime advances from manifest gates, not from prose intent.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:52:- [failure-mode] Build Lite currently has visible contract drift: the manifest description says review still runs, while the generated circuit surface says Lite has no independent review. `skills/build/circuit.yaml:18`, `CIRCUITS.md:156`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:57:- [failure-mode] Dispatch receipt contracts drift across layers: the runtime-core type centers `exchange_id`, the event schema also allows adapter/transport receipts, and command support requires adapter/transport/resolved_from while inventing a `job_id` when absent. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/runtime-core/types.ts:216`, `schemas/event.schema.json:84`, `scripts/runtime/engine/src/command-support.ts:447`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:63:- [failure-mode] Section gates prove only headings and nonempty bodies, not semantic quality; a syntactically complete artifact can still be empty of useful evidence. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/complete-synthesis.ts:47`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:67:## Facts
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:69:- [fact] Circuit ships shared phases Frame, Analyze, Plan, Act, Verify, Review, Close, and Pause, and says progress is saved to disk after every phase. `README.md:73`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:70:- [fact] Canonical run artifacts include `brief.md`, `analysis.md`, `plan.md`, `review.md`, and `result.md`. `README.md:136`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:71:- [fact] Review independence is explicitly framed as a separate session with fresh knowledge of the brief and plan. `README.md:132`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:72:- [fact] Config lookup checks user/global and project config, and project config wins. `README.md:169`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:73:- [fact] Built-in adapters are `agent` and `codex`/`codex-isolated`; custom adapters append prompt and output paths to the wrapper argv. `README.md:214`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:74:- [fact] Verification prerequisites include Node 20+, expected command surface, relay scripts, config discovery, and CLI round trips. `README.md:230`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:75:- [fact] Compose-prompt searches domain skills under user global circuit skills and project-local `.claude/circuit/skills`, excluding plugin-bundled skills from domain lookup. `scripts/relay/compose-prompt.sh:31`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:76:- [fact] Compose-prompt tracks placeholder sources and fails on unresolved placeholders outside fenced code blocks. `scripts/relay/compose-prompt.sh:73`, `scripts/relay/compose-prompt.sh:113`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:77:- [fact] Compose-prompt requires the relay protocol sentinel only when it appends the relay protocol file. `scripts/relay/compose-prompt.sh:205`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:78:- [fact] Hook registration runs SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit commands synchronously. `hooks/hooks.json:1`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:79:- [fact] The UserPromptSubmit shell wrapper invokes the runtime hook with `spawnSync` and a 64 MB max buffer. `hooks/user-prompt-submit.js:3`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:80:- [fact] Hook logic does nothing unless the submitted prompt parses as a Circuit slash command. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/user-prompt-submit.ts:425`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:81:- [fact] The hook materializes `.circuit/bin` helper wrappers and makes them executable. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/user-prompt-submit.ts:120`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:82:- [fact] The prompt surface contracts file is declared as the single source for semantic command-surface fragments. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/prompt-surface-contracts.ts:1`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:83:- [fact] Surface summaries define fast-mode proof artifacts and stop conditions for smoke, review, and handoff flows. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/prompt-surface-contracts.ts:135`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:84:- [fact] Ajv 2020 with formats is used for runtime schema validation. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/schema.ts:9`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:85:- [fact] Schema root discovery walks upward until it finds the `schemas` directory. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/schema.ts:20`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:86:- [fact] Command support validates every event payload before appending. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/command-support.ts:75`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:87:- [fact] Artifact-written events are appended only for observed files that exist. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/command-support.ts:194`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:88:- [fact] Command preconditions require a current step and allowed run status, except for completed no-op routing. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/command-support.ts:258`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:89:- [fact] Route overrides must match a route declared on the manifest step. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/command-support.ts:290`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:90:- [fact] The event schema includes dispatch, job, artifact, gate, checkpoint, run completion, and invocation event types. `schemas/event.schema.json:28`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:91:- [fact] The job-result schema requires `artifacts_written`, `files_changed`, `tests`, `issues`, and `sandbox_limited`. `schemas/job-result.schema.json:5`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:92:- [fact] The state schema encodes statuses including initialized, in progress, waiting checkpoint/worker, terminal statuses, and failed. `schemas/state.schema.json:23`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:93:- [fact] Continuity records require `resume_contract.resume` to be true and `auto_resume` to be false. `schemas/continuity-record.schema.json:77`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:94:- [fact] The continuity index requires `project_root`, `current_run`, and `pending_record`. `schemas/continuity-index.schema.json:6`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:95:- [fact] Continuity status prioritizes pending records over current run context. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/continuity-commands.ts:232`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:96:- [fact] Continuity save validates typed debt bullets and disallows debt bullets in the state markdown body. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/continuity-commands.ts:342`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:97:- [fact] Continuity JSON writes are atomic temp-file renames. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/continuity-control-plane.ts:278`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:98:- [fact] Resume walks manifest order unless recorded routes exist, then follows the recorded route chain with a visited set. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/resume.ts:65`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:99:- [fact] Resume treats a job complete without gated artifacts as incomplete. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/resume.ts:123`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:100:- [fact] Custom circuit slugs cannot collide with reserved slugs, aliases, or shipped catalog entries. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/custom-circuits.ts:191`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:101:- [fact] Custom publish copies `circuit.yaml` and `SKILL.md`, removes the draft, and materializes command surfaces. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/custom-circuits.ts:390`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:102:- [fact] The catalog extractor rejects workflow skills whose `circuit.id` does not match the directory name. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/extract.ts:229`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:103:- [fact] Non-workflow utility skills without `circuit.yaml` must declare a utility or adapter role. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/catalog/extract.ts:208`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:104:- [fact] Runtime-core brands IDs and safe paths, but many public payloads still cross JSON boundaries. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/runtime-core/types.ts:1`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:105:- [fact] Definition IR has an explicit enforcement class taxonomy including runtime-enforced, resolver-enforced, adapter-enforced, receipt-audited, prompt-guidance, and prose-only. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/definition-ir/types.ts:15`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:106:- [fact] Definition IR budget policy includes allowed profiles, parallelism, rounds, premium dispatches, timeout seconds, and cap behavior. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/definition-ir/types.ts:80`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:107:- [fact] The update-batch CLI mutates batch state by appending normalized events and writing the batch file atomically. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/update-batch.ts:662`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:108:- [fact] Batch validation checks phase, type, status, current slice, and done slices with zero attempts. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/update-batch.ts:549`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:109:- [fact] Architecture ratchets ban legacy vocabulary and direct `state.json` canonical reads in live runtime and maintainer code. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/architecture-ratchets.test.ts:219`, `scripts/runtime/engine/src/architecture-ratchets.test.ts:239`
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:113:- [inference] The system is already ledger-oriented in implementation, but several command surfaces still rely on convention-heavy JSON files rather than fully schema-validated exchange contracts.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:115:- [inference] The strongest current abstraction is not "phase"; it is "ledgered step transition with declared files and gates." Phases are user-facing grouping, while events and routes are operational truth.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:116:- [inference] Custom circuits are intentionally user-extensible, but the real compatibility surface is much larger than `circuit.yaml`: prompts, generated command shims, hook context, adapter config, and active-run continuity all participate.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:119:- [inference] Existing tests act partly as architectural policy compiler: ratchets encode removed vocabulary, forbidden state reads, and known sharp edges that types alone do not prevent.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:122:- [inference] The system has converged toward "artifact content plus event proof" rather than "model says it did it" as the core correctness principle.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:129:- [unknown] I did not determine whether external orchestrator usage serializes event writes enough to make append locking unnecessary in practice.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:130:- [unknown] I did not audit every generated/public command shim for parity with prompt-surface contracts.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:131:- [unknown] I did not verify all custom adapter failure paths with actual process executions.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:135:## Abstraction-by-Abstraction Map
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:137:- [inference] Circuit definition: `circuit.yaml` is the executable topology, `SKILL.md` is the behavioral contract, schemas are the static guardrails, and catalog extraction enforces naming and role constraints.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:138:- [inference] Run identity: attached runs are project-local directories with a pinned manifest, event log, derived state, active-run dashboard, and optional continuity index entry.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:139:- [inference] Step execution: orchestrator steps complete by checking artifact sections; dispatch steps complete by reconciling worker result JSON and declared artifacts; checkpoint steps complete by selecting an allowed option.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:140:- [inference] Gates: gates are transition guards tied to artifacts and routes; the schema describes several kinds, but command-specific implementations decide which gate kinds actually execute.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:141:- [inference] Artifacts: canonical artifacts are curated state, while raw worker reports are inputs for parent synthesis unless a workflow explicitly treats them as final outputs.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:143:- [inference] Continuity: pending records are explicit resume contracts, current run is passive context, and the system deliberately avoids auto-resume.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:148:- [inference] Verification/ratchets: architectural constraints are partly enforced by tests that scan source patterns for forbidden residues, not just by types or schemas.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:155:- [inference] Keep prose contracts close to executable topology, but add mechanical drift checks for SKILL/manifests wherever possible.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:158:- [inference] Preserve the distinction between raw reports and canonical artifacts; promotion should be explicit and gated.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:159:- [inference] Continuity should remain opt-in and explicit, but the authority order between pending record, current run, and active-run dashboard must be documented in executable tests.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:163:- [inference] Keep architectural ratchets; they encode institutional memory where the type system cannot see.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:167:- [assumption] Bias acknowledged: I overweighted executable TypeScript, shell behavior, schemas, and ratchets relative to narrative design intent.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md:168:- [assumption] Bias acknowledged: I am skeptical of abstractions that do not compile, validate, or route, so I may underweight useful human-process guidance in skills.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:12:  - **Evidence.** `Workflow.entry_modes[].start_at` is only a `StepId` (`src/schemas/workflow.ts:13-18`); `Workflow.phases` and `Workflow.steps` are independent arrays (`src/schemas/workflow.ts:30-32`); `Phase.steps` is an unconstrained `StepId[]` (`src/schemas/phase.ts:15-20`); `RouteMap` stores route targets as plain `string` (`src/schemas/step.ts:28-30`). The test only covers one self-consistent happy path (`tests/contracts/schema-parity.test.ts:119-155`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:14:  - **Proposed fix.** Add a `superRefine` on `Workflow` that enforces unique ids, all `entry_modes.start_at` in `steps`, all `phases[].steps` in `steps`, every step appears in exactly one phase unless explicitly marked shared, every route target is either `@complete` or an existing `StepId`, and route labels match gate outcomes.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:48:  - **Claim.** The event union lacks enough facts to deterministically rebuild `Snapshot` from scratch, despite the abstraction inventory saying `state.json` is reducer-derived and regenerable from `events.ndjson`.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:49:  - **Evidence.** Inventory states snapshot regeneration is required (`bootstrap/abstraction-inventory.md:184-190`). `Snapshot.steps[].status` includes `pending`, `in_progress`, `gate_failed`, `complete`, and `aborted` (`src/schemas/snapshot.ts:5-14`), but the event union has no `step.completed`, `step.aborted`, `route.selected`, or `workflow.manifest_recorded` event (`src/schemas/event.ts:89-99`). `GateEvaluatedEvent` has only generic `outcome`, optional `missing_sections`, and optional `reason` (`src/schemas/event.ts:37-44`), so replay cannot know which gate definition was evaluated. `DispatchCompletedEvent` has a verdict and duration, but no result path, receipt path, model, effort, skills, or output schema (`src/schemas/event.ts:73-79`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:51:  - **Proposed fix.** Treat replay as a first-class contract. Add events for manifest identity, step completion, route selection, dispatch failure, checkpoint materialization, and gate definition/outcome. Include enough resolved selection and artifact/result paths to replay without reading mutable config.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:95:  - **Evidence.** Existing config supports `dispatch.adapters.gemini.command` (`/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit/circuit.config.example.yaml:32-37`) and role/circuit values as adapter names (`/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit/circuit.config.example.yaml:23-31`). New `DispatchConfig` has only `default`, `roles`, and `circuits`, with no `adapters` registry (`src/schemas/config.ts:7-11`). `BuiltInAdapter` only includes `agent` and `codex` (`src/schemas/adapter.ts:3-4`), while the documented built-in surface includes `codex-isolated` as the real adapter and `codex` as an alias (`/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit/circuit.config.example.yaml:16-20`; `bootstrap/abstraction-inventory.md:127-135`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:101:  - **Evidence.** `Step` only refines `orchestrator -> synthesis|checkpoint` and `worker -> dispatch` (`src/schemas/step.ts:53-64`). `role` is optional on all steps (`src/schemas/step.ts:44`). `Role` includes `orchestrator` (`src/schemas/role.ts:3-4`) even though the surface inventory names only `researcher`, `implementer`, and `reviewer` as dispatch roles (`bootstrap/abstraction-inventory.md:141-149`). `gate` is required but can be any gate kind on any step kind (`src/schemas/step.ts:42`; `src/schemas/gate.ts:24-28`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:102:  - **Why it matters.** A worker dispatch step can omit role; a worker can be assigned `orchestrator`; a checkpoint step can use a `result_verdict` gate; a dispatch step can use a `schema_sections` gate against an artifact it never writes. Those are invalid runtime states masquerading as valid manifests.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:121:- **Gate Source Is An Opaque String Instead Of A Contract Reference.** (Severity: MED)
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:122:  - **Claim.** Gates name `source` as arbitrary strings and are not tied to the step's declared writes or artifact schemas.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:124:  - **Why it matters.** Gates can point at files the step does not produce, can accidentally validate a stale artifact, and cannot express "the source is this step's artifact with schema X" at type level.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:125:  - **Proposed fix.** Replace opaque `source` with a typed source union: `{ kind: 'artifact'; ref: 'writes.artifact' }`, `{ kind: 'checkpoint_response'; ref: 'writes.response' }`, `{ kind: 'dispatch_result'; ref: 'writes.result' }`, with a step-level refinement that the referenced write exists.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:131:  - **Proposed fix.** Delete the helper from the skeleton until rigor semantics are specified, or rename it to the exact property it encodes. If it is intended for escalation, include `autonomous`.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:134:  - **Claim.** `ContinuityRecord` can say it is standalone while carrying a run reference, or run-backed without one. `ResumeContract.mode` can contradict `continuity_kind`.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:135:  - **Evidence.** `continuity_kind` is an enum (`src/schemas/continuity.ts:31`), `run_ref` is optional regardless of kind (`src/schemas/continuity.ts:33-38`), and `resume_contract.mode` independently chooses `resume_run` or `resume_standalone` (`src/schemas/continuity.ts:20-24`). The inventory says continuity has two kinds, with run-backed anchored to an in-progress run (`bootstrap/abstraction-inventory.md:193-202`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:136:  - **Why it matters.** Resume can attach to the wrong execution path or silently drop a run anchor. Continuity is a recovery mechanism; contradictions here become expensive during exactly the sessions where context is already fragile.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:152:  - **Claim.** `ModelTier` combines provider-specific marketing names and abstract tiers, while `Effort` has values that do not line up with all supported adapters.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:153:  - **Evidence.** `ModelTier` is `haiku`, `sonnet`, `opus`, `gpt-5`, `gpt-5-pro`, `gemini-pro` (`src/schemas/selection-policy.ts:5`). `Effort` is `low`, `medium`, `high`, `max` (`src/schemas/selection-policy.ts:8`). The adapter inventory explicitly spans Claude Agent, Codex CLI, custom wrappers, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini surfaces (`bootstrap/abstraction-inventory.md:127-139`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:158:  - **Claim.** The contract test suite mostly proves permissiveness rather than invariants.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:159:  - **Evidence.** It asserts `SelectionPolicy.safeParse({})` succeeds (`tests/contracts/schema-parity.test.ts:63-68`), a snapshot with a completed step and no corresponding event succeeds (`tests/contracts/schema-parity.test.ts:171-183`), and a workflow with a single phase/step succeeds (`tests/contracts/schema-parity.test.ts:119-155`). There are no negative tests for broken graph references, invalid gate/step combos, contradictory continuity records, or config precedence ambiguity.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:161:  - **Proposed fix.** Add negative contract tests for every cross-record invariant before implementation code depends on the permissive behavior.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:194:- **Step variant legality.** Orchestrator synthesis writes artifacts and uses schema-section gates; orchestrator checkpoint writes request/response and uses checkpoint-selection gates; worker dispatch has a worker role, writes receipt/result, and uses result-verdict gates.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:222:- **Event replay sufficiency.** A reducer should be able to reconstruct `Snapshot` without consulting mutable config or artifacts except where an event explicitly names an immutable content hash/path.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:253:- Three gate kinds mirror current file artifacts, checkpoint responses, and worker verdicts; the skeleton does not ask whether "gate = predicate over typed output" would collapse all three.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:256:- Workflow `default_skills` plus config `circuits.*.skills` plus step `selection.skills` duplicates the existing skill injection channels instead of defining one canonical skill override contract.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:263:- Runtime schemas and exported inferred types are co-located, reducing drift between compile-time and runtime contracts.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:264:- `ProtocolId` includes an explicit version suffix, which is defensible for artifact/protocol evolution (`src/schemas/ids.ts:26-30`).
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:271:- I did not run the test suite; this was a type-contract adversarial pass, not implementation verification.
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:277:- I may be treating the skeleton as a near-public contract when the operator intended it as a disposable Phase 0 sketch. Claude could make the same category error if it reads "schema" as "contract."
./bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md:279:- I may be too suspicious of old Circuit's abstractions and therefore attack useful continuity with the prior system. A Claude reviewer trained on the same artifacts may share the "rewrite should shed complexity" prior.

./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:5:Files read (full or partial). Counts are `wc -l`-style where known; `~` means partial read. Each line is labeled `[fact]` because it records a verifiable reading step with a citation.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:7:- `[fact]` Read `~/Code/circuit/ARCHITECTURE.md` (1041 lines, full) — canonical internal architecture reference. Source: `~/Code/circuit/ARCHITECTURE.md:1-1041`.

./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:58:- `[fact]` Read `~/Code/circuit/commands/run.md` (42 lines, full) and `commands/build.md` (27 lines, full) — slash-command shims. Source: `commands/run.md:1-42` and `commands/build.md:1-27`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:59:- `[fact]` `~/Code/circuit/CLAUDE.md` shown as system-reminder context — compiler-owned files warning and bundled-CLI rule. Source: `CLAUDE.md` (system-reminder).
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:60:- `[fact]` Read `~/Code/circuit/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (24 lines, full) — plugin manifest. Source: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-24`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:61:- `[fact]` Intentionally NOT read, per blind-pass constraint: any file under `~/Code/circuit/docs/` and `~/Code/circuit-next/bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md`. Source: mission brief at `bootstrap/brief.md:37-46`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:66:- `[invariant]` `events.ndjson` is append-only and the one true source of run progression. Evidence: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/append-event.ts:82-88` (appendFileSync only) and `derive-state.ts:41-51` (parse per-line). Ledger-over-artifacts is stated explicitly in `ARCHITECTURE.md:97-101` ("Ledger state takes precedence over artifact presence").
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:67:- `[invariant]` `circuit.manifest.yaml` is snapshotted into the run root at bootstrap and mutations are forbidden; on re-bootstrap the content must match exactly. Evidence: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/bootstrap.ts:128-138` throws on "manifest snapshot mismatch". Matters because state replay depends on a stable step/gate graph.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:68:- `[invariant]` Attached run roots must live exactly at `<project>/.circuit/circuit-runs/<slug>`; enforced with realpath canonicalization. Evidence: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/bootstrap.ts:51-68` (`assertAttachedRunRoot`). Keeps continuity index relative paths meaningful and prevents cross-project attachment.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:69:- `[invariant]` Two artifact tiers: canonical `artifacts/*.md` vs worker-private `phases/<step>/reports/*.md`. Parent circuits never promote worker reports directly. Evidence: `ARCHITECTURE.md:104-115` and `ARCHITECTURE.md:545-572` (workers-execute@v1 public vs private file list). Load-bearing because without this boundary, worker rewrites break parent circuits.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:76:- `[invariant]` Dispatch public roles are exactly `implementer | reviewer | researcher`. Evidence: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/dispatch.ts:16-18, 82-94`. Unknown role throws at config-parse time.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:78:- `[invariant]` Every run step produces a named artifact on a well-known path; consumers declare `reads`. `reads` is structurally constrained to known source classes (`user.task`, `repo.{snapshot,diff}`, `artifacts/...`, `checkpoints/...`, `jobs/...`, `handoffs/...`, `entry.*`, `referenced:...`). Evidence: `schemas/circuit-manifest.schema.json:194-197` and `CUSTOM-CIRCUITS.md:181-193`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:80:- `[invariant]` All templates that inline the relay protocol must carry the exact sentinel `<!-- circuit:relay-protocol-inline -->`; absence triggers legacy append of `relay-protocol.md`. Evidence: `scripts/relay/compose-prompt.sh:205-212` and `skills/workers/references/implement-template.md:1`. Sentinel-based detection prevents heuristic misses.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:87:- `[seam]` Orchestrator ↔ worker contract (Public/Private). Public: `jobs/*.{request,receipt,result}.json`, `reports/report*.md`, `reports/report-converge.md`. Private: `batch.json`, `plan.json`, `events.ndjson`, `review-findings/*`. Evidence: `ARCHITECTURE.md:551-572`. Implication: circuit-next must pick whether to preserve this double-sided contract or collapse to a single structured interface.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:91:- `[seam]` Continuity control plane ↔ SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit. `.circuit/control-plane/continuity-index.json` has nullable `current_run` + `pending_record` fields; SessionStart can refresh `active-run.md` or show welcome banner. Evidence: `schemas/continuity-index.schema.json:13-77` + `ARCHITECTURE.md:139-152`. Implication: cross-session memory is a first-class schema, not transient UI state.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:95:- `[seam]` Slash-command shim ↔ skill ↔ bootstrap. Each `/circuit:<name>` resolves to `commands/<name>.md` (generated shim), which points at the skill, which calls `.circuit/bin/circuit-engine bootstrap`. Evidence: `commands/run.md:33-41` + `skills/build/SKILL.md:20-45`. Implication: multiple layers of indirection per invocation, each with its own contract.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:104:- `[failure-mode]` Workflow manifests share ~80% of the frame-checkpoint step and all emit the same artifact paths, but each is a separate `circuit.yaml` file. The `run` workflow's manifest is a single-step "just write active-run.md" circuit (`skills/run/circuit.yaml:22-37`). Evidence: compare `build/circuit.yaml`, `repair/circuit.yaml`, `explore/circuit.yaml`, `sweep/circuit.yaml` — the gate/checkpoint pattern is heavily duplicated. Organic: one-graph-per-workflow was an intentional constraint (ARCHITECTURE.md:701-705), but it rules out factoring the spine into shared phases.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:105:- `[failure-mode]` `build/SKILL.md` is 522 lines of prose-as-contract, densely interleaving bash snippets, cat heredocs, and rigor-gating tables. Evidence: `skills/build/SKILL.md:155-340` (Frame through Act). Claude follows this as the executable program, but it's human-edited markdown with no internal consistency check. Hotspot because: each step's bash is roughly similar but with subtle differences (paths, adapter names, JSON payloads).
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:107:- `[failure-mode]` Resume logic blends `routes` (durable, from events) + artifact gate status + job status + checkpoint status with branching reasons. Evidence: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/resume.ts:129-293`. It has 7+ distinct "reason" strings with non-trivial cascading logic — a readable-but-fragile algorithm to pick "what to do next".
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:109:- `[failure-mode]` The `codex-runtime.ts` module (started at line 1, 150 lines previewed) implements a full PID-based janitor for orphaned Codex subprocesses, with cleanup actions, owned-artifact tracking, sandboxed CODEX_HOME copying of `auth.json`, and SANITIZED_ENV_ALLOWLIST. Evidence: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/codex-runtime.ts:53-150`. This solves a real integration problem (Codex CLI leaves state) but is circuit-specific plumbing that could rot if Codex internals change.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:110:- `[failure-mode]` Gate resolution happens at multiple layers. `schemas/circuit-manifest.schema.json:264-341` enumerates gate kinds. `scripts/runtime/engine/src/resume.ts:235-279` derives gate satisfaction from jobs vs. routes. `runtime-core/types.ts:677-714` has a fully-branded second model of gates (`SchemaSectionsGate`, `AllOutputsPresentGate`, etc.) with slightly different names. Consistency between these three views is manual.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:111:- `[failure-mode]` The per-protocol verdict conditionals in `job-result.schema.json:281-536` are a ~250-line wall of `if protocol == X then verdict in [...]`. Any new protocol requires editing this file; any missing protocol is silently untyped. Organic: easier to add a protocol than to refactor the indexing.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:113:## Facts
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:115:- `[fact]` Plugin version is `0.3.0` per `.claude-plugin/plugin.json:4`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:116:- `[fact]` Five public workflow circuits: `build`, `explore`, `migrate`, `repair`, `sweep`; plus router `run`; plus utilities `create`, `handoff`, `review`; plus internal adapter `workers`. Source: `ls skills/` (enumerated in Bash output) and `CIRCUITS.md:13-30`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:117:- `[fact]` Event types (12): `run_started, step_started, dispatch_requested, dispatch_received, job_completed, artifact_written, gate_passed, gate_failed, checkpoint_requested, checkpoint_resolved, run_completed, run_aborted`. Source: `schemas/event.schema.json:29-43`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:118:- `[fact]` Runtime statuses (10): `initialized, in_progress, waiting_checkpoint, waiting_worker, aborted, completed, stopped, blocked, failed, handed_off`. Source: `schemas/state.schema.json:24-36`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:119:- `[fact]` The `workers` skill runs `plan -> implement -> review -> converge` with circuit-breaker thresholds `impl_attempts > 3` OR `impl_attempts + review_rejections > 5`. Source: `skills/workers/SKILL.md:167-171`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:120:- `[fact]` Rigor profiles are `Lite, Standard, Deep, Tournament, Autonomous`, declared in `entry_modes.*.rigor` per schema: `schemas/circuit-manifest.schema.json:78-84`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:121:- `[fact]` `circuit.config.yaml` precedence: first match in ancestors from cwd up to git-toplevel; then `~/.claude/circuit.config.yaml`. Source: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/config.ts:71-107`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:122:- `[fact]` Dispatch receipts include `adapter`, `transport`, `resolved_from`, and `agent_params` OR `command_argv` depending on transport; can include `adapter_fallback` when start failure triggers automatic agent fallback. Source: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/dispatch.ts:49-62, 398-453`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:123:- `[fact]` Automatic adapter fallback is capped at one retry per attempt: start-failures (missing binary, ENOBUFS, etc.) fall through to `agent`; worker-exit-nonzero does not fall back. Source: `dispatch.ts:388-453` plus comment at line 383-387.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:124:- `[fact]` Per-protocol verdict vocabulary is constrained in `schemas/job-result.schema.json` with ~30 protocol identifiers like `workers-execute@v1`, `seam-proof@v1`, `parallel-review-fanout@v1`. Source: `schemas/job-result.schema.json:281-534`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:125:- `[fact]` Skill composition at dispatch time is via `--skills <csv>`; skills resolve against `CIRCUIT_PLUGIN_SKILL_DIR` (env) then `~/.claude/skills`. Plugin's own `skills/` is intentionally excluded. Source: `scripts/relay/compose-prompt.sh:31-54`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:126:- `[fact]` Catalog compiler regenerates `commands/*.md`, delimited regions of `CIRCUITS.md`, delimited regions of `skills/*/SKILL.md`, `.claude-plugin/public-commands.txt`, and `scripts/runtime/generated/*.json`. Source: CLAUDE.md (system-reminder).
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:127:- `[fact]` The `runtime-core/types.ts` module uses TypeScript branded types (e.g. `type RunId = Brand<string, "RunId">`) across ~50 domain identifiers. Source: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/runtime-core/types.ts:1-68`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:128:- `[fact]` Slash-command shim files (`commands/*.md`) all follow the same ~30-line template: frontmatter description + "## Purpose" + "## Examples" + "## Bootstrap Contract" that points Claude to launch the relevant skill. Source: `commands/run.md:1-42` + `commands/build.md:1-27`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:129:- `[fact]` Node major is runtime-checked. Minimum is 20. Source: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/cli/circuit-engine.ts:31, 283-288`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:130:- `[fact]` The continuity record v1 schema mandates `resume_contract.auto_resume = false` AND `requires_explicit_resume = true` as constants. Source: `schemas/continuity-record.schema.json:82-88`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:131:- `[fact]` `definition-ir` module is marked `schema_version: "3-experimental"` and defines a much richer domain model (`PortableWorkIntent`, `SkillPolicy`, `DomainSkillPolicy`) that is not wired up to the current runtime. Source: `scripts/runtime/engine/src/definition-ir/types.ts:1-80`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:132:- `[fact]` `active-run.md` is regenerated from state; it is not hand-edited and is a "passive runtime dashboard". Source: `ARCHITECTURE.md:140-152, 165`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:133:- `[fact]` SessionStart matcher fires on `startup|resume|clear|compact`. UserPromptSubmit fires on every prompt. Source: `hooks/hooks.json:5-21`.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:137:- `[inference]` Circuit is designed around the twin philosophies of "artifacts are state" and "events are history". Together they let a session crash at any point and still recover because the next session derives everything from events + snapshotted manifest. Basis: `derive-state.ts`, `resume.ts`, append-only `events.ndjson`, `circuit.manifest.yaml` snapshot rule. This is the foundational bet of v2.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:139:- `[inference]` The "runtime-core" directory plus `definition-ir` directory plus `catalog/` directory suggest circuit is mid-migration to a cleaner architecture but hasn't completed it. The classic runtime (`bootstrap.ts`, `derive-state.ts`, `dispatch-step.ts`) is what SKILL.md prose actually invokes via `.circuit/bin/circuit-engine`. Basis: CLI dispatch in `cli/circuit-engine.ts` imports from classic modules, not from `runtime-core/`. The newer layers look like scaffolding for a v3.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:140:- `[inference]` The `/circuit:run` router is intentionally thin (one step, writes `active-run.md`, routes by prose rules in SKILL.md) because classification is a fuzzy task that benefits from the current session's context. Basis: `skills/run/circuit.yaml:22-37` is one synthesis step; all classification logic lives in `skills/run/SKILL.md` prose. This is a deliberate non-formalization.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:145:- `[inference]` The fact that adapter fallback is capped at one retry (explicitly commented) suggests there was a bug where repeated start-failures led to loops. Basis: `dispatch.ts:382-396` comment is an annotated review finding ("Review finding I5").
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:151:- `[unknown]` How custom circuits integrate with the router's signal matching — whether `entry.signals.include` from user-global circuits is considered alongside built-ins by the `run` skill at classification time. `user-prompt-submit.ts:534-549` shows a `renderRunCustomCircuitContext` path that injects overlay info, but the actual match logic is skill prose.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:152:- `[unknown]` Whether `repo.snapshot` or `repo.diff` in `reads:` is actually materialized by the runtime or only consumed by skill prose as a hint. The read-ref grammar permits them but I saw no runtime handler.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:155:- `[unknown]` How the `plan.json` (worker-private, per `ARCHITECTURE.md:569`) interacts with `batch.json` — is it a write-once plan or is there a re-plan flow?
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:160:## Abstraction-by-Abstraction Map
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:162:**Workflow orchestration.** DOES: encode a stepwise DAG per workflow in `circuit.yaml` with `reads`/`writes`/`gate`/`routes`. Routes support normal next-step and terminal `@complete|@stop|@escalate|@handoff`. Resume is derived from replay. DOES NOT: support same-run workflow-to-workflow transfers (Build → Explore is explicitly not v1; the human must restart; `skills/build/SKILL.md:225-229`). HOTSPOT: `resume.ts:129-293` with its layered completeness logic (routes, artifact gate, jobs, checkpoints). QUOTE: "Build is the first workflow where the authored fixed graph and the outer runtime now meet on the real execution path" (`ARCHITECTURE.md:637-638`).
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:164:**Event-sourced state.** DOES: append-only `events.ndjson` + manifest snapshot + pure projection `deriveState`. Events are schema-validated with per-type conditional payloads (Ajv Draft 2020-12). DOES NOT: carry a concurrency control / CAS mechanism in the classic path (the `runtime-core/commit-ledger.ts` has `expectedRevision` + `expected_revision_mismatch` failure, which isn't used by the classic path). HOTSPOT: `derive-state.ts:56-292` — a 230-line switch statement. QUOTE: "Ledger state takes precedence over artifact presence. A step might have produced an artifact ... but resume must not advance from uncommitted local files" (`ARCHITECTURE.md:97-101`).
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:166:**Plugin surface.** DOES: exposes 9 public slash commands (`run, build, explore, repair, migrate, sweep, create, review, handoff`), hooks for `SessionStart` + `UserPromptSubmit`, and two shim dirs (`scripts/relay/` and hook-authored `.circuit/bin/`). Commands are generated from `catalog/prompt-surface-contracts.ts`. DOES NOT: expose MCP servers (none present in `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`); does not register slash commands dynamically beyond overlay manifest. HOTSPOT: the catalog-compiler system (regions with `<!-- BEGIN X -->` / `<!-- END X -->`) — powerful but anti-pattern to hand-edit.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:172:**Cross-model prompting.** DOES: adapter dispatch supports (a) Claude Code Agent, (b) Codex CLI in isolated sandbox, (c) user-defined wrapper argv. All produce a uniform receipt JSON. DOES NOT: adapt the prompt per adapter; the prompt is identical except for an optional `<!-- dispatch-adapter: X -->` HTML comment hint (`compose-prompt.sh:315-320`). HOTSPOT: `codex-runtime.ts` ~hundreds of lines for isolation/cleanup. QUOTE: "The implement/review/converge loop, artifact chain, gates, and report format are identical regardless of adapter" (`skills/workers/SKILL.md:60-62`).
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:174:**Checkpoint / pause.** DOES: `kind: checkpoint` step with `checkpoint.options` + gate `checkpoint_selection`. Request/response JSON files written per-attempt. Rigor profiles gate whether the checkpoint actually blocks (skill prose decides). DOES NOT: support multi-question checkpoints or structured responses beyond `{selection}` (schema-constrained; `schemas/circuit-manifest.schema.json:250-262`). HOTSPOT: `checkpoint-step.ts` handles both request and resolve; prose in `SKILL.md` decides when to actually wait.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:176:**Model/effort/skill-selection policy.** DOES: per-circuit skill override in config. Adapter resolution (which model runtime) via role/circuit/default. DOES NOT: support per-step skill/model selection in manifests (the `capabilities.required/optional` field in the step schema exists but I did not find it consumed); does not surface explicit model identifiers (workers run on whatever the adapter chooses). HOTSPOT: the gap between the schema's declarative `capabilities` and actual runtime behavior.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:187:- `[inference]` Ledger + manifest snapshot + pure projection as the foundation of state ("artifacts are state, events are history"). Basis: Invariants on event log, manifest snapshot, derived state.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:191:- `[inference]` Separation of canonical artifacts from worker-private relay state. Basis: Invariant on two-tier artifact model and seam on orchestrator↔worker contract.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:201:- `[inference]` The `run` skill having a `circuit.yaml` at all for its single-step "just write a markdown file" flow — it's a shim pretending to be a circuit. Basis: Fact on `skills/run/circuit.yaml` having one synthesis step.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:213:- `[fact]` Stayed out of `docs/`: **Yes.** I did not Read, Grep, Glob, or otherwise touch any file under `~/Code/circuit/docs/`. `ls -la ~/Code/circuit/` showed the directory existed (visible in output) but I did not descend into it. ARCHITECTURE.md has inline references to `docs/literate-guide.md`, `docs/control-plane-ownership.md`, `docs/workflow-matrix.md`, `docs/continuity-control-plane-rfc.md` — I noticed these in passing but did not follow any. I was tempted to glance at `docs/literate-guide.md` because ARCHITECTURE.md pitched it as the narrative walkthrough, but I did not.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:214:- `[fact]` Avoided the sibling Codex draft: **Yes.** I did not Read `~/Code/circuit-next/bootstrap/evidence-draft-codex.md`. I verified the target directory was empty (only `.` and `..`) before writing this draft.
./bootstrap/evidence-draft-claude.md:215:- `[assumption]` Bias: over-weight on documentation over code. ARCHITECTURE.md and CIRCUITS.md were both very well-written and I leaned on them heavily. A Codex-first read might weight the TypeScript sources more. I tried to cross-check by actually reading `dispatch.ts`, `derive-state.ts`, `bootstrap.ts`, `resume.ts`, `config.ts`, `cli/user-prompt-submit.ts`, and `runtime-core/types.ts` — so claims are generally dual-sourced, but the framing (artifact chain, gate system, phase spine) is essentially the narrative the docs told me.
./specs/ratchet-floor.json:4:    "contract_test_count": 740
./specs/ratchet-floor.json:8:  "notes": "Pinned ratchet floors that must not regress. The contract_test_count metric is the static declaration count produced by scripts/audit.mjs::countTests (matches /^\\s*(it|test)\\(/gm across git-tracked tests/**/*.test.* files), not the vitest runtime total; the audit uses the static count so the ratchet is deterministic without shelling into vitest. Advancement is an explicit commit action; HEAD~1 moving-window comparison is insufficient (docs-only commits between regression and audit would hide the regression). Close gates depend on this file, not on HEAD~1 alone. See specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md §Slice 26b and ADR-0002 Addendum A. Initial floor 574 pinned post-Slice-26b (post-Codex-fold-in) HEAD static count — per Codex HIGH-1 fold-in on the Slice 26b diff, pinning below the post-slice count leaves the moving-window hole open (trivial revert of the new test file drops HEAD back to the floor and Check 19 stays green). Slice 33 (P2.2) adds tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts (+26 static test declarations for plugin-command-closure enforcement per ADR-0007 CC#P2-3); floor advanced from 574 → 727 in the same Ratchet-Advance slice, closing the moving-window hole per Codex P2.2 MED 7 fold-in. Metadata fields (schema_version, last_advanced_at, last_advanced_in_slice) are audit-enforced via validatePinnedRatchetFloorData; floor zero or negative is rejected (Codex HIGH-2 fold-in). Future slices raise the floor only as an explicit ratchet-advance act."

./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:10:blind internal Circuit extraction (Workers C + D) — before being treated as
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:11:contracts to build on.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:14:(`artifacts/analysis.md`) and AFTER both internal-evidence drafts
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:87:- **Audit verdict**: Adopt the principle; defer the specific profile names to Phase 1 contract authorship based on circuit-next's actual workflow inventory.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:93:- **Audit verdict**: Real principle; the proposed compiler is heavier than needed for what the proof slice actually demonstrates.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:103:- **Audit verdict**: Survives. But the fix is not necessarily a full compiler — it could be (a) a stricter schema that forbids prose-encoded policy at key decision points, plus (b) a contract test that enforces prose/YAML parity.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:110:- **Audit verdict**: The proposal is not wrong, but the evidence does not support making it the foundation of the rewrite. **Take the principles, defer the IR + compiler** until after Phase 1 contract authorship exposes the actual boundary shape.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:118:- External evidence has a clear answer from Codex AGENTS.md + npm: **precedence is a first-class concern**. circuit-next's `SelectionResolution` + `SELECTION_PRECEDENCE` (see `src/schemas/selection-policy.ts`) is the minimum contract that resolves this objection.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:147:- The introduction of `WorkPatternIR` as a cross-workflow abstraction — unsupported; no workflow besides Sweep has been shown to benefit.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:151:1. **Adopt the principles, defer the IR + compiler.** circuit-next's Phase 1 contract authorship should encode:
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:156:2. **Do NOT introduce `WorkPatternIR` in Tier 0 or Tier 1.** Wait until Phase 1 contracts expose whether the current discriminated-union Step + event log is insufficient.
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:157:3. **Enforce prose/YAML parity structurally** — contract test that every `SKILL.md` matches its `circuit.yaml` (adversarial-review #6 + internal evidence from Worker D).
./bootstrap/prior-art-audit.md:158:4. **Treat the 4 prior-art docs as historical artifacts**, not active contracts. They record valuable thinking but were written before the methodology tournament and before cross-model auditing. The tournament decision (now at `specs/methodology/decision.md`) is authoritative.

./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:21:  - tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts (Slice 24 diff surface)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:22:  - specs/contracts/step.md (grandfathered frontmatter)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:23:  - specs/contracts/workflow.md (grandfathered frontmatter)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:27:  - unrelated contract bodies (targeted pass)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:35:REJECT pending HIGH fold-ins. Slice 24 closes the direct "new basename adds `codex_adversarial_review_grandfathered`" bypass, but the remaining CHALLENGER-I3 surface is still too name- and prose-shaped: forward links are not constrained to `specs/reviews/`, grandfathered identity is keyed by basename rather than contract identity, and the three new fields are presence checks more than renewal machinery.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:41:- Affected-claim/artifact: CHALLENGER-I3 claim that new contracts must use `codex_adversarial_review` to link an auditable `specs/reviews/<target>-md-v<version>-codex.md` record.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:42:- Reproduction-path: `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:527-536` only checks that `codex_adversarial_review` resolves to an existing path, and `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:540-555` only parses that resolved file's `contract_target`. It does not require the path to live under `specs/reviews/`, match the contract-review filename pattern, or be one of `listReviewFiles()`. The track spec requires a review file under `specs/reviews/` at `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:55-64`; the Slice 24 failure message repeats that expectation at `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:479-483`.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:43:- Proposed remediation: require `codex_adversarial_review` to match `^specs/reviews/[a-z0-9-]+-md-v\\d+\\.\\d+-codex\\.md$`, resolve under `REVIEWS_DIR`, classify as a contract review, and carry the full contract-review frontmatter. Add a negative fixture in the test that demonstrates a self-linking contract with `contract_target` in its own frontmatter fails.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:46:**2. HIGH — The grandfather allowlist binds a basename, not the historical contract identity.**
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:48:- Affected-claim/artifact: `GRANDFATHERED_CONTRACT_ALLOWLIST` as evidence that no new contract can use the grandfathered escape hatch.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:49:- Reproduction-path: `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:26` allowlists only `step.md` and `workflow.md`; `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:469-475` compares only `basename(contractPath)`. The historical identity fields are present but unchecked: `specs/contracts/step.md:2-10` says `contract: step`, `version: 0.1`, `schema_source: src/schemas/step.ts`, and the Slice 2 scope; `specs/contracts/workflow.md:2-10` says `contract: workflow`, `version: 0.1`, `schema_source: src/schemas/workflow.ts`, and the skeleton scope. A future edit can keep the filename `step.md` while changing the contract identity, schema source, or version and still pass the allowlist test.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:50:- Proposed remediation: make the allowlist a typed record keyed by canonical relative path and expected frontmatter, e.g. `{ path, contract, version, schema_source, artifact_ids, grandfathered_source_ref }`. Assert all expected fields exactly, and use `lstatSync`/`realpathSync` or `git ls-files -s` to reject symlink/path-substitution games for allowlisted entries.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:53:**3. MED — `expires_on_contract_change: true` is asserted but never made operative.**
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:55:- Affected-claim/artifact: the "expires on material change" part of the HIGH #9 remediation and Slice 24 commit claim.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:56:- Reproduction-path: `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:487-495` says the field forces renewal, but `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:501-514` only checks that the string value is exactly `true`. The actual frontmatter at `specs/contracts/step.md:10-11` and `specs/contracts/workflow.md:10-11` names change-triggered exit conditions, but no test compares current contract contents, version, schema source, invariant ids, or schema dependencies against the grandfathered baseline.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:57:- Proposed remediation: either rename the field to `expires_on_contract_change_ack: true` so it is honest prose, or make it enforceable: forbid grandfathering when `version !== 0.1`, when `schema_source` differs from the allowlist record, when numbered invariant ids change, or when the schema files named by the scope have changed without a new review.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:62:- Affected-claim/artifact: HIGH #9 requirement that the source ref be concrete review evidence, not another prose assertion.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:63:- Reproduction-path: `specs/reviews/arc-phase-1-close-codex.md:216-219` specifically asks for a resolvable commit/path. Slice 24 checks only non-empty text at `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:501-512`. The `step.md` ref mixes stable commit `f5a6241` with mutable `PROJECT_STATE.md` prose at `specs/contracts/step.md:9`; `workflow.md` mixes `bootstrap/adversarial-review-codex.md` with mutable `PROJECT_STATE.md` prose at `specs/contracts/workflow.md:9`.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:69:- Affected-claim/artifact: the new scope field as a guard against grandfathering drifting from historical coverage to general exemption.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:70:- Reproduction-path: `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:501-514` requires only non-empty text for `grandfathered_scope`. The scope bodies at `specs/contracts/step.md:10` and `specs/contracts/workflow.md:10` are currently specific, but a future edit can broaden them to "all future v0.x contract changes" and still pass.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:71:- Proposed remediation: add machine-readable scope tokens alongside prose. For example, `grandfathered_scope_ids: [STEP-I1, STEP-I3, STEP-I4, STEP-I6, step.variant-set:v0.1, gate.source.literal-ref]` and `grandfathered_scope_ids: [WF-I1, WF-I2, WF-I3, WF-I4, WF-I5, WF-I6, WF-I7, workflow.schema_version:2]`; assert exact set membership against known allowlist records or against invariant headings in the contract body.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:76:- Affected-claim/artifact: the planned transition from grandfathered record to normal `codex_adversarial_review` record for `step.md` and `workflow.md`.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:77:- Reproduction-path: `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:433-441` accepts a contract when either field exists, but it does not forbid both. The allowlist comments describe exit at `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:22-25`; the test at `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:469-475` still permits the grandfathered field on allowlisted files even after a forward review link is added. HIGH #9's operator response says workflow should remove the grandfathered declaration on close at `specs/reviews/arc-phase-1-close-codex.md:451-452`.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:78:- Proposed remediation: add an XOR invariant: a contract may carry exactly one of `codex_adversarial_review` or `codex_adversarial_review_grandfathered`. If a proper review lands, the grandfathered field and allowlist entry must be removed in the same slice.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:83:- Affected-claim/artifact: `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md` as the durable CHALLENGER-I3 contract.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:84:- Reproduction-path: the frontmatter still says the test was tightened only through Slice 21 at `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:14-16`. The planned-test prose still says every contract may use `codex_adversarial_review_grandfathered: <rationale>` with a 20-character prose declaration at `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:136-140`, with no allowlist, source-ref, scope, or expiry fields.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:85:- Proposed remediation: update the track spec in the same slice: name the Slice 24 tightening, state the grandfathered path is closed to all contracts except `step.md` and `workflow.md`, and document the required structured fields plus their intended enforcement semantics.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:90:- Affected-claim/artifact: `step.md` grandfathered evidence alignment.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:91:- Reproduction-path: `specs/contracts/step.md:10` says the grandfathered scope includes "the Gate.source typed reference" but the exit condition names changes to `schema_source src/schemas/step.ts`. The typed `Gate.source` evidence actually lives in `src/schemas/gate.ts:3-7` and in the gate variants at `src/schemas/gate.ts:49-79`; `src/schemas/step.ts:86-98` only performs the union-level `gate.source.ref` closure refinement.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:97:- Affected-claim/artifact: redundant enforcement and Slice 24 acceptance evidence that `npm run audit` is green.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-24-codex.md:98:- Reproduction-path: `package.json:12-17` separates `npm run test`/`npm run verify` from `npm run audit`. The audit's contract loop at `scripts/audit.mjs:736-789` checks `artifact_ids` reciprocity, not `codex_adversarial_review`, grandfathered allowlists, source refs, or scope expiry. The track spec explicitly keeps the challenger audit dimension unlanded at `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:164-169`.

./specs/invariants.json:3:  "description": "Contract + behavioral-track invariant/property ledger. Closes AR-M2 from specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md. Scope: specs/contracts/*.md + specs/behavioral/*.md. Out of scope: CLAUDE.md hard invariants, specs/evidence.md invariants, ADR-level invariants (documented exclusion per Slice 25 Codex challenger MED #9 fold-in; deferred to v0.2).",
./specs/invariants.json:5:    "test-enforced": "Invariant id appears as a token in at least one binding_ref test-file path (describe/it title or assertion message). Eligibility: required default for any contract invariant stated as 'MUST reject', 'MUST enforce', or 'is rejected'.",
./specs/invariants.json:7:    "static-anchor": "Invariant enforced structurally by schema export or superRefine; binding_refs name the schema file plus at least one test file that parses that schema. Eligibility: structural invariants whose id may not appear as a test title but whose enforcement cannot regress without the schema change firing a named test failure. Grandfathered scope_ids entries in tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts count as binding_refs for step.md and workflow.md.",
./specs/invariants.json:8:    "prose-only": "Policy statement not directly testable (e.g. when the challenger should be invoked; what the challenger cannot replace). Eligibility: behavioral-track meta-invariants only. Requires `rationale` field. A contract 'MUST reject' invariant cannot be prose-only.",
./specs/invariants.json:12:    "AR-H1 persisted-shape binding for invariants to artifacts is deferred to Slice 26a. Optional `artifact_ids` ledger field may be added in a later slice; absence here is intentional and not a silent omission.",

./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:3:description: Codex cross-model challenger pass on ADR-0007 (Phase 2 close criteria governance). Required per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #6 / decision.md challenger protocol (governance ratchet and gate re-deferral).
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:7:review_target: specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:22:fold_in_disposition: all HIGH + all MED + LOW + META incorporated into ADR-0007 via revision in same authoring session on 2026-04-21; see ADR-0007 §Appendix B internal-consistency reconciliation table for per-objection fold-in location.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:25:# Codex challenger objection list — ADR-0007
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:31:### 1. HIGH — ADR-0007 violates the ADR-0006 precedent firewall while claiming not to
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:33:ADR-0007 says it does not cite ADR-0006 as precedent, but the current text does exactly that in several permission-granting places. The frontmatter calls ADR-0006 a "CC#14 retarget precedent pattern," Context says ADR-0007 installs a firewall "analogous to ADR-0006 §5," and the CC#P2-7 rationale says "ADR-0006 precedent shows that re-deferral by ADR ... is a legitimate pattern." That last sentence is a direct clause-7 violation: it uses ADR-0006's existence and retarget pattern as permission for a similar move. Later disclaimers do not undo the earlier precedent claim.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:35:**Remediation:** Remove all permission-granting ADR-0006 language. ADR-0007 may cite ADR-0006 only as historical context for Phase 1.5 authority or as a negative constraint: "ADR-0006 §5 forbids citing ADR-0006 as precedent; ADR-0007 therefore re-authenticates its own basis." Replace the CC#P2-7 rationale bullet with the independent basis itself, not with an analogy to ADR-0006.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:37:### 2. HIGH — CC#P2-7 re-deferral hollows container isolation into policy-only theater
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:39:CLAUDE.md says Phase 2 implementers run in a container with distinct UID and protected mounts, while hard invariants #1-#4 remain non-negotiable except through methodology reopening. ADR-0007 says Phase 2 close does not require container isolation, no audit check enforces CC#P2-7, and the invariants remain "policy (not Phase-2-close-gating)." That is not a preserved invariant in practice; it is a standing permission to run all P2.1-P2.11 work unisolated unless a slice happens to self-identify as "claiming isolation." The Break-Glass sentence does not rescue this, because the ADR defines normal Phase 2 work as not violating the invariants.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:41:**Remediation:** Carry weaker-evidence wording openly: "ADR-0007 weakens Phase 2 close by accepting unisolated implementer execution for Phase 2 close." Add an interim enforcement surface, such as a required per-slice isolation status line and an audit check rejecting any Phase 2 slice that both writes protected surfaces and omits either container evidence or an explicit CC#P2-7 re-deferral citation. If the intent is to amend CLAUDE.md's Phase 2 container sentence, say so and route it as a methodology amendment instead of burying it in a close-criteria ADR.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:43:### 3. HIGH — The CC#P2-7 trigger list misses obvious isolation-adjacent Phase 2 scenarios
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:45:The five triggers do not cover all cases where isolation honesty becomes load-bearing. Missing triggers include: a non-dry-run agent adapter with filesystem or tool access executing against this repo; any generated workflow or plugin command that can write under `specs/`, `tests/`, `.github/`, or config; new visible property tests or behavioral specs whose read-only status matters; CI/proxy/hook config changes that should be read-only to implementers; and any reference-Circuit parity harness that mounts both source and target trees. Trigger 1 also excludes "Claude/Codex under operator direction," which is exactly the current implementer class most likely to mutate protected files.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:49:### 4. HIGH — CC#P2-8 can pass by review-artifact presence without proving CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-7 are closed
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:51:CC#P2-8's enforcement is a set of files plus a Check-21-analog artifact-presence audit. It does not require the close review to enumerate CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-7, cite their exact evidence, mark each as satisfied/not satisfied, or fail closed if any criterion is red, re-deferred, waived, or satisfied by weaker evidence. Worse, it explicitly allows LLM stand-in sections until a genuine non-LLM signal is obtainable, which can recreate the ADR-0006 weaker-evidence pattern at Phase 2 close while still satisfying artifact presence.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:53:**Remediation:** Define CC#P2-8 as the final blocking review over a close matrix: every CC#P2-N must have an evidence path, audit/test result, commit SHA, and status. The audit check should reject a Phase 2 close claim if any prior criterion is missing, red, re-deferred without an active ADR, or supported only by stand-in evidence where the criterion requires operator/non-LLM evidence. Artifact presence alone is not enough.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:57:ADR-0007 repeatedly treats "landing of slice X" as enforcement. That is weaker than a testable binding. CC#P2-5 says the audit check will be "named explicitly in the slice commit body," but names no check. CC#P2-8 depends on P2-CLOSE-REVIEW, which is not yet in the plan. CC#P2-2 allows a real-agent smoke test to be skipped in CI without requiring a durable local transcript or receipt artifact. CC#P2-3 says P2.2 is partial and P2.11 is full, but the audit binding only checks manifest/markdown closure, not Claude Code invokability. These can all be satisfied by weaker evidence without tripping the firewall.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:59:**Remediation:** Replace slice-name enforcement with concrete acceptance artifacts. For each CC#P2-N, name the required test file or audit function, the required fixture/golden path, the required frontmatter fields if a review artifact is involved, and the non-substitutable failure condition. Where the exact test name is not known yet, mark the criterion "not fully locked" or require the later slice to amend ADR-0007 before claiming closure.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:61:### 6. HIGH — CC#P2-6 weakens the plan's `explore` spine and silently resolves Open Question #5
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:63:The plan's P2.3 deliverable says the `explore` fixture should have "full-spine phases — at minimum Frame → Analyze → Synthesize → Review → Close," while Open Question #5 asks whether `explore` starts full-spine or partial-spine. ADR-0007 instead defines `explore` canonical coverage as only {Frame, Analyze, Synthesize}. That makes the close criterion weaker than the plan slice it supposedly locks, and it resolves an open plan question without acknowledging the resolution or amending the plan section.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:65:**Remediation:** Either set CC#P2-6's `explore` canonical phase set to Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review, Close, or explicitly amend Open Question #5 and P2.3 to say Phase 2 parity starts with the three-phase subset. If the latter is chosen, carry weaker-evidence wording because the ADR is lowering the plan's stated minimum.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:67:### 7. HIGH — The "plan amended in place" story is actually pointer-by-another-name
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:69:ADR-0007 says the plan will be amended in place, but the proposed replacement text says the plan is only a "faithful mirror" and does not duplicate enforcement binding text. That leaves future slice authors reading a tactical plan whose close criteria are still terse summaries, while the actual bindings live elsewhere. This is the exact pointer-only failure ADR-0007 claims to avoid under D4 authority-graph discipline.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:71:**Remediation:** Put the locked CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-8 identifiers, target workflow, re-deferral status, and short binding summary directly in `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md`. ADR-0007 can remain authoritative on conflicts, but the plan must carry enough in-place content that a slice author cannot miss the locked semantics.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:75:ADR-0007 says a retarget requires amending the ADR and clearing the firewall, but it does not say what has to change. A fallback to `review` affects CC#P2-1 goldens, CC#P2-6 canonical phase coverage, P2.3 contract authorship, P2.5 fixture paths, artifact-shape expectations, and possibly whether already-landed `explore` work remains a target, a discarded partial, or a second-workflow candidate. Without that payload, "amend the ADR" can degrade into a target-name swap.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:77:**Remediation:** Add a retarget checklist: new target workflow, canonical phases, fixture path, golden path, artifact-shape contract, affected planned slices, disposition of any landed `explore` artifacts, and evidence that the retarget is structurally required rather than merely scope-reducing.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:81:The no-aggregate rule is strong in intent, but the text still uses "partial-green," "takes it to green," "all eight close criteria green," and the especially dangerous "CC#P2-7 is trivially green-by-redeferral." A re-deferred criterion should not be green; it should be `re-deferred`, `not applicable to Phase 2 close`, or `active`. Treating it as green invites exactly the scalar dashboards the ADR is trying to ban.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:83:**Remediation:** Reserve green/red status for active criteria with executable evidence. Use `re-deferred` for CC#P2-7 and ban "green-by-redeferral," "all but," "mostly done," "only N remaining," "complete except," and "near close" alongside the existing forbidden wording. Make the close condition "all active close criteria satisfied and all re-deferred criteria have valid ADR trigger coverage," not "all eight green."
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:87:ADR-0007 repeatedly refers to P2.2's future plugin check as "planned Check 22 after the Slice 31a Check 21 numbering." In `scripts/audit.mjs`, Check 22 is already the verify gate after Slice 31a inserted Check 21. The next new audit check is not Check 22 unless the audit report is renumbered again. This is a small citation error with large drift potential because future commit bodies and tests may bind to the wrong check number.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:89:**Remediation:** Avoid fixed future check numbers or state "next available audit check number, currently after the verify gate." If a number is kept, update it to the actual next slot and require the implementing slice to preserve monotonic printed check order.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:93:The plan names P2.8 router/classifier, P2.10 artifact schema set, status/tier freshness ratchets, golden hashing, and continuity lifecycle as Phase 2 work, but ADR-0007 does not make them close gates or explicitly demote them from close. CC#P2-1 can pass by byte-shape golden without artifact schemas. CC#P2-4 can pass by hook presence plus seeded resume without proving the continuity-record lifecycle. Product ratchets like `status_docs_current` and `tier_claims_current` "continue" but are not required at close.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:95:**Remediation:** Add close criteria or explicit non-gating dispositions for router/classifier, artifact schemas, golden hashing, continuity lifecycle, and inherited product ratchets. At minimum, require all inherited product ratchets named in the plan to be green at Phase 2 close unless a separate ADR re-defers them.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:99:CC#P2-2 excludes a mock adapter, but "externally-produced artifact byte-shape" is not enough to prove a genuine request/receipt/result round-trip. A checked-in artifact, manually produced output, or deterministic script could satisfy byte-shape unless the event log records adapter provenance and the test asserts it. The CI skip also creates a path where the only always-on ratchet is contract shape, not real dispatch.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:101:**Remediation:** Require a durable dispatch transcript or event-log proof: adapter id, non-dry-run flag, request payload hash, receipt id, result artifact hash, and a test asserting the reducer/result-writer consumed that sequence. If CI skips live invocation, require a local smoke artifact path and audit-visible timestamp/commit reference before CC#P2-2 can close.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:109:### 14. META — ADR-0007 is carrying too many governance moves in one artifact
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:111:This ADR locks eight criteria, re-defers a hard-invariant-adjacent gate, defines future retarget rules, designs Phase 2 close review, amends the plan, sets ceremony wording, and declares no new audit check. The breadth is why contradictions slipped in: "verbatim" criteria are not actually verbatim after binding changes, "in-place" amendment becomes a pointer, and "not ADR-0006 precedent" coexists with an ADR-0006 precedent sentence. D10 treats repeated review churn as an artifact-size signal; this ADR is already showing that signal before fold-in.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:113:**Remediation:** Either split the isolation re-deferral into a narrow ADR-0007a and keep ADR-0007 to close-criteria locking, or add an explicit internal consistency table that reconciles each source-plan statement, ADR lock, plan amendment, audit binding, and reopen condition. Do not land the current all-in-one version without folding the contradictions above.
./specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:117:ADR-0007 is directionally useful, but it cannot land as-is. The minimum acceptable fold-in is: remove ADR-0006 precedent leakage, make CC#P2-7's weakening honest and trigger-complete, turn slice-name bindings into concrete evidence bindings, close the CC#P2-8 circularity, fix the `explore` spine mismatch, and make the plan amendment genuinely in-place.
