./specs/artifacts.json:10:      "contract": "specs/contracts/workflow.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:37:      "contract": "specs/contracts/step.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:45:        "ArtifactRef",
./specs/artifacts.json:68:      "contract": "specs/contracts/phase.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:95:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:126:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:153:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:179:      "description": "Byte-for-byte copy of the workflow manifest taken at run bootstrap, persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/manifest.snapshot.json. Carries the exact persisted manifest bytes (base64-encoded for JSON transport) plus a SHA-256 hash over those raw bytes. The byte-match re-entry check rejects any manifest drift between bootstrap and resume. Slice 27c authors this artifact and its writer.",
./specs/artifacts.json:180:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:194:      "trust_boundary": "operator-local persisted state; written once at bootstrap by the manifest snapshot writer at src/runtime/manifest-snapshot-writer.ts. Hash algorithm: SHA-256 over the exact persisted manifest snapshot bytes (`algorithm: 'sha256-raw'`), per ADR-0001 Addendum B §Phase 1.5 Close Criteria #8. Parse-time superRefine rejects any snapshot whose declared hash disagrees with sha256 over decoded bytes_base64; a second reader cannot be tricked into accepting a tampered byte-body under the declared hash.",
./specs/artifacts.json:206:      "description": "User-visible summary of what a run produced, persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/artifacts/result.json once the closing run.closed event is appended. Unlike state.json (reducer-derived, recomputable from run.log) a RunResult is written once at close and never mutated; it is the authoritative 'what happened' artifact independent of future log rewrites. Slice 27c declares the shape; Slice 27d wires the writer into dogfood-run-0.",
./specs/artifacts.json:207:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:216:      "backing_paths": ["<circuit-next-run-root>/artifacts/result.json"],
./specs/artifacts.json:233:      "contract": "specs/contracts/selection.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:256:      "trust_boundary": "operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure and ghost-provenance rejection at parse time. Composition produces selection.resolution (separate artifact).",
./specs/artifacts.json:269:      "contract": "specs/contracts/selection.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:308:      "contract": "specs/contracts/adapter.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:340:      "contract": "specs/contracts/adapter.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:383:      "contract": "specs/contracts/adapter.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:412:      "description": "Top-level Config record contributed by one config layer. Carries schema_version (literal 1), a DispatchConfig (sub-artifact adapter.registry), a record of per-circuit CircuitOverride (sub-artifact config.circuit-override), and a defaults object carrying a SelectionOverride. Surplus keys rejected transitively via .strict().",
./specs/artifacts.json:413:      "contract": "specs/contracts/config.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:435:      "trust_boundary": "operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure + schema_version literal at parse time. Composition of multiple layers is out of v0.1 scope (see specs/contracts/config.md Scope).",
./specs/artifacts.json:448:      "contract": "specs/contracts/config.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:476:      "description": "Per-workflow override slot stored in Config.circuits[workflow_id]. Currently carries an optional SelectionOverride; reserved for additional per-circuit override categories in v0.2. Strict-key rejection forbids smuggling new categories without a contract change.",
./specs/artifacts.json:477:      "contract": "specs/contracts/config.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:508:      "contract": "specs/contracts/skill.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:540:      "contract": "specs/contracts/continuity.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:563:      "reference_evidence": ["specs/reference/legacy-circuit/continuity-characterization.md"],
./specs/artifacts.json:564:      "migration_policy": "deferred; no transparent runtime parse of old Circuit records. If imported later, a separate migration-source contract is required.",
./specs/artifacts.json:573:      "description": "circuit-next continuity resolver/index artifact. Points at the pending record and the current attached run; determines which record is authoritative for resume. Singleton on disk — no in-body identity.",
./specs/artifacts.json:574:      "contract": "specs/contracts/continuity.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:586:      "reference_evidence": ["specs/reference/legacy-circuit/continuity-characterization.md"],
./specs/artifacts.json:587:      "migration_policy": "deferred; no transparent runtime parse of old Circuit index. If imported later, a separate migration-source contract is required.",
./specs/artifacts.json:596:      "description": "Framing artifact produced by the Frame phase of an explore workflow run. Declares the subject, operator task statement, and success condition for the investigation.",
./specs/artifacts.json:597:      "contract": "specs/contracts/explore.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:603:      "backing_paths": ["<run-root>/artifacts/brief.json"],
./specs/artifacts.json:619:      "description": "Analyze-phase artifact produced by an explore workflow run. Decomposes the subject (from explore.brief) into named aspects with evidence citations.",
./specs/artifacts.json:620:      "contract": "specs/contracts/explore.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:626:      "backing_paths": ["<run-root>/artifacts/analysis.json"],
./specs/artifacts.json:644:      "description": "Synthesize-phase artifact — the explore workflow's primary output. A recommendation, decision candidate set, or investigation conclusion with explicit mapping back to the brief's success condition.",
./specs/artifacts.json:645:      "contract": "specs/contracts/explore.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:651:      "backing_paths": ["<run-root>/artifacts/synthesis.json"],
./specs/artifacts.json:655:      "trust_boundary": "model-authored during Synthesize phase (orchestrator invokes the synthesis model/adapter); read-only thereafter within the run; the primary output artifact for explore workflows",
./specs/artifacts.json:667:      "description": "Review-phase artifact produced by an explore workflow run. Adversarial pass over explore.synthesis with verdict, objections, and missed-angle enumeration.",
./specs/artifacts.json:668:      "contract": "specs/contracts/explore.md",
./specs/artifacts.json:674:      "backing_paths": ["<run-root>/artifacts/review-verdict.json"],
./specs/artifacts.json:692:      "description": "Close-phase aggregate artifact — the explore workflow run's terminal result. ./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:3:description: Codex challenger pass on the drafted Slice 26b working-tree diff (status-epoch alignment + status-docs-current + pinned ratchet floor + ADR-0002 Addendum A).
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:23:  - read specs/ratchet-floor.json (initial floor + metadata)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:24:  - read tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts (red/green fixtures)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:28:  - read specs/adrs/ADR-0002-bootstrap-discipline.md (Addendum A)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:35:  - specs/ratchet-floor.json
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:36:  - tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:40:  - specs/adrs/ADR-0002-bootstrap-discipline.md
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:44:  - specs/contracts/ (no contract surface touched by this slice)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:45:  - specs/invariants.json (ledger tracks contract invariants; this slice is audit-machinery and adds no invariants)
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:53:Slice 26b closes three TIER.md `planned → 26b` rows: `status_docs_current`, `pinned_ratchet_floor`, `current_slice_status_epoch`. The slice adds structured `<!-- current_slice: <id> -->` HTML-comment markers to README.md / PROJECT_STATE.md / TIER.md, three new audit dimensions (Check 17/18/19 in `scripts/audit.mjs`), a pinned floor at `specs/ratchet-floor.json`, ADR-0002 Addendum A documenting the pinned-floor pattern, and a new test file `tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts`. The close-gate hole being targeted is that `checkPhaseDrift` is agreement-only (if README and PROJECT_STATE both tell a stale story the audit is green) and the contract-test ratchet (Check 6) uses `HEAD~1` moving-window comparison (a regression commit followed by a docs-only commit slides the window forward and the regression goes green).
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:68:Anchor: `specs/ratchet-floor.json:4`, `scripts/audit.mjs:~1750` (checkPinnedRatchetFloor).
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:69:Rationale: staged tree's static test count is 556 (pre-fold-in) but the draft pinned floor at 530. Deleting `tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts` drops static count back to 530, so Check 19 is still green; Check 6 is red for one commit, then a docs-only commit slides `HEAD~1` and the original false-green returns.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:70:**Disposition: ACCEPT.** Floor raised to 574 (final post-fold-in static count) in `specs/ratchet-floor.json` and the `notes` field rewritten to state "pins post-Slice-26b (post-Codex-fold-in) HEAD static count."
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:73:Anchor: `scripts/audit.mjs:~1736` (previous `floor >= 0` guard), `specs/adrs/ADR-0002-bootstrap-discipline.md:~188`.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:74:Rationale: draft `checkPinnedRatchetFloor` accepted any non-negative integer; `contract_test_count: 0` was valid and returned green when `headCount >= 0`. That contradicts the ADR's "explicit commit action" rule and leaves the ratchet silently relaxable in the same file that claims to enforce it.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:75:**Disposition: ACCEPT.** New `validatePinnedRatchetFloorData` helper requires `contract_test_count > 0` (positive integer). `checkPinnedRatchetFloor` now routes through the helper and returns red on zero-or-negative floors. Red-fixture test added: `it('returns red when the floor field is not a positive integer ...')`. Cross-commit decrease detection (comparing against previous committed floor) deferred as a future enhancement — the positive-integer floor closes the trivially-bypassable relaxation path; the cross-commit case requires git-ancestry reads which the audit does not currently perform.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:80:Anchor: `tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts` — the only `checkStatusDocsCurrent` test accepted `green | yellow | red`, so an implementation that always returned green would still pass. Codex verified the helper DOES return red on a temp-git stale-unison fixture; the suite does not protect that behavior.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:88:**MED-3 — Marker extraction is not anchored and ignores duplicates.**
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:89:Anchor: `scripts/audit.mjs:~1614` (draft `extractCurrentSliceMarker` returned the first regex hit anywhere in the file).
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:91:**Disposition: ACCEPT.** Anchored extraction to the "status-header zone" (everything before the first `#` markdown heading) via new `sliceHeaderZone(text)` helper. Duplicate markers in the zone are rejected (returns null). Red-fixture tests added: marker-after-first-heading (rejected), duplicate-markers-in-header-zone (rejected), markers-below-heading-ignored (embedded in code blocks below the header zone are not read). All three live docs satisfy the zone rule by construction: README and PROJECT_STATE have the marker at line 1; TIER has it between frontmatter and the first `# TIER Claim Matrix` heading.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:94:Anchor: `scripts/audit.mjs:~1716` (previous `checkPinnedRatchetFloor` only validated `floors.contract_test_count`), `specs/adrs/ADR-0002-bootstrap-discipline.md:~188`.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:95:Rationale: `schema_version`, `last_advanced_at`, and `last_advanced_in_slice` could be missing or malformed and Check 19 still went green as long as the numeric floor was acceptable. The ADR's "explicit commit action" rule therefore had no machine surface.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:96:**Disposition: ACCEPT.** New exported helper `validatePinnedRatchetFloorData(floorData)` returns a list of error strings and enforces: `schema_version === 1` (literal), `floors.contract_test_count` positive integer, `last_advanced_at` matches `/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/`, `last_advanced_in_slice` matches `SLICE_ID_PATTERN`. `checkPinnedRatchetFloor` routes through it and returns red with every violation enumerated. Red fixtures added for each metadata field individually plus a combined-violations fixture.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:101:**Disposition: ACCEPT.** Introduced a single shared `SLICE_ID_PATTERN = /^[0-9]+[a-z]?$/` constant + `isValidSliceId(value)` helper. Both `extractCurrentSliceMarker` and `extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject` now post-validate the captured id against the pattern and return null on mismatch. `SLICE_COMMIT_SUBJECT_PATTERN` kept as the initial wide regex for compatibility with existing commit history; validation tightens at the extractor layer. Rejection tests added for `26ba`, `26b-wip`, `27c-b`, `phase-1`, and the empty / trailing-space cases. Widening the pattern requires an ADR amendment.
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:110:Anchor: `scripts/audit.mjs:~1607` (comment said "regex on `^slice-<id>[a-z]?:`") vs `scripts/audit.mjs:~1663` (actual regex `^slice-([0-9a-z-]+?):\s`).
./specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md:111:**Disposition: ACCEPT.** Updated the comment block to describe the actual `SLICE_ID_PATTERN = /^[0-9]+[a-z]?$/` shape and to note that `SLICE_COMMIT_SUBJECT_PATTERN` is deliberately wider (matches any hyphenated id shape) with the extractor-layer post-validation tightening it. Comment now documents why the two layers diverge.

./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:45:I ran `git status --short`, `git log --oneline -5`, `rg --files`, `npm run verify`, `npm run audit`, targeted `rg` searches across `specs/`, `src/`, `tests/`, and `scripts/`, and a `js_repl` inventory of artifact ids, schema files, invariant ids, and test references. `npm run verify` passed with 489 tests across 8 files; `npm run audit` reported 10 green / 0 yellow / 0 red.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:47:Coverage opened: I opened `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `PROJECT_STATE.md`, `specs/evidence.md`, `specs/methodology/decision.md`, ADR-0002/0003, `specs/artifacts.json`, the contract and behavioral frontmatter plus targeted bodies, all schema exports, the relevant audit helper sections, all contract-test files at least by targeted line ranges, and all 12 prior review-record frontmatters plus the prior arc/slice review bodies where the current claims depend on them. I did not read every line of every long contract/review record end-to-end; this is a targeted adversarial pass over the named risk surfaces, not a line-by-line proof.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:51:**1. HIGH - `run.projection` is bound to the wrong persisted artifact shape.**
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:53:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The authority graph believes `run.projection` owns `<circuit-next-run-root>/state.json (derived snapshot)` and is governed by `src/schemas/run.ts` export `RunProjection`. That conflates the on-disk `state.json` shape with an in-memory validation aggregate `{ log, snapshot }`. This is a second Knight-Leveson correlated-miss class after `config.md`: the graph can be forward/reverse green while the artifact row names the wrong runtime shape.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:54:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/artifacts.json:121-140` assigns `run.projection` to `schema_file: src/schemas/run.ts`, `schema_exports: ["RunProjection"]`, and backing path `state.json`; `src/schemas/run.ts:158-163` defines `RunProjection` as an object containing both `log` and `snapshot`; `src/schemas/snapshot.ts:31-47` defines the actual standalone `Snapshot` shape that `state.json` appears to persist; `scripts/audit.mjs:218-221` allowlists `src/schemas/snapshot.ts` as a shared primitive instead of an artifact-bound schema.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:55:- **Proposed remediation** - Split the authority graph: either add a first-class `run.snapshot` artifact with backing path `<circuit-next-run-root>/state.json`, `schema_file: src/schemas/snapshot.ts`, and `schema_exports: ["Snapshot", "StepState", "SnapshotStatus", "StepStatus"]`, while keeping `run.projection` as an in-memory binding with no persisted backing path; or rename/reframe `run.projection` so the persisted artifact is no longer claimed. Add an artifact-authority test that rejects rows whose `backing_paths` name a persisted JSON file while `schema_exports` names only a wrapper/aggregate shape.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:60:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The evidence and run contract treat the Workflow manifest snapshot as immutable per run. Current schemas prove only that `manifest_hash` is copied from `run.bootstrapped` into `Snapshot`; they do not persist manifest bytes, bind a manifest path, name a hashing algorithm, or require re-entry byte-match against a stored manifest.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:61:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/evidence.md:46-48` says event log/state and manifest immutability are enforced; `specs/contracts/run.md:17-26` says a Run includes a Workflow manifest snapshot identified by `manifest_hash`; `src/schemas/event.ts:16-24` stores only `manifest_hash` on `RunBootstrappedEvent`; `src/schemas/snapshot.ts:31-44` stores only `manifest_hash` on `Snapshot`; `src/schemas/run.ts:195-200` checks only equality between those two strings.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:62:- **Proposed remediation** - Add a manifest snapshot artifact/contract surface before runtime implementation: e.g. `run.manifest_snapshot` with `backing_paths` for the copied manifest, a `hash_algorithm`, and a bootstrap/re-entry rule that recomputes and compares bytes. At minimum, downgrade current prose from "byte-match enforced" to "hash parity carried; byte-match is Phase 2 entry" and add a Phase 2 property id such as `run.prop.manifest_snapshot_bytes_match_hash`.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:63:- **Fold-in discipline label** - Disposition: Scoped to Phase 2 entry (Slices 29-31), specifically the Slice 30 event-writer/runtime-boundary contract or the Phase 2 entry ceremony. Reopen trigger: any implementation that writes or resumes a run before a persisted manifest snapshot or explicit no-snapshot ADR lands.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:67:- **Affected claim or artifact** - `npm run audit` reports README/PROJECT_STATE agreement, but the public README still says the project is paused at Slice 7 authority-graph hardening while `PROJECT_STATE.md` says Slice 24 fold-ins landed. The audit checks only numeric phase (`0.5`) and misses stale slice/status text.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:68:- **Reproduction path** - `README.md:3-4` says "Phase 1 contract authorship temporarily paused for Slice 7"; `README.md:28-34` repeats "Phase 0.5 / Slice 7"; `PROJECT_STATE.md:3-23` says Slice 24 fold-ins are landed and the authority graph is 13/13 plane-classified; `scripts/audit.mjs:1012-1052` extracts only numeric phase and returns green when both files say Phase 0.5; `tests/contracts/session-hygiene.test.ts:65-107` mirrors that same phase-only proxy.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:72:**4. HIGH - ADR-0002 citation enforcement is a token-presence proxy, not the citation rule.**
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:74:- **Affected claim or artifact** - ADR-0002 requires every design decision to cite evidence/contracts/methodology with an invariant or section reference, and rejects "Circuit does X" as design justification. The audit accepts any disciplined commit body containing broad tokens such as `CLAUDE.md`, `bootstrap/`, `specs/domain.md`, or `ADR-0002`, regardless of whether the cited material supports the actual design decision.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:75:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/adrs/ADR-0002-bootstrap-discipline.md:56-67` states the citation rule and requires evidence/contract/methodology grounding; `scripts/audit.mjs:41-51` defines broad `CITATION_PATTERNS`; `scripts/audit.mjs:379-380` implements `checkCitation` as `some(pattern)`; `scripts/audit.mjs:1158-1168` reports "All slice commits cite specs/, CLAUDE.md, bootstrap/, or an ADR."
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:76:- **Proposed remediation** - Split the audit dimension into "citation token present" and "anchored design citation." Require at least one anchored reference for Ratchet-Advance commits: contract invariant id (`RUN-I6`), evidence invariant/section (`specs/evidence.md#...` or `Invariant N`), ADR section, or behavioral invariant id. Add constructed-violation tests showing that a commit body with only "Cites: CLAUDE.md" fails the stronger rule.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:77:- **Fold-in discipline label** - Disposition: Incorporable within Phase 1 close arc. Fold into Slice 28's audit/methodology self-compliance work because this is exactly the green-audit/weak-proxy failure the arc is trying to flush out.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:83:- **Affected claim or artifact** - `CLAUDE.md` says every slice must restate arc goal, phase goal, and earlier-slice obsolescence before the framing triplet. `npm run audit` still validates only lane plus failure mode / acceptance evidence / alternate framing.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:90:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The contract/test stack claims named invariants are the trace units, but several invariant ids do not appear anywhere in the current test corpus. Some semantics are likely tested under prose descriptions, but the trace is not machine-visible.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:91:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/contracts/adapter.md:258-271` defines ADAPTER-I8; `specs/contracts/adapter.md:300-310` defines ADAPTER-I11; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:47-53` defines CHALLENGER-I2; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:87-95` defines CHALLENGER-I6; `specs/behavioral/prose-yaml-parity.md:63-74` defines PROSE-YAML-I3. A targeted `rg` over `tests/` found no occurrences of these ids; the closest adapter semantic tests are unlabeled in `tests/contracts/schema-parity.test.ts:3203-3267`.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:92:- **Proposed remediation** - As part of the Slice 25 frontmatter ledger, add `invariant_ids` to contract/track frontmatter and require every non-prose-only invariant id to appear in at least one test name or assertion message. For prose-only invariants, require an explicit `enforcement_state: prose-only-now | audit-only-now | test-enforced | phase2-property`.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:93:- **Fold-in discipline label** - Disposition: Incorporable within Phase 1 close arc. This is aligned with the already-scheduled Slice 25 contract-frontmatter ledger.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:95:**3. MED - `pending_rehome` and `pending-artifact` metadata can rot past the named slice.**
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:97:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The repo made the `config.*` miss and the `event.ts` pending-artifact visible, but the visibility metadata is not an expiry. A positive integer `target_slice` / `tracking_slice` will still pass after Slice 26 or Slice 30 unless a human notices.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:98:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/artifacts.json:238-250` marks Config exports as `pending_rehome` to Slice 26; `scripts/audit.mjs:223-244` marks `src/schemas/event.ts` as `pending-artifact` tracking Slice 30; `tests/contracts/artifact-authority.test.ts:739-753` requires `tracking_slice` and `tracking_objection` but not expiry; `tests/contracts/artifact-authority.test.ts:910-955` requires `pending_rehome` shape and pins Slice 26 but does not fail when the current arc passes that slice.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:99:- **Proposed remediation** - Add an expiry rule keyed to `PROJECT_STATE.md` or an explicit arc schedule file: once current slice number is greater than or equal to `target_slice`/`tracking_slice`, the audit must fail unless the pending entry is removed, converted to an artifact row, or renewed by an ADR/review disposition. If current-slice parsing is too brittle, add `expires_before_phase: 2-entry` and fail at phase-transition ceremony.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:100:- **Fold-in discipline label** - Disposition: Incorporable within Phase 1 close arc for `pending_rehome` (Slice 26), and Scoped to Phase 2 entry (Slices 29-31) for `event.ts` if it survives until the Slice 30 event-writer boundary contract.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:104:- **Affected claim or artifact** - Behavioral track frontmatter and status read as ratified-v0.1, but `prose-yaml-parity` is explicitly a static anchor/sentinel until the catalog compiler lands, and `cross-model-challenger` still names an unlanded audit dimension. That is acceptable only if the enforcement state is explicit; currently it is split between prose and tests.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:105:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/behavioral/prose-yaml-parity.md:1-14` lists structural compiler/audit enforcement even though the compiler is not landed; `tests/contracts/prose-yaml-parity.test.ts:52-70` says PROSE-YAML-I1..I4 are prose-documentation-only at v0.1 and the test pins only frontmatter/cross-references/sentinel markers; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:10-16` lists a planned audit dimension as NOT LANDED; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:187-192` describes that future audit dimension.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:106:- **Proposed remediation** - Add an enforcement matrix to each behavioral track: per invariant, state `test-enforced`, `audit-enforced`, `static-anchor`, `prose-only`, or `phase2-property`, with the exact file enforcing it. Then add a test that rejects `status: ratified-v0.1` unless every invariant has a declared enforcement state.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:111:- **Affected claim or artifact** - CHALLENGER-I6 admits that a challenger cannot replace authority mapping or evidence, and the track's own failure mode says "reviewing the wrong thing" is mitigated by prompt discipline and operator checking citations. The review record schema does not require commands run, opened scope, or skipped scope, so that mitigation remains manual.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:112:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:87-95` says challenger output cannot replace structural checks; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:124-129` says the operator verifies Codex read the target files by checking evidence citations; review frontmatter currently requires base keys only via `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:431-470`, with no opened-scope or commands-run field.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:113:- **Proposed remediation** - Extend arc/contract review records with `commands_run:` and `opened_scope:` frontmatter or required body sections. Add tests that every new review record has non-empty scope, and that arc reviews include an explicit "skipped/not fully read" sentence. This review's preamble follows that pattern manually; make it structural.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:114:- **Fold-in discipline label** - Disposition: Incorporable within Phase 1 close arc if added to Slice 25 review-record/frontmatter normalization; otherwise Scoped to v0.2 with reopen trigger: first review record whose finding is later invalidated because the reviewer did not actually open the cited surface.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:116:**6. MED - Contract prose still leaks future/stale references that no audit catches.**
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:118:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The prior close review flagged "(future)" drift and Slice 23/24 fixed some instances, but the corpus still has stale "future" language on landed or partially landed surfaces. This is a weaker variant of the README false-green problem.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:119:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/contracts/workflow.md:97-100` says the prose-yaml parity contract test is "future" even though `tests/contracts/prose-yaml-parity.test.ts` exists, albeit as a static anchor; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:203-209` says "the five committed challenger records" while listing six contract reviews; `specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md:218-227` says v0.2 will land `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts` even though the frontmatter says it landed and was tightened through Slice 24.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:127:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The artifact-authority tests still describe `schema_exports` as `export const` existence even though Slice 23 broadened the checker to `export const|type|function|class`. This does not weaken the gate, but it makes the test title/error message lie about what is accepted.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:128:- **Reproduction path** - `tests/contracts/artifact-authority.test.ts:140-153` says every `schema_exports` name is actually "`export const <name>`d"; `scripts/audit.mjs:282-295` says the checker accepts `export const|type|function|class`; `tests/contracts/artifact-authority.test.ts:190-207` confirms function/type/class acceptance.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:134:- **Affected claim or artifact** - The review-record schema now requires `reviewer_model`, `review_kind`, `review_date`, `verdict`, and `authored_by`, but values still drift (`GPT-5 Codex`, `gpt-5-codex via codex exec`, `gpt-5.1 via codex exec`, `operator + claude-opus-4-7`, `Codex`). That may be fine, but the schema should either bless free-form values or normalize them.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:135:- **Reproduction path** - `specs/reviews/arc-phase-1-close-codex.md:8-14`, `specs/reviews/adapter-md-v0.1-codex.md:4-8`, and `specs/reviews/run-md-v0.1-codex.md:4-8` show the value-shape drift; `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts:431-470` checks key presence, not value vocabulary, except `target_kind`.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:141:- **Affected claim or artifact** - Audit reports "440 tests at HEAD" while `npm run verify` reports 489 tests. The docblock explains this is a static authored-test floor, not runtime total, but the audit output label still says "tests" without qualification.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:142:- **Reproduction path** - `scripts/audit.mjs:397-407` explains the static floor and dynamic-test gap; `scripts/audit.mjs:1226-1254` prints the contract-test ratchet detail as `${headCount} tests at HEAD`; `npm run verify` on this pass reported 489 runtime tests while audit reported 440.
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:148:- The new correlated miss is not "another file missing from the graph" in the same style as `config.md`; it is subtler. `run.projection` is present, classified, contract-bound, reciprocated, and green under audit, but the row binds a persisted path to a wrapper schema rather than the persisted shape. The current authority graph proves path/existence/export consistency, not "this export is the shape actually written at this backing path."
./specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md:149:- Several objections above are weaker-proxy failures: audit/test names say "phase consistency," "citation rule," or "contract test ratchet," while the implementation checks a narrower property. The project has been good at documenting caveats once Codex finds them; the remaining risk is that green summary lines are read faster than caveats.

./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:63:of every artifact a contract governs **before** the contract can be
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:68:Every artifact declared in `specs/artifacts.json` MUST carry exactly one
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:73:| **greenfield** | No existing live or reference runtime surface materially informs this artifact. The artifact is invented by circuit-next. |
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:74:| **successor-to-live** | circuit-next is designing a new surface that replaces or reinterprets a live Circuit surface, but direct runtime compatibility is not required. The live surface must be characterized; the new surface may reject legacy records at runtime. |
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:75:| **legacy-compatible** | circuit-next must directly parse, read, or write existing Circuit artifacts at runtime. Fixture parity and differential tests are required. |
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:76:| **migration-source** | Existing Circuit artifacts are NOT accepted by normal runtime paths, but MAY be consumed by a future explicit migration/import tool. The importer is a separate contract; it does not live inside the runtime schema. |
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:77:| **external-protocol** | Shape is constrained by an external API, file format, or third-party protocol. Characterization is against the external spec, not live Circuit. |
./specs/adrs/ADR-0003-authority-graph-gate.md:78:| **unknown-blocking** | (Discovery marker) The relationship to prior, live, or external reality is unknown. Contracts touching this artifact MAY NOT be drafted until the class is resolved. |

./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:3:description: Codex cross-model challenger pass on specs/contracts/explore.md v0.1 (Phase 2 first-parity workflow contract). Required per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #6 / decision.md challenger protocol (first Phase 2 contract authorship).
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:5:contract_target: explore
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:6:contract_version: 0.1
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:17:fold_in_disposition: All HIGH + MED + LOW folded into explore.md + fixture + invariants.json + artifacts.json in same authoring session on 2026-04-21; META (isolation posture) is a commit-body requirement recorded in the ceremony commit. See explore.md §Invariant + §Deferred properties + §Artifact reader/writer graph + §schema_sections gate placeholder note + §Reopen conditions for per-objection fold-in locations.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:24:`specs/contracts/explore.md` is directionally useful, but it is not yet a clean first Phase 2 workflow contract. The largest issue is not taste: the current ledger state already fails `tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts` because EXPLORE-I2 through EXPLORE-I5 are contract-surface `prose-only` invariants. The rest of the objections are the contract-shape and fixture-closure reasons I would not fold this in as-is.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:28:### 1. HIGH — EXPLORE-I2 through EXPLORE-I5 violate the invariant-ledger eligibility rule
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:30:`specs/invariants.json` classifies EXPLORE-I2, EXPLORE-I3, EXPLORE-I4, and EXPLORE-I5 as `surface_type: "contract"` plus `enforcement_state: "prose-only"`. The existing ledger ratchet explicitly forbids that combination for contract invariants, and the targeted run confirms the slice is red: `npm test -- tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts` fails on `contract-surface invariants cannot be prose-only`. This is also semantically right: `explore.md` says "The runtime MUST reject" violations, so those rows cannot be treated as prose-only promises.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:32:**Remediation:** Change deferred EXPLORE invariants to an allowed deferred shape, most likely `enforcement_state: "phase2-property"` with concrete `target_slice` and `reopen_condition`, or weaken the contract prose so only EXPLORE-I1 is a current "MUST reject" invariant and the others are explicitly future obligations. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:34:### 2. HIGH — The fixture writes an unregistered `explore.result@v1` artifact
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:36:`explore.md` declares four artifact ids and says Close emits a final aggregate with "no new artifact id in v0.1." The fixture nevertheless has `close-step.writes.artifact.schema = "explore.result@v1"` at `artifacts/result.json`. That creates a fifth artifact-like surface with no `specs/artifacts.json` entry, no contract `artifact_ids` declaration, no reader/writer registry, and no deferred schema row. This is exactly the artifact-registry drift the contract layer is meant to prevent.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:38:**Remediation:** Either remove `close-step.writes.artifact` and model Close as a terminal result outside the artifact registry, or register `explore.result` everywhere: `explore.md` frontmatter, ubiquitous-language section, `specs/artifacts.json`, `specs/invariants.json` property text, and future P2.10 schema commitments. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:40:### 3. HIGH — EXPLORE-I1 claims more than `checkSpineCoverage` enforces
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:42:EXPLORE-I1 requires exact canonicals, `spine_policy.mode = partial`, `omits = [plan, verify]`, and "a rationale >=20 characters mentioning investigation or synthesis-in-act semantics." `checkSpineCoverage` only checks the canonical set, mode, and omit set. It does not inspect rationale length or content, does not validate the `id` plus filename heuristic described in the contract, and does not parse each scanned fixture through `Workflow.safeParse`. The temp tests even use `steps: []` fixtures whose phase and entry references would not satisfy the base workflow schema. That means Check 24 can return green for a malformed workflow so long as the label set is correct.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:44:**Remediation:** Either narrow EXPLORE-I1 to the three facts Check 24 actually enforces, or extend `checkSpineCoverage` to parse the fixture with `Workflow.safeParse`, reject malformed `spine_policy.rationale`, and add negative tests for missing/garbage rationale plus an `explore/` path whose `id` disagrees with the directory. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:46:### 4. HIGH — `Synthesize` mapped to canonical `act` is an undeclared amendment to CC#P2-6
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:48:ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 binds the first workflow's canonical set in human phase terms as Frame -> Analyze -> Synthesize -> Review -> Close. The contract silently translates Synthesize to canonical `act`, while omitting canonical `plan`. That may be the right final answer, but the contract does not prove it. In an investigation workflow, synthesis often means building candidate conclusions, tradeoffs, or a plan of attack; mapping it to `act` instead of `plan` changes what `plan` omission means under PHASE-I4 partial-spine semantics.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:50:**Remediation:** Add an explicit "title-to-canonical translation" subsection that cites ADR-0007 as accepting `Synthesize -> act`, or amend ADR-0007 / the Phase 2 plan to state the canonical id set `{frame, analyze, act, review, close}` rather than only title labels. Also add a short rejected-alternative note explaining why `Synthesize -> plan` was not chosen. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:52:### 5. MED — Artifact readers disagree across the contract, registry, and fixture
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:54:The contract post-condition says Close reads prior artifacts. The fixture Close step reads only `artifacts/synthesis.json` and `artifacts/review-verdict.json`. `specs/artifacts.json` lists `explore.brief` as read by close-step even though the fixture does not read it, and does not list `explore.analysis` as read by close-step even though "prior artifacts" would include it. This leaves no single source of truth for whether Close is allowed to re-check the original success condition and evidence chain.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:56:**Remediation:** Decide whether Close reads all prior artifacts or only the synthesis and review verdict. Then make `explore.md`, `.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json`, and every `specs/artifacts.json` reader list match exactly. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:60:`explore.prop.canonical_phase_set_is_correct` is marked `test-enforced` with a `binding_refs` kind of `test_title`, but no `describe(...)` or `it(...)` title in `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts` contains that property id. The tests do mention EXPLORE-I1 in a describe title, so the invariant row is anchored, but the property row is not. If property ids are supposed to be ledger-addressable evidence, this is a false binding.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:66:`explore.md` says EXPLORE-I2 through EXPLORE-I5 land at P2.5, while the property rows in `specs/invariants.json` use `target_slice: 29` and a fallback "property harness lands" reopen condition. This slice is described as likely git slice id 34, so `target_slice: 29` looks stale or at least dimensionally different from the P2.5 roadmap. The result is that a future maintainer cannot tell whether the obligation expires at P2.5, Slice 29, Slice 34+n, or whenever the property harness appears.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:68:**Remediation:** Normalize the deferral vocabulary: use the real target slice ids if the ledger is slice-numbered, and mention the P2.5 milestone only as prose context. Each deferred EXPLORE invariant/property should have a concrete reopening condition if that exact target passes without enforcement. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:72:The contract calls this an `explore`-kinded workflow, but the base schema has no `kind` field and the audit hardcodes `WORKFLOW_KIND_CANONICAL_SETS[fixture.id]`. Unknown fixture ids pass through information-only, duplicate ids across skill directories are not rejected here, and a future `explore-mini` or `research` fixture has no typed relationship to the `explore` contract. That is acceptable as a temporary adapter seam only if the contract says it is temporary and names the migration trigger sharply.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:80:**Remediation:** Rephrase the rationale to say `verify` is omitted because no mechanical verification artifact exists in v0.1, while adversarial review is a weaker substitute to be strengthened later. If real adversarial dispatch is required for `explore`, make EXPLORE-I4 depend on the P2.4 adapter or add a future semantic-adequacy property for the Review phase. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:82:### 10. MED — `schema_sections` gates are placeholders without a schema contract
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:84:The fixture gates require strings like `subject`, `success_condition`, `recommendation`, and `maps_to_success_condition`, but those fields are not tied to any schema file until P2.10. As a result, the gate names look schema-like without a schema source, and a future artifact schema could rename a field while the fixture gate remains stale. This is tolerable as scaffolding, but the contract currently presents the gates as meaningful acceptance discipline.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:86:**Remediation:** Add an explicit P2.3 limitation that `schema_sections` gates are provisional field-name guards, not real artifact schemas. Add a P2.10 reopen condition requiring the gate `required` arrays, artifact schemas, and `specs/artifacts.json` `schema_exports` to be reconciled in one slice. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:90:`tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts` covers missing canonical, extra canonical, strict mode, missing omit, and extra omit. It does not test empty rationale, garbage rationale, a rationale that omits investigation/synthesis semantics, directory/id mismatch, duplicate `explore` fixtures, or the contentious `Synthesize -> act` mapping beyond the happy fixture. For EXPLORE-I1 as written, this is not enough mutation coverage.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:92:**Remediation:** Add negative tests for rationale quality and id/path mismatch. Add either a fixture-title assertion proving `Synthesize` is the phase mapped to `act`, or explicitly state that titles are non-semantic and only canonical ids matter. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:96:The reopen list covers retargeting, canonical-set changes, artifact schemas, and a future `kind` field. It does not cover P2.5 passing without EXPLORE-I2/I3/I4/I5 enforcement, artifact-reader graph changes, provisional gate fields diverging from P2.10 schemas, or discovery that `explore.result` must be a registered artifact. Those are the realistic ways this v0.1 contract will rot.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:98:**Remediation:** Add reopen bullets for deferred invariant non-delivery, artifact graph drift, gate/schema reconciliation, and the result-artifact decision. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:102:`specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §P2.3` says the deliverable includes `specs/contracts/explore-md-codex.md`, but the repository's review convention and this contract use `specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md`. The contract file is right; the plan text is stale. For the first Phase 2 contract, stale evidence paths are unnecessary friction.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:104:**Remediation:** Amend the P2.3 plan deliverable line to the canonical `specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md` path, or phrase it generically as `specs/reviews/<contract>-md-v<version>-codex.md`. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:108:ADR-0007 CC#P2-7 requires the commit body for this slice to contain `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)` or an allowed alternative. That cannot be validated from the working tree review files alone. If this review is folded into the same slice, the final commit still needs the exact isolation citation or Check 22 will red the slice regardless of the contract content.
./specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:110:**Remediation:** Include `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)` in the slice commit body unless the operator intentionally invokes the ADR-0007 re-deferred wording or Break-Glass lane. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.

./specs/reviews/adversarial-yield-ledger.md:65:**Pass 2 context.** Pass 2 is an L3 non-LLM cold-read, delegated to Claude + Codex as LLM stand-ins under operator authorization while the operator was asleep. The delegation is recorded as reviewer_role `LLM-standin-for-human-cold-read` in `specs/reviews/phase-1-close-reform-human.md`. Counts are 0 HIGH / 8 MED / 8 LOW; all MED and LOW findings were folded back into the plan in this planning pass. Pass 2 did not violate D10 artifact-size signal (pass count 2 < governance cap 3). A genuine operator cold-read on pickup may append a third evaluator section to the review record; that addition does not count as a new adversarial pass unless it raises defects.

./specs/adrs/ADR-0004-control-plane-data-plane-split.md:62:| **data-plane** | Operator-local, runtime-produced, engine-computed, or model-authored. May carry operator-written text, model-authored narrative, or engine-computed state. Heavier schema-file invariants (transitive `.strict()`, own-property closure, path-safety primitives where identity fields derive filesystem paths) are properties of data-plane **schema files**, enforced by existing contract tests and path-safety audit rules. The class-conditional audit added in this slice focuses on the trust-boundary prose rule; structural-invariant enforcement via the plane classifier (e.g., "every data-plane schema file must compile under a strict-mode checker") is scoped to a future slice. |

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	adr: 0007
     3	title: Phase 2 Close Criteria — Locked Governance, Independent Ratchets, Container Isolation Re-Deferral
     4	status: Accepted
     5	date: 2026-04-21
     6	author: claude-opus-4-7 (drafted + fold-ins incorporated) + gpt-5-codex (challenger, 7 HIGH / 5 MED / 1 LOW / 1 META folded in)
     7	supersedes: none
     8	related:
     9	  - ADR-0001 Addendum B (§Phase 1.5 → Phase 2 transition; authority surface this ADR operates under)
    10	  - ADR-0006 (historical-context-only for CC#14 retarget; ADR-0006 §5 clause 7 forbids citing ADR-0006 as precedent — ADR-0007 therefore re-authenticates its own basis and is NOT analogous or derivative of ADR-0006)
    11	  - specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §Phase 2 close criteria (draft list this ADR locks)
    12	amends:
    13	  - specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §Phase 2 close criteria — status "draft — candidate, not locked" → locked by this ADR
    14	  - CLAUDE.md §Phase discipline — "Phase 2 — Implementation. … Implementer runs in a container with distinct UID" — amended at the close-criterion layer only by CC#P2-7 re-deferral §Decision.1 CC#P2-7; the CLAUDE.md sentence remains policy-level authoritative (see §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 and §Consequences.Accepted for the explicit weakening disclosure)
    15	---
    16	
    17	# ADR-0007 — Phase 2 Close Criteria (Governance)
    18	
    19	## Context
    20	
    21	Phase 1.5 Alpha Proof closed at Slice 31a (ceremony commit `0223d11`,
    22	Phase 1.5 → Phase 2 transition per ADR-0001 Addendum B as amended by
    23	ADR-0006). Phase 2 — Implementation — is now open.
    24	
    25	`specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md` (commit `58b53a5`, amended at
    26	`3bf4868`) enumerated **eight candidate Phase 2 close criteria** under
    27	its §Phase 2 close criteria section, explicitly marked **draft —
    28	candidate, not locked**. The plan further stipulated: *"They are
    29	authored here for ranging purposes and must be locked via an ADR before
    30	any slice claims one of them."* That ADR is this one.
    31	
    32	Locking close criteria in governance before any code slice can cite
    33	them protects the arc. Without a locked anchor, any near-term slice
    34	(P2.2 plugin surface, P2.3 `explore` contract, P2.4 `agent` adapter,
    35	P2.5 end-to-end fixture) would either invent its own partial close
    36	definition or drift — producing the failure mode where Phase 2 "closes"
    37	on vibes rather than on pre-specified, independently-trackable gates.
    38	
    39	The criteria themselves (see §Decision.1 below) are lifted from the
    40	plan's draft list; this ADR's job is to make them authoritative, bind
    41	each to a concrete executable enforcement mechanism (named test file,
    42	named audit function, named artifact paths — not merely slice names),
    43	state the no-aggregate-scoring rule inline, resolve plan Open Question
    44	#3 (container isolation) by re-deferral with named trigger conditions,
    45	resolve plan Open Question #5 (`explore` spine policy) by aligning
    46	CC#P2-6 with the plan's P2.3 deliverable, and install a §Precedent
    47	firewall (§6) that prevents ADR-0007 from being cited as permission
    48	for any future retarget, relaxation, or substitution of a close
    49	criterion.
    50	
    51	**Relationship to ADR-0006 (read carefully).** ADR-0006 §5 clause 7
    52	forbids citing ADR-0006 as precedent for future retargets of phase-
    53	close criteria. ADR-0007 therefore does **not** cite ADR-0006 as
    54	precedent, pattern, template, or analogy in any permission-granting
    55	way. ADR-0006 appears in this document only as (i) historical
    56	Phase-1.5-close authority under §References, and (ii) a negative
    57	constraint under ADR-0006 §5 clause 7 that ADR-0007 explicitly honors.
    58	ADR-0007's §6 Precedent firewall is authored independently from first
    59	principles, not derivatively from ADR-0006 §5.
    60	
    61	**This ADR is a governance ratchet, not a product claim.** Locking the
    62	close criteria does not move Phase 2 closer to done; it defines what
    63	*done* means so that subsequent slices can make real progress against a
    64	fixed target.
    65	
    66	## Decision
    67	
    68	### 1. The eight Phase 2 close criteria — locked with concrete enforcement bindings
    69	
    70	Phase 2 — Implementation is considered closed when and only when **all
    71	active** criteria below are **satisfied** and **all re-deferred
    72	criteria** carry a valid ADR-covered trigger-condition set. Each
    73	criterion is tracked as a separate dimension; no aggregate score is
    74	computed across them (see §Decision.3). Status values per criterion
    75	are exactly one of:
    76	
    77	- `active — red` (enforcement binding fails)
    78	- `active — satisfied` (enforcement binding passes, evidence on-file)
    79	- `re-deferred` (not required for Phase 2 close per this ADR; trigger
    80	  conditions in force)
    81	
    82	**There is no `green-by-redeferral` status.** A re-deferred criterion
    83	is neither green nor red; it is `re-deferred`. Any summary, dashboard,
    84	or review that treats a re-deferred criterion as green is rejected on
    85	§6 Precedent firewall grounds (see §Decision.3).
    86	
    87	---
    88	
    89	**CC#P2-1 — One-workflow parity.**
    90	
    91	The target workflow locked in `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md`
    92	§Target workflow for first parity (as of this ADR: **`explore`**) runs
    93	end-to-end in circuit-next with real agent dispatch and produces the
    94	same artifact shape as the corresponding reference Circuit workflow at
    95	`~/Code/circuit`.
    96	
    97	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
    98	  - Test file: `tests/runner/explore-e2e-parity.test.ts` (to be
    99	    authored at P2.5; exact filename may be refined, but the test
   100	    must exist and must exercise the full-spine explore fixture
   101	    through the runtime boundary using the `agent` adapter).
   102	  - Golden artifacts: `tests/fixtures/golden/explore/` (committed at
   103	    P2.5; sha256 of normalized-JSON result artifacts; per plan Open
   104	    Question #4).
   105	  - Contract: `specs/contracts/explore.md` (authored at P2.3).
   106	  - Fixture: `.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json` (authored at
   107	    P2.3, extended at P2.5).
   108	- **Non-substitutable failure conditions:** byte-shape parity with the
   109	  reference Circuit `explore` artifact on at least one canonical
   110	  phase transition; failing the golden assertion must fail CC#P2-1.
   111	- **Retarget rule:** if the target workflow is reselected (plan permits
   112	  `review` as a scope-reducing fallback), this ADR is amended in place
   113	  via the retarget checklist at §Decision.4b. Silent rename in the
   114	  plan is rejected on §6 grounds.
   115	
   116	---
   117	
   118	**CC#P2-2 — Real agent dispatch.**
   119	
   120	At least one non-dry-run adapter (`agent` in-process Anthropic subagent
   121	or `codex` cross-process) lands with a concrete request → receipt →
   122	result round-trip verified by the runtime boundary (event-writer →
   123	reducer → result-writer) and exercised by a fixture.
   124	
   125	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   126	  - Adapter file: `src/runtime/adapters/agent.ts` (or sibling for
   127	    `codex`-first flip per plan Open Question #2).
   128	  - Test file: `tests/runner/agent-dispatch-roundtrip.test.ts` (to be
   129	    authored at P2.4; must assert on a durable dispatch transcript,
   130	    not byte-shape of a single output).
   131	  - **Durable dispatch transcript requirement (non-substitutable):**
   132	    the test must verify the event log contains, in order, a
   133	    `dispatch.started` event whose `resolved_adapter.name` is the
   134	    real adapter id, a `dispatch.request` with a non-empty request
   135	    payload hash, a `dispatch.receipt` with a receipt id, and a
   136	    `dispatch.result` with a result artifact hash. The reducer must
   137	    have consumed that sequence and the result-writer must have
   138	    produced the result artifact. A mock adapter returning a fixed
   139	    byte string cannot satisfy this test even if the byte-shape
   140	    matches.
   141	  - **CI skip semantics:** if the agent smoke path is CI-skipped via
   142	    `AGENT_SMOKE=0`, the ADR-0007 close claim requires a local smoke
   143	    artifact at `tests/fixtures/agent-smoke/last-run.json` with:
   144	    timestamp, commit SHA of the smoke run, adapter id, request/
   145	    receipt/result hash triple, and a manifest signature. An audit
   146	    check at Phase 2 close verifies that this artifact's commit SHA
   147	    is an ancestor of HEAD.
   148	- **Binding:** `dispatch_realness` product ratchet (plan §Product
   149	  ratchets Phase 2 will carry) advances to `active — satisfied` when
   150	  the test above passes on a non-CI-skip invocation and the local
   151	  smoke artifact is on-file.
   152	
   153	---
   154	
   155	**CC#P2-3 — Plugin command registration.**
   156	
   157	`/circuit:<workflow>` slash commands exposed via `.claude-plugin/`,
   158	invokable in Claude Code (not merely manifest/markdown closure), with
   159	the plugin manifest (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`) carrying a command
   160	block whose entries are closure-consistent with the markdown files
   161	under `.claude-plugin/commands/`.
   162	
   163	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   164	  - Audit check: `checkPluginCommandClosure` (function name in
   165	    `scripts/audit.mjs`; number assigned at implementation time using
   166	    the next-available-slot rule at §Decision.7). Verifies:
   167	    - `plugin.json` `commands` array entries each correspond to an
   168	      existing `.claude-plugin/commands/*.md` file.
   169	    - Each `commands/*.md` file has a non-empty `description` and
   170	      body.
   171	    - At least one command entry for the target workflow (currently
   172	      `/circuit:explore`) and one for the router (`/circuit:run`)
   173	      exists.
   174	  - **Claude Code invokability binding (non-substitutable):** a
   175	    contract test under `tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts` (to
   176	    be authored at P2.2) parses `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` with the
   177	    plugin-manifest schema and asserts the workflow command is
   178	    registered; additionally, a manual-invokability check note lives
   179	    in the P2.11 slice evidence (a recorded invocation of the
   180	    `/circuit:<workflow>` command inside Claude Code with a transcript
   181	    or screenshot, committed under `specs/reviews/p2-11-invoke-
   182	    evidence.md`). Closure alone is insufficient.
   183	- **Binding:** `plugin_surface_present` product ratchet advances to
   184	  `active — partial` after P2.2 and `active — satisfied` after P2.11.
   185	
   186	---
   187	
   188	**CC#P2-4 — Session hooks.**
   189	
   190	SessionStart continuity resume + SessionEnd handoff wired through
   191	`.claude/hooks/` to `circuit-engine`, matching the behavior reference
   192	Circuit already exhibits (pending-continuity pickup, stark HANDOFF
   193	banner at stopping points per operator feedback memory), **plus**
   194	continuity-record lifecycle proven end-to-end (create → persist →
   195	resume on next session → clear on `done`).
   196	
   197	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   198	  - Hook scripts: `.claude/hooks/SessionStart.sh` and
   199	    `.claude/hooks/SessionEnd.sh` shelling to
   200	    `.circuit/bin/circuit-engine continuity`.
   201	  - Audit check: `checkSessionHooksPresent` verifies both hook scripts
   202	    exist, are executable, and shell to the circuit-engine helper.
   203	  - Test file: `tests/runner/continuity-lifecycle.test.ts` (to be
   204	    authored at P2.7) seeds a continuity record, simulates SessionEnd
   205	    persistence, re-reads the record via the SessionStart resume path,
   206	    simulates `done`, and asserts the record is cleared. Must assert
   207	    on the pending-continuity banner content (operator feedback memory
   208	    requirement: stark HANDOFF SAVED / NOT SAVED at stopping points).
   209	- **Binding:** continuity contract invariants in
   210	  `specs/contracts/continuity.md` remain test-enforced through the new
   211	  lifecycle test (not substituted).
   212	
   213	---
   214	
   215	**CC#P2-5 — P2-MODEL-EFFORT landed.**
   216	
   217	Workflow contract v0.3 — explicit per-step `model` + `effort`
   218	assignment — landed, with schema-parity tests and a named audit check
   219	rejecting unknown-model-ids.
   220	
   221	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   222	  - Schema: `src/schemas/workflow.ts` v0.3 with per-step `model` and
   223	    `effort` fields.
   224	  - Test files: `tests/contracts/schema-parity.test.ts` extended for
   225	    v0.3; plus `tests/contracts/workflow-model-effort.test.ts` (to be
   226	    authored at P2-MODEL-EFFORT) exercising per-step assignment.
   227	  - Audit check: `checkUnknownModelIds` verifies that every `model`
   228	    string in every workflow fixture under `.claude-plugin/skills/*/
   229	    circuit.json` is a known model id (enumerated in the workflow
   230	    schema or a sibling registry file). Rejects unknown ids.
   231	  - Slice spec: `specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md §Slice
   232	    P2-MODEL-EFFORT` (incorporated by reference; not redefined here).
   233	
   234	---
   235	
   236	**CC#P2-6 — Spine policy coverage (aligned with plan P2.3).**
   237	
   238	The target workflow's fixture declares and exercises the canonical
   239	phase set its kind requires. For `explore`, the canonical set matches
   240	the plan's P2.3 deliverable: **Frame → Analyze → Synthesize → Review →
   241	Close** (five phases). This aligns with `specs/plans/phase-2-
   242	implementation.md §P2.3 Deliverable` wording *"full-spine phases — at
   243	minimum Frame → Analyze → Synthesize → Review → Close mapped to
   244	canonical phase ids"* and resolves plan Open Question #5 to **full-
   245	spine at Standard rigor**.
   246	
   247	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   248	  - Contract: `specs/contracts/explore.md` declares canonical phase
   249	    set for `explore` as {Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review, Close}.
   250	  - Fixture: `.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json` declares
   251	    steps covering those five phases.
   252	  - Audit check: `checkSpineCoverage` (reusable across workflows)
   253	    verifies the fixture's declared phase set matches the canonical
   254	    set for its workflow kind. Rejects missing phases.
   255	  - Test file: `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts` (to be
   256	    authored at P2.3) property-tests the spine-coverage check against
   257	    fixtures with missing/extra/renamed phases.
   258	- **Plan amendment note:** this ADR resolves plan Open Question #5 by
   259	  adopting the full-spine option. The plan's Open Question #5 is
   260	  amended to `RESOLVED 2026-04-21 via ADR-0007 CC#P2-6: full-spine
