/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba specs/contracts/explore.md | sed -n '1,380p'" in /Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	contract: explore
     3	status: draft
     4	version: 0.1
     5	schema_source: .claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json (fixture; no dedicated src/schemas/ file at v0.1 — explore is a workflow-specific contract, not a new domain contract. Artifact schemas authored at P2.10.)
     6	last_updated: 2026-04-21
     7	depends_on: [workflow, phase, step, selection, rigor, lane, skill]
     8	codex_adversarial_review: specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md
     9	artifact_ids:
    10	  - explore.brief
    11	  - explore.analysis
    12	  - explore.synthesis
    13	  - explore.review-verdict
    14	  - explore.result
    15	invariant_ids: [EXPLORE-I1]
    16	property_ids:
    17	  - explore.prop.canonical_phase_set_is_correct
    18	  - explore.prop.artifact_emission_ordered
    19	  - explore.prop.review_after_synthesis
    20	  - explore.prop.no_skip_to_close
    21	  - explore.prop.reachable_close_only_via_review
    22	---
    23	
    24	# Explore Workflow Contract
    25	
    26	The **Explore** workflow is circuit-next's first-parity target per
    27	ADR-0007 CC#P2-1 (operator decision 2026-04-21 adopting Codex
    28	challenger recommendation over in-session Claude methodology
    29	recommendation of `review`). It exercises a full-spine investigation:
    30	frame the investigation, analyze the subject, synthesize findings,
    31	review the synthesis adversarially, and close with a final artifact.
    32	
    33	Unlike the base domain contracts (`workflow.md`, `step.md`, `phase.md`,
    34	etc.), which govern the shape of ANY workflow, this contract governs a
    35	SPECIFIC workflow instance: `explore`. It binds the workflow's
    36	canonical phase set, declares the artifact ids it emits, and names
    37	the one runtime-enforced invariant (EXPLORE-I1) plus four deferred
    38	properties that become enforceable at later slices.
    39	
    40	## Scope note (explicit)
    41	
    42	This is **not** a runtime-schema contract with its own `src/schemas/`
    43	file. The `explore` fixture is validated by the base `Workflow`
    44	schema (`src/schemas/workflow.ts`). This contract is the
    45	**workflow-specific** discipline layer over that base schema — it
    46	names one invariant (EXPLORE-I1) that the base schema cannot express
    47	(because it is workflow-kind-specific, not workflow-general), plus
    48	five artifact ids the workflow's phases emit, plus four property ids
    49	tracking deferred semantic guarantees for later-slice enforcement.
    50	
    51	If future refactoring introduces a workflow-kind concept at the Zod
    52	layer (e.g., `kind: 'explore'` field), EXPLORE-I1 and the four
    53	properties migrate into that layer as kind-specific checks, and this
    54	contract becomes a pointer to the kind schema.
    55	
    56	**Workflow-kind seam (Codex MED 8 fold-in).** P2.3 uses the fixture's
    57	top-level `id` field (the string `'explore'`) as the kind signal. The
    58	base `Workflow` schema has no `kind` field at v0.2. This is a
    59	**temporary adapter** — the correct long-term shape is `Workflow.kind`
    60	as a first-class field, resolved at P2-MODEL-EFFORT (workflow schema
    61	v0.3) or a dedicated slice. Until then, `checkSpineCoverage`
    62	hardcodes the `{id → canonical set}` map at `scripts/audit.mjs`
    63	`WORKFLOW_KIND_CANONICAL_SETS`. Reopen triggers for this seam:
    64	duplicate `id` across skill directories; an `explore-mini` or
    65	`research` fixture with no `kind` binding to this contract; or
    66	landing of the `Workflow.kind` field.
    67	
    68	## Ubiquitous language
    69	
    70	See `specs/domain.md#core-types` for canonical definitions of
    71	**Workflow**, **Phase**, **Step**. This contract adds five artifact
    72	ids:
    73	
    74	- **Explore brief** (`explore.brief`): the framing artifact emitted by
    75	  the Frame phase. Names the subject, the operator's task statement,
    76	  and the success condition for the investigation.
    77	- **Explore analysis** (`explore.analysis`): the artifact emitted by
    78	  the Analyze phase. Decomposes the subject into named aspects with
    79	  evidence citations.
    80	- **Explore synthesis** (`explore.synthesis`): the artifact emitted by
    81	  the Synthesize phase. Produces the investigation's primary output —
    82	  a recommendation, decision candidate set, or investigation
    83	  conclusion — with explicit mapping back to the brief's success
    84	  condition.
    85	- **Explore review verdict** (`explore.review-verdict`): the artifact
    86	  emitted by the Review phase. Adversarial pass over the synthesis;
    87	  reports objections, missed angles, and overall verdict.
    88	- **Explore result** (`explore.result`): the aggregate artifact
    89	  emitted by the Close phase. A summary + verdict-snapshot plus
    90	  pointers to the four prior artifacts. The run's final return shape.
    91	
    92	## Canonical phase set (ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 binding) — and the title-to-canonical translation
    93	
    94	ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 names the explore canonical phase set using
    95	**workflow-specific titles**: `{Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review,
    96	Close}`. Those titles are human-readable and match the reference
    97	Circuit explore workflow. They are **not** all canonical phase ids —
    98	`Synthesize` in particular is not in the CanonicalPhase enum at
    99	`src/schemas/phase.ts` (which is the seven-phase spine `frame,
   100	analyze, plan, act, verify, review, close`).
   101	
   102	This contract records the **title-to-canonical translation** as
   103	follows. The translation is the authoritative reading of CC#P2-6 for
   104	v0.1 — any future ADR amending CC#P2-6 or this translation must clear
   105	ADR-0007 §6 Precedent firewall:
   106	
   107	| Workflow-specific title | Canonical phase id | Role |
   108	|---|---|---|
   109	| Frame                  | `frame`    | State the subject and success condition. |
   110	| Analyze                | `analyze`  | Decompose the subject into aspects with evidence. |
   111	| Synthesize             | `act`      | Produce the investigation's primary output. |
   112	| Review                 | `review`   | Adversarial pass over the synthesis. |
   113	| Close                  | `close`    | Final aggregate artifact + closure. |
   114	
   115	**Canonical set:** `{frame, analyze, act, review, close}`.
   116	**Omits:** `{plan, verify}` (partial spine).
   117	
   118	**Rationale for `Synthesize → act` (Codex HIGH 4 fold-in — rejected
   119	alternative).** The contentious mapping is `Synthesize`. Two
   120	candidate canonicals are reasonable:
   121	
   122	- **`act`** (chosen): Synthesize is the primary work-producing phase.
   123	  It consumes the brief + analysis and emits the explore.synthesis
   124	  artifact (the investigation's output). In the canonical seven-phase
   125	  spine, `act` is the "do the work" phase — the place where the
   126	  workflow's primary deliverable is produced. This matches.
   127	- **`plan`** (rejected): Synthesize could be read as producing a plan
   128	  for downstream action. This reading would imply the explore
   129	  workflow's primary output IS a plan (to be executed elsewhere).
   130	  That is one legitimate use case, but it is narrower than the
   131	  general explore contract: an explore workflow whose synthesis is a
   132	  decision artifact (not a plan) would be miscategorized. Picking
   133	  `plan` would also leave the `act` canonical unfilled, producing a
   134	  different partial-spine shape (omits = {act, verify}) that would
   135	  not match PHASE-I4 semantic-adequacy expectations for the act
   136	  phase.
   137	
   138	**Rationale for `plan` omission:** explore is an investigation
   139	workflow. Investigation-planning is folded into the Frame phase
   140	(where the subject, success condition, and investigation scope are
   141	stated). There is no separate plan-mode producing a plan artifact;
   142	the explore.brief serves that role.
   143	
   144	**Rationale for `verify` omission (Codex MED 9 fold-in — weaker-
   145	substitute wording).** Explore produces investigation output (not
   146	executable artifacts), so there is no mechanical verification step
   147	analogous to `build`'s test-run gate. The Review phase provides an
   148	adversarial pass over the synthesis that is a **weaker substitute**
   149	for verify, not a full replacement: the v0.1 Review phase has
   150	`executor: "orchestrator"` and `kind: "synthesis"` — it is not a
   151	distinct-adapter review dispatch, does not cross a Knight-Leveson
   152	model boundary, and does not emit a result-verdict gate distinct
   153	from the phase gate. A future variant that wants stronger review
   154	(e.g., dispatching Codex as the reviewer via the P2.4 agent adapter)
   155	would either strengthen the Review phase semantics in a subsequent
   156	slice or introduce a `verify` canonical with a mechanical check. v0.1
   157	is scoped narrower than that.
   158	
   159	## Invariant (single — EXPLORE-I1)
   160	
   161	The runtime MUST reject any `explore`-kinded workflow that violates
   162	EXPLORE-I1. Other semantic guarantees are recorded as **deferred
   163	properties** (see §Deferred properties below). This single-invariant
   164	shape is the Codex HIGH 1 fold-in: EXPLORE-I2..I5 from v0.1-draft
   165	were demoted from "MUST reject at runtime" to "property-tracked
   166	obligations satisfied by later slices" because the v0.1 contract-
   167	surface-enforced-at-runtime layer cannot express them without
   168	implementing enforcement that belongs to later slices.
   169	
   170	- **EXPLORE-I1 — Canonical phase set matches kind, spine_policy is
   171	  partial with omits {plan, verify}.** Any workflow fixture whose
   172	  top-level `id` equals the string `'explore'` MUST:
   173	  1. Declare phases whose `canonical` fields collectively equal the
   174	     set `{frame, analyze, act, review, close}`. Extra canonicals
   175	     (e.g. `plan`, `verify`) are rejected; missing canonicals are
   176	     rejected.
   177	  2. Declare `spine_policy.mode = 'partial'` with `omits = [plan,
   178	     verify]` (order-independent set equality).
   179	
   180	  **Scope of EXPLORE-I1 enforcement (Codex HIGH 3 fold-in — narrowed
   181	  to what is actually enforced).** `checkSpineCoverage` (Check 24)
   182	  enforces (1) and (2) above. It does **not** currently enforce:
   183	  - Rationale length or rationale-content (the phase.md PHASE-I4
   184	    rationale ≥20 chars is a schema-level check, not an
   185	    explore-specific semantic check).
   186	  - The `id`-vs-filename convention (e.g. a fixture at
   187	    `.claude-plugin/skills/explore-mini/` with `id: 'explore'` would
   188	    pass Check 24 today; that would need a directory-binding check
   189	    in a later slice).
   190	  - Full `Workflow.safeParse` validation (Check 24 hand-parses the
   191	    canonical phase set from `fixture.phases` without running the
   192	    base schema; malformed fixtures pass Check 24 if their phase
   193	    canonicals match, even when their steps/entry-modes/routes are
   194	    broken).
   195	
   196	  These scope-gaps are recorded honestly here. A stricter variant
   197	  of EXPLORE-I1 (parsing the fixture through `Workflow.safeParse`
   198	  + enforcing rationale content) is deferred to P2.5.
   199	
   200	  Enforced by `checkSpineCoverage` (Check 24 at P2.3 landing) and
   201	  `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts`.
   202	
   203	## Deferred properties (enforcement-deferred, ledger-phase2-property)
   204	
   205	The following properties describe semantic guarantees the contract
   206	intends the `explore` workflow to satisfy but that are not runtime-
   207	enforced at v0.1. Each is recorded in `specs/invariants.json` as
   208	`enforcement_state: phase2-property` with a concrete
   209	`reopen_condition`. When P2.5 (end-to-end fixture test) lands, these
   210	properties gain test bindings; the contract amends at that slice to
   211	reflect the new enforcement state.
   212	
   213	- **`explore.prop.canonical_phase_set_is_correct`** — (test-enforced
   214	  via `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts`). Property-id sibling
   215	  of EXPLORE-I1 for ledger addressability. One or more test titles
   216	  in the spine-coverage suite contain this property-id token so the
   217	  ledger binding is honest (Codex MED 6 fold-in).
   218	- **`explore.prop.artifact_emission_ordered`** — The five phases
   219	  emit artifacts in order: Frame → `explore.brief`, Analyze →
   220	  `explore.analysis`, Synthesize → `explore.synthesis`, Review →
   221	  `explore.review-verdict`, Close → `explore.result`. A fixture that
   222	  routes Analyze→Review (skipping Synthesize) or whose Frame step
   223	  emits no `explore.brief` violates this property. Deferred to P2.5.
   224	- **`explore.prop.review_after_synthesis`** — The Review phase MUST
   225	  execute after the Synthesize phase in every viable execution path.
   226	  Reviewing a synthesis that doesn't exist is structurally incoherent.
   227	  Deferred to P2.5.
   228	- **`explore.prop.no_skip_to_close`** — No execution path from any
   229	  `EntryMode.start_at` reaches `@complete` without passing through
   230	  the Review phase. Deferred to P2.5.
   231	- **`explore.prop.reachable_close_only_via_review`** — Symmetric to
   232	  `no_skip_to_close`; stated for emphasis. Deferred to P2.5.
   233	
   234	**Enforcement consolidation (Codex MED 7 fold-in).** The
   235	`target_slice` in `specs/invariants.json` for these properties is
   236	P2.5 (the end-to-end fixture run, currently planned as slice 38+
   237	depending on P2.2→P2.4 drift). References to "property harness Slice
   238	29" in prior draft ledger entries were carried over from
   239	`workflow.prop.*` property ids and are not the right milestone for
   240	`explore.prop.*` — they were amended at P2.3 landing.
   241	
   242	## Pre-conditions
   243	
   244	- The fixture file at `.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json`
   245	  parses under the base `Workflow.safeParse`.
   246	- The fixture's `id` equals the string literal `'explore'`.
   247	- All five artifact ids under §artifact_ids are registered in
   248	  `specs/artifacts.json` with appropriate writers and readers.
   249	
   250	## Post-conditions
   251	
   252	After an `explore` fixture is accepted:
   253	
   254	- The fixture's phase set covers `{frame, analyze, act, review,
   255	  close}` with `spine_policy.mode = 'partial'` and
   256	  `omits = [plan, verify]`.
   257	- The fixture emits five named artifacts in phase order
   258	  (enforcement at P2.5).
   259	- The fixture exposes at least one entry mode (`default` or
   260	  `explore`) starting at the Frame phase's step.
   261	- No execution path reaches `@complete` without passing through the
   262	  Review phase (enforcement at P2.5).
   263	
   264	## Artifact reader/writer graph (normative — Codex MED 5 fold-in)
   265	
   266	The following table is the **authoritative reader/writer graph** for
   267	v0.1. `specs/artifacts.json` writers/readers lists and
   268	`.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json` step `reads` arrays
   269	MUST match this table exactly; any divergence is a ratchet violation.
   270	
   271	| Artifact                 | Writer (phase/step) | Readers (phase/step)                                                                 |
   272	|--------------------------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
   273	| `explore.brief`          | Frame / frame-step  | Analyze / analyze-step; Synthesize / synthesize-step; Review / review-step           |
   274	| `explore.analysis`       | Analyze / analyze-step | Synthesize / synthesize-step; Review / review-step                                |
   275	| `explore.synthesis`      | Synthesize / synthesize-step | Review / review-step; Close / close-step                                     |
   276	| `explore.review-verdict` | Review / review-step | Close / close-step                                                                  |
   277	| `explore.result`         | Close / close-step  | *(none — terminal artifact; consumed by the run result consumer only)*               |
   278	
   279	**Close reads synthesis + review-verdict only** (not brief or
   280	analysis). The rationale: the synthesis artifact encapsulates the
   281	investigation output; the review verdict encapsulates the
   282	adversarial pass. The brief + analysis are upstream inputs already
   283	composed into the synthesis; re-reading them at Close would
   284	duplicate input rather than add value. A future slice may widen
   285	Close to read all prior artifacts if the aggregate result shape
   286	requires it — that is an explicit amendment trigger.
   287	
   288	## `schema_sections` gate placeholder note (Codex MED 10 fold-in)
   289	
   290	The fixture's `schema_sections` gates declare required field names
   291	(e.g. `subject`, `success_condition`, `recommendation`) that are
   292	**provisional field-name guards**, not references to any schema
   293	source. The artifact schemas these gate names reference do not yet
   294	exist; they are deferred to P2.10 (artifact schema set, plan
   295	§Mid-term slices).
   296	
   297	This means:
   298	
   299	- At v0.1, the gate checks produce a pass/fail on "does the artifact
   300	  JSON contain the named top-level keys?" — not on "does the
   301	  artifact validate against a typed schema?".
   302	- A future P2.10 slice reconciles the gate `required` arrays with
   303	  the concrete artifact schemas and the `specs/artifacts.json`
   304	  `schema_exports` fields in one slice. Any drift between the three
   305	  is a ratchet violation at P2.10 landing.
   306	
   307	## Property ids (reserved; some enforced now, most deferred)
   308	
   309	Registered in `specs/invariants.json`. See §Deferred properties
   310	above for semantics.
   311	
   312	- `explore.prop.canonical_phase_set_is_correct` — **test-enforced at
   313	  P2.3** via `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts` describe title.
   314	- `explore.prop.artifact_emission_ordered` — deferred to P2.5.
   315	- `explore.prop.review_after_synthesis` — deferred to P2.5.
   316	- `explore.prop.no_skip_to_close` — deferred to P2.5.
   317	- `explore.prop.reachable_close_only_via_review` — deferred to P2.5.
   318	
   319	## Reopen conditions
   320	
   321	This contract is reopened if any of:
   322	
   323	1. **Target retarget.** Per ADR-0007 §4b retarget checklist, if the
   324	   operator reselects `review` as the first-parity target, this
   325	   contract is deprecated in place (status → `retargeted`) and a new
   326	   `specs/contracts/review.md` replaces it as CC#P2-1's binding.
   327	2. **Canonical phase set change.** If a future slice amends the
   328	   canonical phase set for `explore` (e.g., adds `plan` back in, or
   329	   maps Synthesize to `plan` instead of `act`), this contract must
   330	   be amended and re-reviewed by Codex per ADR-0007 §6 Precedent
   331	   firewall. The title-to-canonical translation table is the
   332	   authoritative surface; any change to it is an ADR-level decision.
   333	3. **Artifact-schema refactor.** If the five artifact ids gain
   334	   concrete Zod schemas at P2.10, this contract amends §artifact
   335	   id section with schema file pointers and the artifact
   336	   round-trip invariant.
   337	4. **Workflow-kind concept introduced.** If the base Workflow
   338	   schema gains a `kind` field (projected at P2-MODEL-EFFORT or
   339	   dedicated slice), EXPLORE-I1 and the deferred properties migrate
   340	   into that layer as kind-specific checks, and this contract
   341	   becomes a pointer to the kind schema. The workflow-kind seam
   342	   note in §Scope note records this migration target.
   343	5. **P2.5 lands without enforcing deferred properties** (Codex LOW
   344	   12 fold-in). If P2.5 end-to-end fixture test lands but
   345	   `explore.prop.artifact_emission_ordered`,
   346	   `explore.prop.review_after_synthesis`,
   347	   `explore.prop.no_skip_to_close`, and
   348	   `explore.prop.reachable_close_only_via_review` are not promoted
   349	   from `phase2-property` to `test-enforced`, reopen this contract
   350	   to re-evaluate the deferral plan.
   351	6. **Artifact reader/writer graph drift** (Codex LOW 12 fold-in).
   352	   If the §Artifact reader/writer graph table diverges from
   353	   `specs/artifacts.json` or the fixture, reopen to resolve the
   354	   divergence — the contract table is authoritative.
   355	7. **Gate/schema reconciliation skipped at P2.10** (Codex LOW 12
   356	   fold-in). If P2.10 lands without reconciling the fixture's
   357	   `schema_sections` `required` arrays with concrete artifact
   358	   schemas and `specs/artifacts.json` `schema_exports`, reopen to
   359	   hold P2.10 accountable.
   360	8. **`explore.result` needs a dedicated schema** (Codex LOW 12
   361	   fold-in). If downstream consumers (run-result readers, operator
   362	   summaries) depend on a richer `explore.result` shape than the
   363	   current `{summary, verdict_snapshot}` placeholder, reopen to
   364	   author the result schema explicitly and update the artifact
   365	   registry row.
   366	
   367	## Authority
   368	
   369	- `specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md §Decision.1
   370	  CC#P2-1` (one-workflow parity — `explore` target)
   371	- `specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md §Decision.1
   372	  CC#P2-6` (spine policy coverage — canonical set
   373	  {frame, analyze, act, review, close} mapped from workflow-
   374	  specific titles Frame/Analyze/Synthesize/Review/Close; Open
   375	  Question #5 resolved to full-spine Standard rigor)
   376	- `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §P2.3` (slice framing)
   377	- `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §Target workflow for first
   378	  parity` (operator decision + Codex rationale preserved verbatim)

 succeeded in 0ms:
   236	**CC#P2-6 — Spine policy coverage (aligned with plan P2.3).**
   237	
   238	The target workflow's fixture declares and exercises the canonical
   239	phase set its kind requires. For `explore`, the canonical set matches
   240	the plan's P2.3 deliverable: **Frame → Analyze → Synthesize → Review →
   241	Close** (five phases). This aligns with `specs/plans/phase-2-
   242	implementation.md §P2.3 Deliverable` wording *"full-spine phases — at
   243	minimum Frame → Analyze → Synthesize → Review → Close mapped to
   244	canonical phase ids"* and resolves plan Open Question #5 to **full-
   245	spine at Standard rigor**.
   246	
   247	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   248	  - Contract: `specs/contracts/explore.md` declares canonical phase
   249	    set for `explore` as {Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review, Close}.
   250	  - Fixture: `.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json` declares
   251	    steps covering those five phases.
   252	  - Audit check: `checkSpineCoverage` (reusable across workflows)
   253	    verifies the fixture's declared phase set matches the canonical
   254	    set for its workflow kind. Rejects missing phases.
   255	  - Test file: `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts` (to be
   256	    authored at P2.3) property-tests the spine-coverage check against
   257	    fixtures with missing/extra/renamed phases.
   258	- **Plan amendment note:** this ADR resolves plan Open Question #5 by
   259	  adopting the full-spine option. The plan's Open Question #5 is
   260	  amended to `RESOLVED 2026-04-21 via ADR-0007 CC#P2-6: full-spine
   261	  Standard rigor`.
   262	
   263	---
   264	
   265	**CC#P2-7 — Container isolation — RE-DEFERRED (with explicit weakening disclosure).**
   266	
   267	CLAUDE.md §Phase discipline §Phase 2 contains the sentence:
   268	*"Implementer runs in a container with distinct UID; `specs/`,
   269	`tests/properties/visible/`, `tests/mutation/`, `specs/behavioral/`, CI
   270	configuration mounted read-only; `tests/properties/hidden/` not mounted
   271	at all."* CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #1–#4 restate the substance.
   272	
   273	**ADR-0007 weakens Phase 2 close by accepting unisolated implementer
   274	execution for Phase 2 close.** This is weaker than the CLAUDE.md
   275	policy-level statement. The weakening is recorded openly here, not as
   276	an appendix, and is surfaced in §Consequences.Accepted and in
   277	PROJECT_STATE.md's P2.1 landing note.
   278	
   279	**Scope of the weakening (narrow):**
   280	
   281	- Only the *close-criterion layer* is weakened: Phase 2 may close
   282	  without container isolation implemented.
   283	- The policy-level statements (CLAUDE.md §Phase discipline §Phase 2
   284	  sentence + Hard invariants #1–#4) remain load-bearing for any slice
   285	  that claims to run under isolation or whose failure mode depends on
   286	  isolation. A slice violating the policy statements without
   287	  declaring re-deferral citation or Break-Glass lane is rejected by
   288	  audit (see interim enforcement below).
   289	
   290	**Rationale for re-deferral (independent of ADR-0006 precedent):**
   291	
   292	- No current slice in the Phase 2 plan (P2.1–P2.11 + P2-MODEL-EFFORT)
   293	  requires container isolation to land honestly. Each near-term slice
   294	  (P2.1 governance; P2.2 plugin scaffold; P2.3 contract authorship;
   295	  P2.4 adapter; P2.5 end-to-end fixture) is authored by the operator
   296	  + Claude/Codex pair in the operator session, not by a distinct
   297	  implementer process with filesystem authority.
   298	- Forcing container isolation into Phase 2 close would couple Phase 2
   299	  closure to Tier-2+ substrate work (container tooling, UID mapping,
   300	  mount discipline) that `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md
   301	  §Deferred to Phase 2+` has already placed outside Phase 2 scope.
   302	- The honest distinction is between (i) LLM drafting in the operator
   303	  session, under operator acceptance, where no process separate from
   304	  the operator's editor exists, and (ii) an agent/tool process with
   305	  filesystem authority (adapter-driven writes, workflow-execution
   306	  writes, hook-driven writes, CI-driven writes). Only case (ii)
   307	  requires isolation; case (i) does not. The trigger conditions below
   308	  make this distinction operative.
   309	
   310	**Named trigger conditions for re-opening CC#P2-7 (any one triggers
   311	re-opening; list is extensible — adding a trigger is an amendment,
   312	removing one requires a §6 firewall pass):**
   313	
   314	1. **Implementer-operator split.** A Phase 2+ slice lands an actual
   315	   separate implementer process/user/role with filesystem authority
   316	   (not merely a naming convention, and not LLM drafting in the
   317	   operator session). The first such slice must cite this ADR and
   318	   promote CC#P2-7 from `re-deferred` to `active`.
   319	2. **Hidden test pool lands.** Any `tests/properties/hidden/` content
   320	   is committed to the repo, or any tooling begins to treat a set of
   321	   tests as "hidden" (not mounted to implementer).
   322	3. **Mutation-testing gate lands.** `tests/mutation/` content or a
   323	   mutation-testing ratchet begins enforcing invariants that depend
   324	   on specs+tests being read-only to the mutator.
   325	4. **External contributor (non-operator) implementer.** Any slice
   326	   implemented by a human or agent other than the operator or
   327	   Claude/Codex under operator direction, on operator-owned
   328	   hardware, in an operator-owned session.
   329	5. **Phase 3 open.** Regardless of other triggers, Phase 2 close must
   330	   re-evaluate CC#P2-7's re-deferral as part of the Phase 2 → Phase 3
   331	   transition. If no other trigger has fired, Phase 3 may still
   332	   re-defer, but must do so by its own ADR (not by citing this one).
   333	6. **Agent/tool process with repo write capability.** Any landed
   334	   adapter, hook, workflow step, plugin command, or subprocess that
   335	   can (by its type or by runtime capability) write under `specs/`,
   336	   `tests/`, `.github/`, `.claude-plugin/`, `src/`, `scripts/`, or
   337	   CI/hooks configuration. Example: a P2.4 `agent` adapter run with
   338	   tool use that has file-write capability.
   339	7. **Protected-surface mutation by workflow execution.** Any
   340	   instance of the runner or a workflow step producing changes to
   341	   `specs/`, `tests/properties/visible/`, `tests/mutation/`,
   342	   `specs/behavioral/`, or CI config during a non-interactive run
   343	   (i.e. not a human-typed edit in the operator session).
   344	8. **New property, behavioral, mutation, or CI enforcement surface.**
   345	   Any new visible property test, behavioral spec, mutation gate, or
   346	   CI enforcement check whose correctness depends on read-only
   347	   specs/tests for the implementer.
   348	9. **Reference-Circuit parity harness.** Any harness that mounts both
   349	   the reference Circuit tree at `~/Code/circuit` and the
   350	   circuit-next tree simultaneously, or otherwise requires source-of-
   351	   truth separation between reference and target.
   352	
   353	**Interim enforcement surface (new audit check, adds an audit-
   354	coverage ratchet advance in this slice).**
   355	
   356	A new audit check, `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation`, is added to
   357	`scripts/audit.mjs` in the same ceremony commit as this ADR. The check
   358	inspects each slice commit in Phase 2 (commits after `0223d11` whose
   359	body declares a Lane) and rejects the commit if **both** of the
   360	following hold:
   361	
   362	- The commit's file changes touch any of `specs/`, `tests/`,
   363	  `.github/`, `.claude-plugin/`, `src/`, `scripts/`, or CI/hooks
   364	  configuration; **and**
   365	- The commit body does not contain either (i) the string
   366	  `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)`
   367	  or (ii) the string `Isolation: re-deferred per ADR-0007 CC#P2-7` or
   368	  (iii) a Break-Glass lane declaration.
   369	
   370	This is an in-commit-body citation requirement, not a filesystem
   371	capability check. Its job is to force slice authors to declare
   372	isolation status explicitly rather than silently normalize unisolated
   373	Phase 2 execution. The check is a Phase-2-only gate (it does not run
   374	against pre-Phase-2 commits) and it is tuned to be satisfiable with
   375	a one-line commit-body addition.
   376	
   377	**Audit numbering:** the new check takes the next available audit-
   378	check slot after Check 22 (currently the verify gate). Check numbering
   379	is not fixed in this ADR; the implementing commit must preserve
   380	monotonic printed Check-N order (see §Decision.7).
   381	
   382	---
   383	
   384	**CC#P2-8 — Close review (final blocking review, not artifact-presence only).**
   385	
   386	Phase 2 close requires a final adversarial review that, unlike
   387	Slice-31a-style artifact-presence checks, **fails closed** if any
   388	prior criterion is not in the required state. CC#P2-8 is the final
   389	blocking gate for Phase 2 close.
   390	
   391	- **Enforcement binding (executable):**
   392	  - Close matrix file: `specs/reviews/phase-2-close-matrix.md` with
   393	    exactly one row per CC#P2-N (N = 1..7) containing:
   394	    - Criterion id
   395	    - Status (`active — satisfied` / `active — red` / `re-deferred`)
   396	    - Evidence path (test file, audit function, artifact file —
   397	      non-substitutable per CC#P2-N enforcement binding above)
   398	    - Passing-commit SHA (for `active — satisfied`) or ADR citation
   399	      (for `re-deferred`) or red-diagnosis pointer (for `active —
   400	      red`)
   401	    - Structural evidence type (non-LLM mechanical / operator /
   402	      cross-model challenger / test-enforced / audit-enforced)
   403	  - Codex challenger pass: `specs/reviews/phase-2-close-codex.md`
   404	    with frontmatter `review_kind: challenger-objection-list`,
   405	    `target_kind: phase-close`, objection list in the same shape as
   406	    `specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md`. HIGH + MED must be folded in
   407	    before Phase 2 close lands.
   408	  - Operator product-direction note:
   409	    `specs/reviews/phase-2-operator-product-check.md` with
   410	    `review_kind: operator-product-direction-check`,
   411	    `scope: product-direction-only`, `not_claimed` covering forbidden
   412	    wording analogous to ADR-0006 14a.
   413	  - Audit check: `checkPhase2CloseMatrix` (new; added at the Phase 2
   414	    close ceremony slice, not in this P2.1 slice). Rejects the close
   415	    claim if:
   416	    - Any row's status is `active — red`;
   417	    - Any row's status is `re-deferred` without a corresponding ADR
   418	      citation path that exists on disk and is in `Accepted` status;
   419	    - Any row's `structural_evidence_type` is `cross-model
   420	      challenger` or `test-enforced via LLM stand-in` only, with no
   421	      corresponding operator or non-LLM mechanical evidence elsewhere
   422	      in the matrix (CC#15-style structural-separation rule);
   423	    - The Codex challenger pass closing verdict is not
   424	      `ACCEPT-WITH-FOLD-INS` or `ACCEPT`;
   425	    - The operator product-direction note's `not_claimed` is missing
   426	      or its forbidden-wording list is empty.
   427	- **Non-substitutable failure conditions:** artifact presence alone
   428	  does not satisfy CC#P2-8. Each row of the matrix must show
   429	  executable evidence (commit SHA + test/audit result); LLM-only
   430	  stand-in evidence is insufficient; aggregate-style wording in the
   431	  matrix or review artifacts fails the audit.
   432	- **Circularity check:** CC#P2-8 can only pass *after* CC#P2-1
   433	  through CC#P2-7 are each in a final state (`active — satisfied` or
   434	  `re-deferred` with valid ADR). CC#P2-8 cannot declare itself
   435	  satisfied.
   436

   473	Order-of-authority for close-criteria semantics after this ADR:

   483	### 3. No-aggregate-scoring rule (explicit, inline, tightened)
   484	
   485	**No aggregate score is computed across the eight Phase 2 close
   486	criteria.** Phase 2 closes when:
   487	
   488	- Every `active` criterion is in status `active — satisfied`, **AND**
   489	- Every `re-deferred` criterion has valid ADR-covered trigger
   490	  conditions and has not had a trigger fire.
   491	
   492	A situation where five criteria are `active — satisfied` and three
   493	are `active — red` does not close Phase 2. Nor does a situation where
   494	six criteria are satisfied, one is `active — red`, and one is
   495	`re-deferred`. There is no weighted rollup, no "score of 6/8," no
   496	"substantially complete" wording. This is the direct application of
   497	CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #8: *"No aggregate scoring across
   498	ratchets; each dimension tracked independently."*
   499	
   500	**Forbidden status/progress wording (rejected on §6 grounds):**
   501	
   502	- "N-of-8 complete", "N of 8 green", "8/8", "7/8"
   503	- "substantially complete", "mostly done", "nearly done", "close to
   504	  done", "near close"
   505	- "all but one", "all except X"
   506	- "only N remaining"
   507	- "complete except for X"
   508	- "green-by-redeferral", "trivially green" (for any criterion)
   509	- "aggregate green", "composite status"
   510	- Any scalar summary of Phase 2 close progress
   511	
   512	Why aggregate scoring is excluded: aggregate scores hide regressions.
   513	A Phase-2-close number of "7/8" on the day before closure — where
   514	CC#P2-7 is re-deferred and CC#P2-6 spine coverage is red on the target
   515	workflow — would mask the single load-bearing failure. Independent
   516	tracking with explicit `active — satisfied` / `active — red` /
   517	`re-deferred` status values forces the failure to be visible as a
   518	named non-green gate.
   519	
   520	Ratchet-advance semantics per criterion: the product ratchets named

   523	is a substitute for a close criterion; none of the close criteria is
   524	a substitute for its ratchet. The two surfaces are complementary, not

   527	### 4a. Scope of this ADR (explicit)
   528	
   529	This ADR **does**:
   530	
   531	- Lock the eight close criteria with concrete executable enforcement
   532	  bindings (not slice-name-only bindings).
   533	- Resolve plan Open Question #3 (container isolation) by re-deferral
   534	  with nine named trigger conditions and an interim per-slice citation
   535	  audit check.
   536	- Resolve plan Open Question #5 (spine policy for `explore`) by
   537	  adopting the full-spine set {Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review,
   538	  Close}.
   539	- State the no-aggregate-scoring rule inline, with an explicit
   540	  forbidden-wording list.
   541	- Install a §6 Precedent firewall preventing ADR-0007 from being
   542	  cited as precedent, pattern, or template.
   543	- Amend the plan's §Phase 2 close criteria status and locked-summary
   544	  table in place.
   545	- Add one interim audit check (`checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation`)
   546	  advancing the audit-coverage ratchet independently of the close-
   547	  criteria-authority ratchet.
   548	
   549	This ADR **does not**:
   550	
   551	- Re-sequence slices P2.1–P2.11 or P2-MODEL-EFFORT. Ordering stays
   552	  with the plan.
   553	- Retarget the first-parity workflow (that decision was recorded at
   554	  plan commit `3bf4868`, operator 2026-04-21). Future retargets
   555	  require amending this ADR via the retarget checklist at §4b.
   556	- Waive, relax, or weaken any Phase 1.5 close criterion.
   557	- Claim Phase 2 is closer to done. See §Consequences.Accepted.
   558	- Weaken CLAUDE.md hard invariants #1–#4 at the policy layer. The
   559	  close-criterion-layer re-deferral at CC#P2-7 is explicitly
   560	  acknowledged as a weakening of Phase 2 close only, not of the
   561	  policy-level statements.
   562	- Introduce aggregate scoring. See §Decision.3.
   563	- Re-scope `circuit:create`, `circuit:handoff` (workflow parity),
   564	  registry-lookup install wrapper, intelligent routing selector,
   565	  third-voice challenger, or any item under plan §Deferred to Phase
   566	  2+. Those remain deferred.
   567	- Add Phase 2 close gates for P2.8 router, P2.10 artifact schemas,
   568	  or continuity-lifecycle-beyond-CC#P2-4. See §Decision.4c for
   569	  explicit non-gating disposition.

   571	### 4b. Retarget checklist (CC#P2-1 target-workflow reselection)
   572	
   573	If the operator exercises the plan's documented `review` fallback for
   574	the first-parity target (per `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md
   575	§Target workflow for first parity — DECIDED.Fallback`), the retarget
   576	requires this ADR to be amended. The amendment payload must include:
   577	
   578	1. **New target workflow name.** The workflow id as it appears in
   579	   `specs/contracts/` and `.claude-plugin/skills/`.
   580	2. **New canonical phase set** (CC#P2-6 binding). For `review`, the
   581	   canonical set is not yet enumerated in this repo; the retarget
   582	   amendment must author `specs/contracts/review.md` first.
   583	3. **New fixture path.** `.claude-plugin/skills/<target>/circuit.json`.
   584	4. **New golden path.** `tests/fixtures/golden/<target>/`.
   585	5. **New test file names.** `tests/runner/<target>-e2e-parity.test.ts`,
   586	   and any workflow-specific contract tests.
   587	6. **Artifact-shape contract update.** If the new target produces
   588	   different artifact shapes (review: verdict/objections; explore:
   589	   brief/analysis/synthesis), CC#P2-1's byte-shape golden definition
   590	   must be updated inline.
   591	7. **Affected planned slices.** Which of P2.3, P2.5, P2.9, P2.10,
   592	   P2.11 require re-scoping or re-ordering.
   593	8. **Disposition of any landed `explore` artifacts.** Are they kept
   594	   as partial/discarded/second-workflow-candidate? The retarget
   595	   amendment must state the disposition inline.
   596	9. **Evidence the retarget is structurally required, not merely
   597	   scope-reducing.** Per §6 Precedent firewall clause 2.
   598	10. **Codex challenger pass** on the retarget amendment per §6 clause
   599	    6, with HIGH + MED fold-ins before landing.
   600	11. **Operator product-direction acknowledgment** recorded in the
   601	    amendment commit body, explicit about `not_claimed` (e.g. "this
   602	    retarget is not a claim that `explore` was the wrong choice; it
   603	    is a scope-pivot under documented fallback").
   604	
   605	A target-name swap without this payload is rejected on §6 grounds.
   606	
   607	### 4c. Non-gating disposition for mid-term plan items
   608	
   609	The following items appear in `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md`
   610	as Phase 2 work but are **not** Phase 2 close gates. They are
   611	explicitly demoted to non-gating status by this ADR; their absence
   612	at Phase 2 close does not block closure unless a specific CC#P2-N
   613	binding above requires them:
   614	
   615	- **P2.8 router (`/circuit:run` classifier)** — non-gating for Phase 2
   616	  close. A single-workflow parity claim (CC#P2-1) does not require a
   617	  multi-workflow classifier. If a second workflow lands before Phase 2
   618	  close (P2.9), the router may become load-bearing — at that point,
   619	  a future ADR may add a CC#P2-9 gate. This ADR does not pre-install
   620	  that gate.
   621	- **P2.10 artifact schema set (brief/analysis/synthesis/result/
   622	  verdict)** — partially load-bearing via CC#P2-1 byte-shape golden
   623	  (the golden artifact *is* the schema definition operationally for
   624	  Phase 2 close). Full Zod schemas for each artifact are non-gating;
   625	  their absence does not block close. If the P2.5 golden cannot be
   626	  produced without a Zod schema, CC#P2-1 fails first, not CC#P2-10.
   627	- **Continuity lifecycle beyond CC#P2-4 scope** — the lifecycle test
   628	  at CC#P2-4 covers create/persist/resume/clear. Broader continuity
   629	  lifecycle work (multiple concurrent records, branch-aware resume,
   630	  cross-worktree hygiene) is non-gating.
   631	
   632	**Inherited product ratchets.** The seven ratchets inherited from
   633	Phase 1.5 close (`runner_smoke_present`, `workflow_fixture_runs`,
   634	`event_log_round_trip`, `snapshot_derived_from_log`,
   635	`manifest_snapshot_byte_match`, `status_docs_current`,
   636	`tier_claims_current`) and the three added by Phase 2
   637	(`dispatch_realness`, `workflow_parity_fixtures`,
   638	`plugin_surface_present`) must be green at Phase 2 close. This is
   639	separate from close criteria and is enforced by existing audit checks
   640	on those ratchets (or by the audit checks added by their implementing
   641	slices). A red inherited ratchet at close blocks Phase 2 close
   642	independently of CC#P2-1..8 status; CC#P2-8 close-matrix audit check
   643	includes a row for each inherited ratchet and fails if any is red.
   644	
   645	### 5. Reopen basis — explicit one-time exception (re-authenticated independently)
   646	
   647	This ADR does **not** cite ADR-0006 as precedent, pattern, template,
   648	or analogy for its re-deferral of CC#P2-7 or for any other provision.
   649	Per ADR-0006 §5 clause 7, that citation is forbidden. Instead, this
   650	ADR's re-deferral and governance moves rest on their own four-part
   651	basis, re-authenticated fresh from first principles:
   652	
   653	- **(a) Named failure mode the existing close-criterion list cannot
   654	  absorb.** The draft list in the plan was marked "draft — candidate,
   655	  not locked" precisely because it could not govern real slices
   656	  without ADR authority. Without locking, any near-term slice could
   657	  either invent a partial close definition or drift — the
   658	  methodology-instead-of-product failure D1 was installed to prevent.
   659	  Locking the list is itself the named failure-mode remediation.
   660	- **(b) D1 impact analysis.** Locking the close criteria does not
   661	  substitute methodology work for executable product evidence; it
   662	  *defines* what executable product evidence will satisfy Phase 2
   663	  close. CC#P2-1 (end-to-end workflow parity), CC#P2-2 (real agent
   664	  dispatch), CC#P2-6 (spine policy coverage) all require *executable*
   665	  evidence produced by later slices. This ADR does not manufacture
   666	  its own satisfaction; it only names what satisfaction looks like.
   667	- **(c) Cross-model challenger pass.** Codex pass on this ADR lands
   668	  at `specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md`. This is a governance ratchet
   669	  change per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #6, and the challenger pass is
   670	  mandatory per the §Cross-model challenger protocol. Opening verdict
   671	  on the as-authored ADR: REJECT PENDING FOLD-INS (7 HIGH / 5 MED /
   672	  1 LOW / 1 META). All HIGH + MED + LOW + META folded in via this
   673	  revision (same authoring session).
   674	- **(d) Written explanation why re-deferral of CC#P2-7 is honest.**
   675	  See §Decision.1 CC#P2-7: current Phase 2 slices do not require
   676	  container isolation to land honestly; the LLM-drafting-in-operator-
   677	  session case is operationally distinct from the agent-tool-process-
   678	  with-filesystem-authority case; the nine named trigger conditions
   679	  re-open the gate the moment the distinction breaks.
   680	
   681	**This four-part basis is not a standing reopen condition.** Future
   682	methodology amendments claiming this basis must re-authenticate each
   683	of (a)–(d) freshly and cannot cite ADR-0007 as precedent (see §6).
   684	
   685	### 6. Precedent firewall
   686	
   687	Any future ADR, plan amendment, commit body, audit script, review
   688	artifact, or operator-facing summary proposing to retarget, waive,
   689	relax, substitute, re-defer, or aggregate any Phase 2 close criterion
   690	(including but not limited to retargeting the first-parity workflow,
   691	re-opening or re-closing CC#P2-7 container isolation, weakening any
   692	of CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-8, or introducing aggregate-style status
   693	wording) **must** clear all of the following to be accepted:
   694	
   695	1. **Identify the original criterion being replaced or amended** with
   696	   its exact CC#P2-N identifier and the specific enforcement binding
   697	   under this ADR's §Decision.1.
   698	2. **Prove the amendment is necessary** — not merely convenient, not
   699	   merely scope-reducing, but structurally required given evidence
   700	   that emerged after this ADR's date (2026-04-21). Scope-reducing
   701	   pivots (e.g. `review` fallback for CC#P2-1) are acceptable only
   702	   under the retarget checklist at §4b, not under general amendment.
   703	3. **Name compensating evidence of a different structural type** if
   704	   weakening — not LLM-on-LLM delegation alone. If the only
   705	   compensation is cross-model or LLM stand-in evidence, the
   706	   amendment must be rejected on CC#15-style structural-separation
   707	   grounds (see ADR-0001 Addendum B CC#15 for the underlying
   708	   principle; this ADR re-authenticates the principle independently
   709	   and does not cite ADR-0006's application of it as precedent).
   710	4. **Carry weaker-evidence wording openly** on every authority
   711	   surface the amendment touches (this ADR's body if amended, any
   712	   referenced plan section, PROJECT_STATE, README). "This is a
   713	   weaker substitute" must be recorded in-line, not as an appendix
   714	   pointer.
   715	5. **Add expiry or reopen trigger** — a concrete condition under
   716	   which the amendment is revisited. For CC#P2-7 specifically, the
   717	   nine trigger conditions at §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 are in force; any
   718	   future amendment to CC#P2-7 must either retire one of those
   719	   triggers (with justification and re-authenticated (a)–(d)) or add
   720	   new ones, never silently remove all.
   721	6. **Get a cross-model challenger pass with an objection list** per
   722	   CLAUDE.md §Cross-model challenger protocol; HIGH + MED objections
   723	   must be folded in before landing. The yield-ledger row for the
   724	   challenger pass must be class = `governance`.
   725	7. **Explicit non-precedent clause** stating that ADR-0007 is not
   726	   being cited as precedent, pattern, template, or analogy, and
   727	   re-authenticating (1)–(6) on fresh grounds. An amendment claiming
   728	   ADR-0007 as precedent — whether by name, by pattern-matching
   729	   ("similar to ADR-0007's CC#P2-7 re-deferral"), or by implicit
   730	   template-reuse ("applying the ADR-0007 approach") — is rejected
   731	   on §Precedent firewall grounds alone.
   732	
   733	**Inline restatement of CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #8 (aggregate-
   734	scoring firewall clause specific to this ADR).** Any future ADR,
   735	audit check, ratchet dashboard, operator-facing summary, review file,
   736	commit body, or README that computes or displays any of the
   737	following is rejected on sight:
   738	
   739	- A scalar or composite Phase 2 close status ("6/8", "75% complete",
   740	  "composite green")
   741	- Any of the forbidden wordings enumerated at §Decision.3
   742	- A status graph/chart/table that visually aggregates Phase 2 close
   743	  criteria into a single indicator
   744	- A "progress percentage" field in any Phase 2 governance artifact
   745	- A "close criteria completion" metric in any ratchet dashboard
   746	
   747	This restatement is operative in this document. The firewall does not
   748	merely cite CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #8; it restates the rule as a
   749	rejection-on-sight condition for this specific ADR and its domain.
   750

   754	presence), this ADR adds **one** new audit check:
   755	`checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` (see CC#P2-7 interim enforcement).
   756	No per-criterion satisfaction checks are added by this ADR (those are
   757	added by the slices that land each criterion — CC#P2-3 check at P2.2,
   758	CC#P2-6 check at P2.3, etc.).
   759	
   760	**Audit numbering rule (to prevent stale Check-N citations):**

 succeeded in 0ms:
   760	**Audit numbering rule (to prevent stale Check-N citations):**
   761	
   762	- The current audit script ends at Check 22 (verify gate).
   763	  `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` takes the next available slot
   764	  after Check 22 (projected: Check 23, but not fixed by this ADR).
   765	- Future slices adding audit checks MUST use the next available slot
   766	  at the time of their commit, not any number pre-allocated by this
   767	  or prior ADRs. The printed Check-N order in audit output MUST be
   768	  monotonic.
   769	- Any audit-check number cited in this ADR (Check 23, Check 24, etc.)
   770	  is projected, not locked. The implementing slice assigns the final
   771	  number.
   772	
   773	## Rationale
   774	
   775	Three separate lines of reasoning support locking the close criteria
   776	now rather than later. ("Separate," not "independent" — these lines
   777	are authored by the same Claude-Codex pair and share training
   778	distribution per Knight & Leveson 1986; they are not epistemically
   779	independent of each other.)
   780	
   781	1. **Anchor before pressure.** The plan's draft list cannot govern
   782	   near-term slices without ADR authority. The first code slice that
   783	   cites a close criterion (projected: P2.2 or P2.3) would either
   784	   gain an authority vacuum or invent its own partial definition.
   785	   Locking the list in governance before the first code-carrying
   786	   slice removes that ambiguity. This is the Contract-First pillar
   787	   applied at the phase-close layer.
   788	
   789	2. **Re-deferral with explicit weakening beats either silent
   790	   deferral or forced compliance.** Plan Open Question #3 had two
   791	   acceptable resolutions: lock CC#P2-7 as a Phase 2 gate, or
   792	   re-defer it by ADR with named triggers. Leaving it as a draft
   793	   open question would produce ambiguous Phase 2 close later. This
   794	   ADR picks re-deferral because no Phase 2 slice honestly requires
   795	   container isolation to land. Re-deferral is weaker than the
   796	   CLAUDE.md policy-level statement, and ADR-0007 acknowledges the
   797	   weakening openly (§Decision.1 CC#P2-7, §Consequences.Accepted).
   798	   The nine named trigger conditions + interim per-slice citation
   799	   audit check ensure re-deferral has enforceable exit conditions.
   800	
   801	3. **Aggregate-score firewalling must be inline, not cited.**
   802	   CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #8 is authoritative, but past
   803	   operator-facing summaries and review drafts have reached for
   804	   aggregate-sounding wording under time pressure. Restating the
   805	   no-aggregate rule inline in the §Precedent firewall — not merely
   806	   citing it — gives future readers a single authoritative surface
   807	   to point to when rejecting aggregate-style summaries of Phase 2
   808	   progress.
   809	
   810	## What changes
   811	
   812	### 1. `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §Phase 2 close criteria`
   813	
   814	Status line amended from:
   815	
   816	> These are the candidate gates for calling Phase 2 done. They are
   817	> authored here for ranging purposes and must be locked via an ADR
   818	> before any slice claims one of them.
   819	
   820	To:
   821	
   822	> **Status: LOCKED via ADR-0007 (2026-04-21).** These are the
   823	> authoritative Phase 2 close criteria with concrete executable
   824	> enforcement bindings. The locked summary table (see ADR-0007
   825	> §Decision.2) is mirrored in full below; on any conflict, ADR-0007
   826	> §Decision.1 is authoritative. Any change — retarget, weakening,
   827	> relaxation, introduction of aggregate scoring — must clear
   828	> ADR-0007 §6 Precedent firewall before landing.
   829	
   830	Followed by the full locked summary table from §Decision.2 above,
   831	inlined in the plan — not a pointer.
   832	
   833	Plan Open Questions updated:
   834	
   835	- **#3 (container isolation)**: `RESOLVED 2026-04-21 via ADR-0007
   836	  CC#P2-7: re-deferred with nine named trigger conditions; interim
   837	  per-slice citation audit check added in ceremony slice.`
   838	- **#5 (spine policy for `explore`)**: `RESOLVED 2026-04-21 via
   839	  ADR-0007 CC#P2-6: full-spine Standard rigor — Frame, Analyze,
   840	  Synthesize, Review, Close.`
   841	
   842	P2.3 deliverable text and CC#P2-6 canonical phase set are
   843	consistent; no change to P2.3 prose required beyond the Open Question
   844	#5 resolution annotation.
   845	
   846	### 2. New ADR file
   847	
   848	`specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md` (this document). First
   849	ADR authored under Phase 2 — Implementation.
   850	
   851	### 3. Codex challenger pass artifact
   852	
   853	`specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md` — objection list from Codex
   854	cross-model challenger pass. Opening verdict: REJECT PENDING FOLD-INS
   855	(7 HIGH / 5 MED / 1 LOW / 1 META). Disposition: all HIGH + MED + LOW
   856	+ META folded in via this ADR's revision (same authoring session),
   857	per §Decision.5(c).
   858	
   859	### 4. Yield-ledger row
   860	
   861	`specs/reviews/adversarial-yield-ledger.md` appends a row for the
   862	ADR-0007 Codex pass:
   863	
   864	- `pass_date`: 2026-04-21
   865	- `artifact_path`: `specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md`
   866	- `artifact_class`: governance
   867	- `pass_number_for_artifact`: 1
   868	- `reviewer_id`: gpt-5-codex
   869	- `mode`: llm-review
   870	- `HIGH_count`: 7 / `MED_count`: 5 / `LOW_count`: 1 (META not
   871	  columnar but noted in the review artifact)
   872	- `verdict`: REJECT PENDING FOLD-INS → incorporated → ACCEPT-WITH-
   873	  FOLD-INS
   874	- `rigor_profile`: standard
   875	- `why_continue_failure_class`: n/a (pass 1)
   876	- `prior_execution_commit_sha`: n/a (pass 1)
   877	
   878	### 5. Ceremony commit body — required framing (with expanded forbidden wording)
   879	
   880	The ceremony commit landing this ADR **must** carry:
   881	
   882	- **Lane:** Ratchet-Advance (governance ratchet — close-criteria
   883	  authority ratchet; advances independently from Slice 31a's phase-
   884	  graph authority ratchet per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #8; additionally
   885	  advances the audit-coverage ratchet via
   886	  `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation`).
   887	- **Trajectory:** P2.1 → Phase 2 close-criteria arc → Phase 2 entry
   888	  goal. No earlier slice makes this one smaller or mis-sequenced.
   889	- **Failure mode:** draft close criteria in the plan cannot govern
   890	  near-term slices; first code slice to cite them would invent or
   891	  drift.
   892	- **Acceptance evidence:** this ADR file; Codex challenger pass at
   893	  `specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md`; yield-ledger row; plan §Phase 2
   894	  close criteria amended in place with full locked summary table;
   895	  Open Questions #3 and #5 marked RESOLVED; new audit check
   896	  `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` added;
   897	  `npm run verify` + `npm run audit` green.
   898	- **Alternate framing:** author ADR-0007 after P2.2–P2.5 land, once
   899	  real surface pressure emerges. Rejected because lane discipline is
   900	  weaker without an authoritative anchor.

    37	// These tests exercise checkPluginCommandClosure (audit.mjs Check 23,

    53	
    54	function writeRel(root: string, rel: string, body: string) {

    33	
    34	// Minimal valid explore fixture body — the canonical phase set
    35	// {frame, analyze, act, review, close} + omits {plan, verify}.
    36	function validExploreFixture(overrides: Record<string, unknown> = {}) {
    37	  return {
    38	    schema_version: '2',
    39	    id: 'explore',
    40	    version: '0.1.0',
    41	    purpose: 'test fixture',
    42	    entry: { signals: { include: [], exclude: [] }, intent_prefixes: [] },
    43	    entry_modes: [
    44	      {
    45	        name: 'default',
    46	        start_at: 'frame-step',
    47	        rigor: 'standard',
    48	        description: 'test entry mode',
    49	      },
    50	    ],
    51	    phases: [

    60	      omits: ['plan', 'verify'],
    61	      rationale:
    62	        'Test: explore is an investigation workflow — plan folded into frame, verify covered by review.',

     8	  "notes": "Pinned ratchet floors that must not regress. The contract_test_count metric is the static declaration count produced by scripts/audit.mjs::countTests (matches /^\\s*(it|test)\\(/gm across git-tracked tests/**/*.test.* files), not the vitest runtime total; the audit uses the static count so the ratchet is deterministic without shelling into vitest. Close gates depend on this file, not on HEAD~1 alone. See specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md §Slice 26b and ADR-0002 Addendum A. Slice 33 (P2.2) adds tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts (+26 static test declarations for plugin-command-closure enforcement per ADR-0007 CC#P2-3); floor advanced from 574 → 727 in the same Ratchet-Advance slice, closing the moving-window hole per Codex P2.2 MED 7 fold-in. Metadata fields (schema_version, last_advanced_at, last_advanced_in_slice) are audit-enforced via validatePinnedRatchetFloorData; floor zero or negative is rejected (Codex HIGH-2 fold-in). Future slices raise the floor only as an explicit ratchet-advance act."

     1	import { execSync } from 'node:child_process';
     2	import { mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     3	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
     4	import { join } from 'node:path';
     5	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     6	
     7	// Slice 26b — Status epoch alignment + pinned ratchet floor.
     8	//
     9	// Closes three TIER.md `planned → 26b` rows: status_docs_current,
    10	// pinned_ratchet_floor, current_slice_status_epoch. The agreement-only
    11	// checkPhaseDrift cannot detect the case where README / PROJECT_STATE /
    12	// TIER consistently tell the same stale story. Slice 26b adds:
    13	//   (1) a structured `<!-- current_slice: <id> -->` marker on all three
    14	//       docs, checked by checkStatusEpochAlignment;
    15	//   (2) a freshness check that compares the aligned marker against the
    16	//       most recent `^slice-<id>:` git log subject — checkStatusDocsCurrent;
    17	//   (3) a pinned ratchet floor in specs/ratchet-floor.json so close gates
    18	//       no longer depend purely on the HEAD~1 moving window —
    19	//       checkPinnedRatchetFloor.
    20	//
    21	// Each check is exercised against (a) the live repo, (b) constructed
    22	// temp-dir fixtures that simulate green and red states without touching
    23	// the project's git history. For checkStatusDocsCurrent we spin up a real
    24	// ephemeral git repo per-test so the helper's `git log` calls see actual
    25	// commit subjects, closing the "both stale is red is not pinned by tests"
    26	// gap that Codex MED-1 flagged on the draft. Fold-in set: HIGH-1 HIGH-2
    27	// MED-1..MED-5 LOW-1 LOW-2 per specs/reviews/arc-slice-26b-codex.md.
    28	
    29	import {
    30	  CURRENT_SLICE_MARKER_PATTERN,
    31	  SLICE_COMMIT_SUBJECT_PATTERN,
    32	  SLICE_ID_PATTERN,
    33	  checkPinnedRatchetFloor,
    34	  checkStatusDocsCurrent,
    35	  checkStatusEpochAlignment,
    36	  extractCurrentSliceMarker,
    37	  extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject,
    38	  isValidSliceId,
    39	  readPinnedRatchetFloor,
    40	  validatePinnedRatchetFloorData,
    41	} from '../../scripts/audit.mjs';
    42	
    43	function makeTempRoot(): string {
    44	  return mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'circuit-next-slice-26b-'));
    45	}
    46	
    47	function writeThreeDocs(
    48	  root: string,
    49	  markers: { readme: string | null; projectState: string | null; tier: string | null },
    50	) {
    51	  const readme =
    52	    markers.readme === null ? '# repo\n' : `<!-- current_slice: ${markers.readme} -->\n\n# repo\n`;
    53	  const projectState =
    54	    markers.projectState === null
    55	      ? '# PROJECT_STATE\n'
    56	      : `<!-- current_slice: ${markers.projectState} -->\n\n# PROJECT_STATE\n`;
    57	  const tier =
    58	    markers.tier === null
    59	      ? '---\nname: tier\n---\n\n# TIER\n'
    60	      : `---\nname: tier\n---\n\n<!-- current_slice: ${markers.tier} -->\n\n# TIER\n`;
    61	  writeFileSync(join(root, 'README.md'), readme);
    62	  writeFileSync(join(root, 'PROJECT_STATE.md'), projectState);
    63	  writeFileSync(join(root, 'TIER.md'), tier);
    64	}
    65	
    66	function writeFloor(
    67	  root: string,
    68	  floor: number | 'missing' | 'invalid',
    69	  metadataOverrides: Partial<{
    70	    schema_version: unknown;
    71	    last_advanced_at: unknown;
    72	    last_advanced_in_slice: unknown;
    73	  }> = {},
    74	) {
    75	  const specsDir = join(root, 'specs');
    76	  mkdirSync(specsDir, { recursive: true });
    77	  const floorPath = join(specsDir, 'ratchet-floor.json');
    78	  if (floor === 'missing') return;
    79	  if (floor === 'invalid') {
    80	    writeFileSync(floorPath, '{"not":"valid","floors":"nope"}');
    81	    return;
    82	  }
    83	  const payload = {
    84	    schema_version: 1,
    85	    floors: { contract_test_count: floor },
    86	    last_advanced_at: '2026-04-20',
    87	    last_advanced_in_slice: '26b',
    88	    ...metadataOverrides,
    89	  };
    90	  writeFileSync(floorPath, JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
    91	}
    92	
    93	function initGitRepo(root: string) {
    94	  execSync('git init -q -b main', { cwd: root });
    95	  execSync('git config user.email test@example.com', { cwd: root });
    96	  execSync('git config user.name test', { cwd: root });
    97	}
    98	
    99	function commitWithSubject(root: string, subject: string, fileSuffix = '') {
   100	  // Write a small marker file so the commit has a diff.
   101	  writeFileSync(join(root, `commit${fileSuffix || Date.now()}.txt`), `${subject}\n`);
   102	  execSync('git add -A', { cwd: root });
   103	  execSync(`git commit -q --allow-empty -m ${JSON.stringify(subject)}`, {
   104	    cwd: root,
   105	    env: { ...process.env, GIT_COMMITTER_DATE: '2026-04-20T00:00:00Z' },
   106	  });
   107	}
   108	
   109	describe('SLICE_ID_PATTERN + isValidSliceId (Slice 26b, Codex MED-5 fold-in)', () => {
   110	  it('accepts canonical slice ids', () => {
   111	    for (const ok of ['25', '25a', '25b', '26', '26a', '26b', '27c', '100z']) {

   114	  });
   115	
   116	  it('rejects draft / provisional / WIP subjects', () => {

   133	describe('extractCurrentSliceMarker (Slice 26b pure helper)', () => {

   136	  });

   138	  it('is tolerant of interior whitespace', () => {

   146	  it('rejects slice-ids that do not match SLICE_ID_PATTERN (Codex MED-5)', () => {

   148	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker('<!-- current_slice: 26b-wip -->\n# body')).toBe(null);

   150	  });
   151	
   152	  it('returns null for missing markers', () => {
   153	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker('# no marker here')).toBe(null);
   154	  });
   155	
   156	  it('returns null for malformed inputs', () => {
   157	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker('<!-- current_slice -->\n# body')).toBe(null);
   158	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker(42 as unknown as string)).toBe(null);
   159	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker(null as unknown as string)).toBe(null);
   160	  });
   161	
   162	  it('rejects markers that appear AFTER the first markdown heading (Codex MED-3 zone anchor)', () => {
   163	    const body = '# heading first\n\n<!-- current_slice: 26b -->\nbody';

   165	  });
   166	
   167	  it('rejects DUPLICATED markers in the header zone (Codex MED-3)', () => {
   168	    const body = '<!-- current_slice: 26a -->\n<!-- current_slice: 26b -->\n# body';
   169	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker(body)).toBe(null);
   170	  });
   171	
   172	  it('ignores markers embedded in quoted code/diff below the first heading', () => {
   173	    // Only the first-heading-zone is scanned; historical quoted content is ignored.
   174	    const body =
   175	      '<!-- current_slice: 26b -->\n\n# heading\n\n```\n<!-- current_slice: 99z -->\n```\n';
   176	    expect(extractCurrentSliceMarker(body)).toBe('26b');
   177	  });
   178	
   179	  it('exposes the underlying regex pattern', () => {
   180	    expect(CURRENT_SLICE_MARKER_PATTERN).toBeInstanceOf(RegExp);
   181	    expect(CURRENT_SLICE_MARKER_PATTERN.flags).toContain('i');
   182	  });

   184	
   185	describe('extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject (Slice 26b pure helper)', () => {
   186	  it('extracts the slice id from a conventional slice commit subject', () => {
   187	    expect(
   188	      extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject('slice-26a: Run snapshot artifact split (HIGH #1)'),
   189	    ).toBe('26a');
   190	  });
   191	
   192	  it('rejects draft / WIP / hyphenated-suffix subjects (Codex MED-5)', () => {
   193	    expect(extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject('slice-26b-wip: draft')).toBe(null);
   194	    expect(extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject('slice-27c-b: hyphenated')).toBe(null);

   197	
   198	  it('returns null for non-slice subjects (docs, fix, chore)', () => {
   199	    expect(extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject('docs: PROJECT_STATE update')).toBe(null);
   200	    expect(extractSliceIdFromCommitSubject('fix: typo in ADR-0003')).toBe(null);

   203	
   204	  it('returns null for malformed inputs', () => {

   209	
   210	  it('exposes the underlying regex pattern', () => {

   214	
   215	describe('checkStatusEpochAlignment (Slice 26b full-file check)', () => {
   216	  it('returns green when all three docs carry matching markers', () => {
   217	    const root = makeTempRoot();
   218	    try {
   219	      writeThreeDocs(root, { readme: '26b', projectState: '26b', tier: '26b' });
   220	      const result = checkStatusEpochAlignment(root);
   221	      expect(result.level).toBe('green');
   222	      expect(result.detail).toContain('26b');
   223	    } finally {
   224	      rmSync(root, { recursive: true, force: true });
   225	    }
   226	  });
   227	
   228	  it('returns red when any doc is missing the marker', () => {
   229	    const root = makeTempRoot();

   231	      writeThreeDocs(root, { readme: '26b', projectState: null, tier: '26b' });
   232	      const result = checkStatusEpochAlignment(root);
   233	      expect(result.level).toBe('red');
   234	      expect(result.detail).toContain('PROJECT_STATE.md');
   235	      expect(result.detail).toContain('missing');
   236	    } finally {
   237	      rmSync(root, { recursive: true, force: true });
   238	    }
   239	  });

  2276	
  2277	function commitIsSliceShaped(commit) {
  2278	  // Merge commits, reverts, and plain housekeeping without a Lane don't warrant slice
  2279	  // discipline checks. A "slice" is any commit that declares a Lane.
  2280	  return checkLane(commit.body) !== null;
  2281	}
  2282	
  2283	// Phase 2 open commit — Slice 31a ceremony per ADR-0001 Addendum B as amended
  2284	// by ADR-0006. Used by checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation to scope the check
  2285	// to Phase-2-only commits.
  2286	const PHASE_2_OPEN_COMMIT = '0223d1162b35458c22c4b8680859f872a83897c0';
  2287	
  2288	// Paths whose modification during a Phase 2 slice triggers the isolation-
  2289	// citation requirement. Ordered roughly from most-invariant (specs, tests) to
  2290	// least (hooks/CI). A commit touching any file whose path starts with one of
  2291	// these prefixes must declare its isolation posture in the commit body (see
  2292	// ADR-0007 §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 interim enforcement).
  2293	const ISOLATION_PROTECTED_PREFIXES = [
  2294	  'specs/',
  2295	  'tests/',
  2296	  '.github/',
  2297	  '.claude-plugin/',
  2298	  '.claude/hooks/',
  2299	  'src/',
  2300	  'scripts/',
  2301	];
  2302	
  2303	function commitIsAtOrAfterPhase2Open(hash) {
  2304	  try {
  2305	    execSync(`git merge-base --is-ancestor ${PHASE_2_OPEN_COMMIT} ${hash}`, {
  2306	      stdio: 'ignore',
  2307	    });
  2308	    return true;
  2309	  } catch {
  2310	    return false;
  2311	  }
  2312	}
  2313	
  2314	function commitChangedFiles(hash) {
  2315	  const raw = shSafe(`git show --name-only --pretty=format: ${hash}`);
  2316	  return raw
  2317	    .split('\n')
  2318	    .map((l) => l.trim())
  2319	    .filter(Boolean);
  2320	}
  2321	
  2322	// Returns true if ADR-0007 exists in the commit's tree. Used to grandfather
  2323	// pre-ADR-0007 Phase 2 commits out of the isolation-citation check (those
  2324	// commits could not have known to carry the posture string since the ADR
  2325	// requiring it did not yet exist at their authoring time).
  2326	function adr0007InCommitTree(hash) {
  2327	  try {
  2328	    execSync(`git cat-file -e ${hash}:specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md`, {
  2329	      stdio: 'ignore',
  2330	    });
  2331	    return true;
  2332	  } catch {
  2333	    return false;
  2334	  }
  2335	}
  2336	
  2337	// Check 23 (ADR-0007 CC#P2-7 interim enforcement, P2.1). For every Phase 2
  2338	// slice commit (past the discipline floor AND at or after Phase 2 open) that
  2339	// touches isolation-protected paths, require an explicit isolation posture
  2340	// in the commit body: `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation
  2341	// required)` OR `Isolation: re-deferred per ADR-0007 CC#P2-7` OR a Break-
  2342	// Glass lane declaration. Purpose: force slice authors to declare isolation
  2343	// posture openly rather than silently normalize unisolated Phase 2 execution.
  2344	// This is NOT a filesystem-capability check; it is an in-commit-body citation
  2345	// audit. Advances the audit-coverage ratchet independently from the close-
  2346	// criteria-authority ratchet advanced by the P2.1 ceremony.
  2347	export function checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation(disciplinedCommits) {
  2348	  const POSTURES = [
  2349	    'Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)',
  2350	    'Isolation: re-deferred per ADR-0007 CC#P2-7',
  2351	  ];
  2352	
  2353	  // Two-stage filter: (1) commit is at or after Phase 2 open; (2) commit's
  2354	  // tree already contains ADR-0007 (grandfathering pre-ADR-0007 Phase 2
  2355	  // commits that could not have known the posture-string requirement).
  2356	  const phase2Commits = disciplinedCommits
  2357	    .filter((c) => commitIsAtOrAfterPhase2Open(c.hash))
  2358	    .filter((c) => adr0007InCommitTree(c.hash));
  2359	
  2360	  if (phase2Commits.length === 0) {
  2361	    return {
  2362	      level: 'green',
  2363	      detail: 'No post-ADR-0007 Phase 2 slice commits in scanned window; check not applicable',
  2364	    };
  2365	  }
  2366	
  2367	  const offenders = [];
  2368	  for (const c of phase2Commits) {
  2369	    const files = commitChangedFiles(c.hash);
  2370	    const touchesProtected = files.some((f) =>
  2371	      ISOLATION_PROTECTED_PREFIXES.some((p) => f.startsWith(p)),
  2372	    );
  2373	    if (!touchesProtected) continue;
  2374	
  2375	    const lane = checkLane(c.body);
  2376	    const hasPosture = POSTURES.some((p) => c.body.includes(p));
  2377	    const isBreakGlass = lane === 'Break-Glass';
  2378	
  2379	    if (!hasPosture && !isBreakGlass) {
  2380	      offenders.push(c);
  2381	    }
  2382	  }
  2383	
  2384	  if (offenders.length > 0) {
  2385	    const lines = offenders.map(
  2386	      (c) => `${c.short} "${c.subject}" — missing isolation posture in commit body`,
  2387	    );
  2388	    return {
  2389	      level: 'red',
  2390	      detail: `Phase 2 slice(s) touch isolation-protected paths without declaring isolation posture (ADR-0007 §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 interim enforcement):\n      ${lines.join('\n      ')}`,
  2391	    };
  2392	  }
  2393	
  2394	  return {
  2395	    level: 'green',
  2396	    detail: `${phase2Commits.length} Phase 2 slice commit(s) scanned; all touching isolation-protected paths declare posture or Break-Glass`,
  2397	  };
  2398	}
  2399	
  2400	// Check 23 (ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 enforcement, P2.2). Verifies closure between the
  2401	// plugin manifest's `commands` array and the markdown files under
  2402	// `.claude-plugin/commands/`. Enforcement (tightened via P2.2 Codex fold-ins):
  2403	//   (a) `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` parses as JSON with a top-level `commands`
  2404	//       array of `{ name, file, description }` entries (all non-empty; names
  2405	//       and files unique).
  2406	//   (b) Every `commands[].file` matches the grammar `commands/<basename>.md`
  2407	//       with no nested directory components and no non-.md extensions

  3205	  counters[cc14Retarget.level]++;
  3206	  findings.push({
  3207	    level: cc14Retarget.level,
  3208	    check: 'CC#14 retarget presence (ADR-0006)',
  3209	    detail: cc14Retarget.detail,
  3210	  });
  3211	
  3212	  // Check 22: Phase 2 slice isolation citation (ADR-0007 CC#P2-7 interim
  3213	  // enforcement, P2.1). For every Phase 2 slice commit touching isolation-
  3214	  // protected paths (specs/, tests/, .github/, .claude-plugin/, .claude/hooks/,
  3215	  // src/, scripts/), require explicit isolation posture in the commit body
  3216	  // OR a Break-Glass lane. Advances the audit-coverage ratchet independently
  3217	  // from the close-criteria-authority ratchet advanced by the P2.1 ceremony.
  3218	  const phase2Isolation = checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation(disciplinedCommits);
  3219	  counters[phase2Isolation.level]++;
  3220	  findings.push({
  3221	    level: phase2Isolation.level,
  3222	    check: 'Phase 2 slice isolation citation (ADR-0007 CC#P2-7)',
  3223	    detail: phase2Isolation.detail,
  3224	  });
  3225	
  3226	  // Check 23: Plugin command closure (ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 enforcement, P2.2).
  3227	  // Verifies that `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` carries a `commands` array,
  3228	  // every entry has a corresponding `.claude-plugin/commands/*.md` file with
  3229	  // non-empty frontmatter and body, no orphan command files exist on disk,
  3230	  // and the required anchor commands (`circuit:run`, `circuit:explore`) are
  3231	  // present. Advances the `plugin_surface_present` product ratchet to
  3232	  // partial at P2.2 and to green at P2.11.
  3233	  const pluginClosure = checkPluginCommandClosure();
  3234	  counters[pluginClosure.level]++;
  3235	  findings.push({
  3236	    level: pluginClosure.level,
  3237	    check: 'Plugin command closure (ADR-0007 CC#P2-3)',
  3238	    detail: pluginClosure.detail,
  3239	  });
  3240	
  3241	  // Check 24: Spine coverage (ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 enforcement + EXPLORE-I1,
  3242	  // P2.3). For every workflow fixture under `.claude-plugin/skills/<kind>/
  3243	  // circuit.json` whose `id` matches a registered workflow-kind canonical
  3244	  // set, verify declared phases cover exactly the expected canonical set
  3245	  // and `spine_policy.omits` matches. Unknown kinds pass through
  3246	  // information-only; `dogfood-run-0` is exempt as the Phase 1.5 Alpha
  3247	  // Proof partial fixture. Inserted here shifts the prior verify-gate
  3248	  // check to Check 25.
  3249	  const spineCoverage = checkSpineCoverage();
  3250	  counters[spineCoverage.level]++;
  3251	  findings.push({
  3252	    level: spineCoverage.level,
  3253	    check: 'Spine coverage (ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 / EXPLORE-I1)',
  3254	    detail: spineCoverage.detail,
  3255	  });
  3256	
  3257	  // Check 25: npm run verify currently green. (Runs last so the report's
  3258	  // bottom line is the verify-gate status; numbering bumped 24 → 25 by P2.3
  3259	  // which inserted Check 24 for spine coverage. Prior bumps: P2.2 23 → 24;
  3260	  // P2.1 22 → 23; Slice 31a 21 → 22; Slice 26b 15 → 20; Slice 25d 20 → 21.)
  3261	  const verify = verifyStatus();
  3262	  if (verify.pass) {
  3263	    counters.green++;
  3264	    findings.push({
  3265	      level: 'green',
  3266	      check: 'Verify gate',
  3267	      detail: 'npm run verify passes (tsc --strict + biome + vitest)',
  3268	    });
  3269	  } else {
  3270	    counters.red++;
  3271	    findings.push({
  3272	      level: 'red',
  3273	      check: 'Verify gate',
  3274	      detail: `FAIL — tail:\n${verify.detail}`,
  3275	    });

     6	# /circuit:run — classifier (P2.2 scaffold)

    11	P2.2 scaffold entry. Its job in this slice is to hold a canonical path
    12	under `.claude-plugin/commands/` so the plugin manifest can register
    13	the command and the plugin-command-closure audit check can pass.
    14	
    15	Invoking this command in Claude Code will surface the placeholder
    16	behavior defined in this file body. The router logic itself —
    17	classifier heuristics, workflow selection, rigor profile resolution —
    18	is deferred to plan slice **P2.8** (`/circuit:run` classifier in
    19	`specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §Mid-term slices`).

    24	
    25	- §Near-term slices — P2.2 (this scaffold)

    27	  workflow classifier: given task text + entry signals, selects among
    28	  registered workflows)
    29	
    30	Phase 2 close binds `plugin_surface_present` as a product ratchet
    31	that advances to `active — partial` after this slice (P2.2) lands
    32	and to `active — satisfied` after slice P2.11 (plugin-level skill
    33	wiring) lands.
