    46	- Real router behavior (classifier, rigor resolution, workflow
    47	  dispatch, continuation handling) — deferred to P2.8.

codex
So far the canonical `Synthesize → act` translation is present in the contract and fixture, and Check 24 enforces the fixture’s canonical ids/omits. The seam I’m now checking is whether that gives a false “full-spine” or artifact graph claim while route/order/schema properties remain deferred.

specs/artifacts.json:129:      "writers": ["circuit-next engine (projection computation, in-memory only)"],
specs/artifacts.json:130:      "readers": [
specs/artifacts.json:138:      "path_derived_fields": [],
specs/artifacts.json:152:      "description": "Reducer-derived run-local state checkpoint persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/state.json. Always recomputable from run.log by replaying events; never independently authored. Does NOT include the event log itself — for the in-memory {log, snapshot} aggregate see run.projection. Slice 27c lands the reducer-derived writer and byte-match gate.",
specs/artifacts.json:153:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
specs/artifacts.json:154:      "schema_file": "src/schemas/snapshot.ts",
specs/artifacts.json:156:      "writers": ["circuit-next engine (reducer-derived snapshot writer; authored by Slice 27c)"],
specs/artifacts.json:157:      "readers": [
specs/artifacts.json:165:      "path_derived_fields": [],
specs/artifacts.json:167:      "trust_boundary": "operator-local derived state; state.json is written only by the reducer-derived snapshot writer at src/runtime/snapshot-writer.ts (never by step executors). A byte-match against a fresh reducer pass over run.log is the re-entry gate. Slice 27c lands the writer; re-entry byte-match enforcement in resume lands in Slice 27d+ per plan.",
specs/artifacts.json:179:      "description": "Byte-for-byte copy of the workflow manifest taken at run bootstrap, persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/manifest.snapshot.json. Carries the exact persisted manifest bytes (base64-encoded for JSON transport) plus a SHA-256 hash over those raw bytes. The byte-match re-entry check rejects any manifest drift between bootstrap and resume. Slice 27c authors this artifact and its writer.",
specs/artifacts.json:180:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
specs/artifacts.json:181:      "schema_file": "src/schemas/manifest.ts",
specs/artifacts.json:183:      "writers": ["circuit-next engine (manifest snapshot writer; authored by Slice 27c)"],
specs/artifacts.json:184:      "readers": [
specs/artifacts.json:192:      "path_derived_fields": [],
specs/artifacts.json:194:      "trust_boundary": "operator-local persisted state; written once at bootstrap by the manifest snapshot writer at src/runtime/manifest-snapshot-writer.ts. Hash algorithm: SHA-256 over the exact persisted manifest snapshot bytes (`algorithm: 'sha256-raw'`), per ADR-0001 Addendum B §Phase 1.5 Close Criteria #8. Parse-time superRefine rejects any snapshot whose declared hash disagrees with sha256 over decoded bytes_base64; a second reader cannot be tricked into accepting a tampered byte-body under the declared hash.",
specs/artifacts.json:206:      "description": "User-visible summary of what a run produced, persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/artifacts/result.json once the closing run.closed event is appended. Unlike state.json (reducer-derived, recomputable from run.log) a RunResult is written once at close and never mutated; it is the authoritative 'what happened' artifact independent of future log rewrites. Slice 27c declares the shape; Slice 27d wires the writer into dogfood-run-0.",
specs/artifacts.json:207:      "contract": "specs/contracts/run.md",
specs/artifacts.json:208:      "schema_file": "src/schemas/result.ts",
specs/artifacts.json:210:      "writers": ["circuit-next engine (result writer; wired by Slice 27d dogfood-run-0)"],
specs/artifacts.json:211:      "readers": [
specs/artifacts.json:218:      "path_derived_fields": [],

specs/invariants.json:3:  "description": "Contract + behavioral-track invariant/property ledger. Closes AR-M2 from specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md. Scope: specs/contracts/*.md + specs/behavioral/*.md. Out of scope: CLAUDE.md hard invariants, specs/evidence.md invariants, ADR-level invariants (documented exclusion per Slice 25 Codex challenger MED #9 fold-in; deferred to v0.2).",
specs/invariants.json:5:    "test-enforced": "Invariant id appears as a token in at least one binding_ref test-file path (describe/it title or assertion message). Eligibility: required default for any contract invariant stated as 'MUST reject', 'MUST enforce', or 'is rejected'.",
specs/invariants.json:7:    "static-anchor": "Invariant enforced structurally by schema export or superRefine; binding_refs name the schema file plus at least one test file that parses that schema. Eligibility: structural invariants whose id may not appear as a test title but whose enforcement cannot regress without the schema change firing a named test failure. Grandfathered scope_ids entries in tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts count as binding_refs for step.md and workflow.md.",
specs/invariants.json:8:    "prose-only": "Policy statement not directly testable (e.g. when the challenger should be invoked; what the challenger cannot replace). Eligibility: behavioral-track meta-invariants only. Requires `rationale` field. A contract 'MUST reject' invariant cannot be prose-only.",
specs/invariants.json:12:    "AR-H1 persisted-shape binding for invariants to artifacts is deferred to Slice 26a. Optional `artifact_ids` ledger field may be added in a later slice; absence here is intentional and not a silent omission.",

specs/invariants.json:748:      "rationale": "Policy statement about compiler direction authorship (prose → YAML is hand-authored; YAML → prose is compiler-owned). The catalog compiler has not landed at v0.1; no round-trip surface exists yet. The SENTINEL test in tests/contracts/prose-yaml-parity.test.ts fires when compiler markers appear, at which point PROSE-YAML-I3 is re-classified. Permitted prose-only with target-promotion trigger."

     1	import { existsSync, readFileSync, readdirSync } from 'node:fs';
     2	import { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path';
     3	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
     4	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     5	import {
     6	  SCHEMA_FILE_ALLOWLIST,
     7	  collectSchemaExports,
     8	  contractReciprocatesArtifact,
     9	  planeIsValid,
    10	  readFrontmatter,
    11	  schemaExportPresent,
    12	  trustBoundaryHasOriginToken,

    14	
    15	const HERE = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
    16	const REPO_ROOT = resolve(HERE, '..', '..');
    17	const ARTIFACTS_PATH = join(REPO_ROOT, 'specs', 'artifacts.json');
    18	
    19	const SURFACE_CLASSES = new Set([
    20	  'greenfield',
    21	  'successor-to-live',
    22	  'legacy-compatible',
    23	  'migration-source',
    24	  'external-protocol',
    25	  'unknown-blocking',
    26	]);
    27	
    28	const COMPATIBILITY_POLICIES = new Set(['n/a', 'clean-break', 'parse-legacy', 'unknown']);
    29	
    30	const REQUIRED_BASE_FIELDS = [
    31	  'id',
    32	  'surface_class',
    33	  'compatibility_policy',
    34	  'description',
    35	  'schema_file',
    36	  'schema_exports',
    37	  'writers',
    38	  'readers',
    39	  'backing_paths',
    40	  'identity_fields',
    41	  'dangerous_sinks',
    42	  'trust_boundary',
    43	];
    44	
    45	const NON_GREENFIELD_REQUIRED = [
    46	  'reference_surfaces',
    47	  'reference_evidence',
    48	  'migration_policy',
    49	  'legacy_parse_policy',
    50	];
    51	
    52	type Artifact = {
    53	  id: string;
    54	  surface_class: string;
    55	  compatibility_policy: string;
    56	  plane?: string;
    57	  description: string;
    58	  contract: string;
    59	  schema_file: string | null;
    60	  schema_exports: string[];
    61	  writers: string[];
    62	  readers: string[];
    63	  resolvers?: string[];
    64	  backing_paths: string[];
    65	  identity_fields: string[];
    66	  path_derived_fields?: string[];
    67	  dangerous_sinks: string[];
    68	  trust_boundary: string;
    69	  reference_surfaces?: string[];
    70	  reference_evidence?: string[];
    71	  migration_policy?: string;
    72	  legacy_parse_policy?: string;
    73	  dangling_reference_policy?: string;
    74	};
    75	
    76	type ArtifactsFile = {
    77	  version: number;
    78	  artifacts: Artifact[];
    79	};
    80	
    81	function loadArtifacts(): ArtifactsFile {
    82	  const raw = readFileSync(ARTIFACTS_PATH, 'utf-8');
    83	  return JSON.parse(raw);
    84	}
    85	
    86	describe('specs/artifacts.json — authority graph shape', () => {
    87	  const file = loadArtifacts();
    88	
    89	  it('parses as JSON', () => {
    90	    expect(file).toBeDefined();
    91	    expect(file.version).toBe(2);
    92	    expect(Array.isArray(file.artifacts)).toBe(true);
    93	    expect(file.artifacts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    94	  });
    95	
    96	  it('has unique artifact ids', () => {
    97	    const ids = file.artifacts.map((a) => a.id);
    98	    const unique = new Set(ids);
    99	    expect(unique.size).toBe(ids.length);
   100	  });
   101	
   102	  it('uses only known surface_class values', () => {
   103	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   104	      expect(
   105	        SURFACE_CLASSES.has(artifact.surface_class),
   106	        `${artifact.id} has unknown surface_class ${artifact.surface_class}`,
   107	      ).toBe(true);
   108	    }
   109	  });
   110	
   111	  it('uses only known compatibility_policy values', () => {
   112	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   113	      expect(
   114	        COMPATIBILITY_POLICIES.has(artifact.compatibility_policy),
   115	        `${artifact.id} has unknown compatibility_policy ${artifact.compatibility_policy}`,
   116	      ).toBe(true);
   117	    }
   118	  });
   119	
   120	  it('every artifact has required base fields', () => {
   121	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   122	      for (const field of REQUIRED_BASE_FIELDS) {
   123	        expect(
   124	          Object.hasOwn(artifact, field),
   125	          `${artifact.id} is missing required base field ${field}`,
   126	        ).toBe(true);
   127	      }
   128	    }
   129	  });
   130	
   131	  it('schema_exports is a non-empty array for every artifact with a schema_file', () => {
   132	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   133	      if (artifact.schema_file) {
   134	        expect(Array.isArray(artifact.schema_exports)).toBe(true);
   135	        expect(artifact.schema_exports.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   136	      }
   137	    }
   138	  });
   139	
   140	  // Slice 11 — schema_exports existence hardening.
   141	  it('every schema_exports name is actually `export const <name>`d from its schema_file', () => {
   142	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   143	      if (!artifact.schema_file) continue;
   144	      const schemaAbs = join(REPO_ROOT, artifact.schema_file);
   145	      const src = readFileSync(schemaAbs, 'utf-8');
   146	      for (const name of artifact.schema_exports) {
   147	        expect(
   148	          schemaExportPresent(src, name),
   149	          `${artifact.id}: schema_exports names "${name}" but it is not export const-d from ${artifact.schema_file}`,
   150	        ).toBe(true);
   151	      }
   152	    }
   153	  });
   154	});
   155	
   156	describe('schema_exports existence checker — constructed-violation guard (Slice 11)', () => {
   157	  it('accepts names that appear as `export const <name>`', () => {
   158	    const src = 'export const Foo = 1;\nexport const Bar = 2;\n';
   159	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Foo')).toBe(true);
   160	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Bar')).toBe(true);
   161	  });
   162	
   163	  it('rejects names that do not appear at all', () => {
   164	    const src = 'export const Foo = 1;\n';
   165	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Missing')).toBe(false);
   166	  });
   167	
   168	  it('rejects names that appear only as export {name} re-exports (not the defining site)', () => {
   169	    const src = "export { Foo } from './other.js';\n";
   170	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Foo')).toBe(false);
   171	  });
   172	
   173	  it('rejects substring matches (word-boundary enforced)', () => {
   174	    const src = 'export const FooBar = 1;\n';
   175	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Foo')).toBe(false);
   176	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Bar')).toBe(false);
   177	  });
   178	
   179	  it('accepts name at end of file (trailing newline not required)', () => {
   180	    const src = 'export const Foo = 1';
   181	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Foo')).toBe(true);
   182	  });
   183	
   184	  it('accepts name on its own line even when other exports surround it', () => {
   185	    const src = '// header\n\nexport const Alpha = z.string();\nexport const Beta = z.number();\n';
   186	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Alpha')).toBe(true);
   187	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Beta')).toBe(true);
   188	  });
   189	
   190	  // Slice 23 (Codex HIGH #6 fold-in): regex was broadened from `export const`
   191	  // only to `export (const|type|function|class)` so that type-only exports
   192	  // like `AppliedEntry` and `JsonObject` are not silently outside the
   193	  // ledger. Full TS AST-based coverage remains v0.2 scope.
   194	  it('accepts `export function <name>` defining form', () => {
   195	    const src = 'export function helper() {}\n';
   196	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'helper')).toBe(true);
   197	  });
   198	
   199	  it('accepts `export type <name>` defining form (type-only alias)', () => {
   200	    const src = 'export type AppliedEntry = { layer: string };\n';
   201	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'AppliedEntry')).toBe(true);
   202	  });
   203	
   204	  it('accepts `export class <name>` defining form', () => {
   205	    const src = 'export class Helper {}\n';
   206	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Helper')).toBe(true);
   207	  });
   208	
   209	  it('rejects when `export const` is commented out', () => {
   210	    const src = '// export const Foo = 1\n';
   211	    expect(schemaExportPresent(src, 'Foo')).toBe(false);
   212	  });
   213	});
   214	
   215	describe('specs/artifacts.json — class-conditional fields', () => {
   216	  const file = loadArtifacts();
   217	
   218	  it('every non-greenfield / non-external-protocol artifact declares reference + migration fields', () => {
   219	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   220	      if (
   221	        artifact.surface_class === 'greenfield' ||
   222	        artifact.surface_class === 'external-protocol'
   223	      ) {
   224	        continue;
   225	      }
   226	      for (const field of NON_GREENFIELD_REQUIRED) {
   227	        expect(
   228	          Object.hasOwn(artifact, field),
   229	          `${artifact.id} (${artifact.surface_class}) missing ${field}`,
   230	        ).toBe(true);
   231	      }
   232	      expect(
   233	        artifact.compatibility_policy,
   234	        `${artifact.id} has compatibility_policy=unknown which blocks contract authorship`,
   235	      ).not.toBe('unknown');
   236	    }
   237	  });
   238	
   239	  it('successor-to-live artifacts declare a non-empty reference characterization', () => {
   240	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   241	      if (artifact.surface_class !== 'successor-to-live') continue;
   242	      expect(
   243	        artifact.reference_surfaces?.length ?? 0,
   244	        `${artifact.id} has empty reference_surfaces`,
   245	      ).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   246	      expect(
   247	        artifact.reference_evidence?.length ?? 0,
   248	        `${artifact.id} has empty reference_evidence`,
   249	      ).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   250	    }
   251	  });
   252	
   253	  it('every declared reference_evidence file exists on disk', () => {
   254	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   255	      if (!artifact.reference_evidence) continue;
   256	      for (const relPath of artifact.reference_evidence) {
   257	        const abs = join(REPO_ROOT, relPath);
   258	        expect(existsSync(abs), `${artifact.id}: reference_evidence missing — ${relPath}`).toBe(
   259	          true,
   260	        );
   261	      }
   262	    }
   263	  });
   264	});
   265	
   266	describe('specs/artifacts.json — continuity is successor-to-live clean-break', () => {
   267	  const file = loadArtifacts();
   268	  const byId = new Map(file.artifacts.map((a) => [a.id, a]));
   269	
   270	  it('contains continuity.record', () => {
   271	    expect(byId.has('continuity.record')).toBe(true);
   272	  });
   273	
   274	  it('contains continuity.index', () => {
   275	    expect(byId.has('continuity.index')).toBe(true);
   276	  });
   277	
   278	  it('continuity.record is NOT greenfield', () => {
   279	    const record = byId.get('continuity.record');
   280	    expect(record?.surface_class).not.toBe('greenfield');

   282	
   283	  it('continuity.index is NOT greenfield', () => {

   286	  });
   287

   289	    expect(byId.get('continuity.record')?.compatibility_policy).toBe('clean-break');
   290	  });
   291	
   292	  it('continuity.index has compatibility_policy=clean-break', () => {

   294	  });
   295	
   296	  it('continuity.record and continuity.index have legacy_parse_policy=reject', () => {

   381	describe('plane classifier helper — constructed-violation guard (Slice 12)', () => {

   393	
   394	  it('planeIsValid rejects non-string values', () => {

   402	describe('trust-boundary origin-token helper — constructed-violation guard (Slice 12)', () => {
   403	  it('accepts prose containing operator-local', () => {
   404	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('operator-local persisted state; append-only')).toBe(true);
   405	  });
   406	
   407	  it('accepts prose containing engine-computed', () => {

   409	  });
   410	
   411	  it('accepts prose containing model-authored', () => {

   414	
   415	  // Codex fold-in HIGH #3 (ADR-0004): `mixed` is a cardinality of origins, not an

   418	  // as data-plane under the worst-case-producer rule; their trust_boundary prose no
   419	  // longer begins with "mixed;".
   420	  it('rejects prose containing only "mixed" (mixed is not an origin)', () => {
   421	    expect(
   422	      trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('mixed; plugin-layer is author-signed, operator layers differ'),
   423	    ).toBe(false);
   424	  });
   425	
   426	  it('accepts prose with multiple origin tokens', () => {
   427	    expect(
   428	      trustBoundaryHasOriginToken(
   429	        'operator-local persisted state; may contain model-authored narrative',
   430	      ),
   431	    ).toBe(true);
   432	  });
   433	
   434	  it('rejects prose with no origin token', () => {
   435	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only')).toBe(
   436	      false,
   437	    );
   438	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('author-signed static catalog projection')).toBe(false);
   439	  });
   440	
   441	  it('rejects empty or non-string inputs', () => {

   444	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken(null)).toBe(false);
   445	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken(0)).toBe(false);
   446	  });
   447	
   448	  // Case-insensitive match — origin tokens are lowercased before comparison. Documented here
   449	  // because the rule is prose-based and a case-bump in trust_boundary should still pass.
   450	  it('matches case-insensitively', () => {
   451	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('Operator-Local persisted state')).toBe(true);
   452	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('ENGINE-COMPUTED projection')).toBe(true);
   453	  });
   454	
   455	  // Codex fold-in HIGH #2: naive substring matching would accept "never operator-local"
   456	  // because `operator-local` is a substring. The negation-window check rejects origin-token
   457	  // matches whose immediate left-context ends in "not ", "non-", "no ", or "never ".

   459	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('not operator-local; author-signed only')).toBe(false);
   460	  });
   461	
   462	  it('rejects "never X" — negated single-clause origin', () => {
   463	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('never operator-local; author-signed only')).toBe(false);
   464	  });
   465	
   466	  it('rejects "no X" — negated single-clause origin', () => {
   467	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('no operator-local writes; author-signed')).toBe(false);
   468	  });
   469	
   470	  it('rejects "non-X" — negated single-clause origin', () => {
   471	    expect(trustBoundaryHasOriginToken('non-operator-local content only')).toBe(false);
   472	  });
   473	
   474	  // Positive-after-negative: the first clause negates, but a second clause affirms. The
   475	  // rule is per-match; as long as ONE unnegated origin token appears, the prose passes.
   476	  it('accepts prose where a later clause affirms an origin after earlier negation', () => {
   477	    expect(
   478	      trustBoundaryHasOriginToken(
   479	        'never read from remote; operator-local persisted state authoritative',
   480	      ),
   481	    ).toBe(true);
   482	  });

   486	  // The "never" here refers to authority, not to "operator-local". The rule must pass.
   487	  it('accepts run.projection-style prose (late-clause "never" does not negate early-clause origin)', () => {
   488	    expect(
   489	      trustBoundaryHasOriginToken(

   491	      ),

   496	// ADR-0005 v2 — the deferral allowlist from Slice 12 is removed. `plane` is structurally
   497	// required on every artifact. This block replaces the v1 deferral tests with a total-
   498	// coverage assertion (back-stopped by the positive classifications above) and Codex
   499	// MED #5 fold-in: a broader guard against reintroducing an escape hatch under any name.
   500	describe('specs/artifacts.json — plane coverage (ADR-0005 v2)', () => {
   501	  const file = loadArtifacts();
   502	
   503	  it('every artifact has a plane (no deferrals)', () => {

   505	      expect(
   506	        Object.hasOwn(artifact, 'plane'),
   507	        `${artifact.id}: v2 requires plane on every artifact (ADR-0005). No deferral allowlist exists.`,
   508	      ).toBe(true);
   509	    }
   510	  });
   511	
   512	  // Codex MED #5 fold-in — the v1 escape hatch was named `plane_deferred` in the draft
   513	  // and `PLANE_DEFERRED_IDS` in audit.mjs. A future regression might reintroduce the
   514	  // concept under a different name. This test bans the most plausible aliases on every
   515	  // artifact row, plus any key matching the pattern `plane_*defer*` case-insensitively.
   516	  // If a genuine new field is needed, rename it to something that does not match the
   517	  // pattern or write a superseding ADR.
   518	  const DEFERRAL_ALIAS_FIELDS = [
   519	    'plane_deferred',
   520	    'plane_pending',
   521	    'plane_status',
   522	    'plane_rationale',
   523	    'plane_exception',
   524	    'plane_exempt',
   525	  ];
   526	
   527	  it('no artifact reintroduces a known deferral-alias field', () => {
   528	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   529	      const rec = artifact as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
   530	      for (const alias of DEFERRAL_ALIAS_FIELDS) {
   531	        expect(
   532	          Object.hasOwn(rec, alias),
   533	          `${artifact.id}: ${alias} field is not part of the v2 schema — remove it or write a superseding ADR`,
   534	        ).toBe(false);
   535	      }
   536	    }
   537	  });
   538	
   539	  it('no artifact has a field matching /plane.*defer|plane.*exempt|plane.*pending/i', () => {
   540	    const pattern = /plane.*(?:defer|exempt|pending)/i;
   541	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   542	      const rec = artifact as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
   543	      for (const key of Object.keys(rec)) {
   544	        expect(
   545	          pattern.test(key),
   546	          `${artifact.id}: field "${key}" matches a deferral-alias pattern; the v2 schema has no deferral escape hatch`,
   547	        ).toBe(false);

   553	// Codex LOW #9 fold-in — pin the exact v2 trust_boundary strings for the three
   554	// worst-case-classified artifacts. The token-match helper only asserts *an* origin
   555	// appears; these tests assert the **specific** strings ADR-0005 committed to, so a
   556	// silent prose edit that drops an origin or reintroduces "mixed;" would fail a named
   557	// test rather than hide behind the generic token-match.
   558	describe('specs/artifacts.json — v2 trust_boundary prose pinned (ADR-0005, Codex LOW #9)', () => {

   562	  const EXPECTED_TRUST_BOUNDARIES: Record<string, string> = {
   563	    'selection.override':
   564	      'operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure and ghost-provenance rejection at parse time. Composition produces selection.resolution (separate artifact).',
   565	    'adapter.registry':
   566	      'operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates reserved-name disjointness, own-property closure, and registry-key/descriptor-name parity at parse time.',
   567	    'adapter.reference':
   568	      'operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates named-ref registry closure during resolution. MUST NOT appear in run.log (resolved-form-only per ADAPTER-I10).',
   569	  };
   570	
   571	  for (const [id, expected] of Object.entries(EXPECTED_TRUST_BOUNDARIES)) {
   572	    it(`${id} trust_boundary matches the v2 pinned string`, () => {
   573	      const artifact = byId.get(id);

   575	      expect(artifact?.trust_boundary).toBe(expected);
   576	    });
   577	  }
   578	});

   580	// Codex fold-in MED #6: control-plane artifacts must not derive identity from filesystem

   598	// Slice 23 — reverse reciprocation check (HIGH #4, arc-phase-1-close-codex.md).
   599	// An artifact row that names a contract file must be reciprocated: the contract's
   600	// frontmatter `artifact_ids` list must include the artifact's id. Catches the
   601	// failure mode where an artifact points at a contract that no longer claims it
   602	// back (rename drift, copy-paste mistake, or dropped id during reorganization).
   603	describe('specs/artifacts.json — contract reciprocation (ADR-0003, HIGH #4)', () => {
   604	  const file = loadArtifacts();
   605	
   606	  it('every artifact.contract points to an existing file', () => {
   607	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   608	      if (!artifact.contract) continue;
   609	      const abs = join(REPO_ROOT, artifact.contract);
   610	      expect(
   611	        existsSync(abs),
   612	        `${artifact.id}: contract "${artifact.contract}" does not exist on disk`,
   613	      ).toBe(true);
   614	    }
   615	  });
   616	
   617	  it("every artifact.contract's frontmatter lists the artifact id back in artifact_ids", () => {
   618	    for (const artifact of file.artifacts) {
   619	      if (!artifact.contract) continue;
   620	      const abs = join(REPO_ROOT, artifact.contract);
   621	      const { ok, frontmatter, error } = readFrontmatter(abs);
   622	      expect(ok, `${artifact.id}: contract frontmatter unreadable — ${error ?? ''}`).toBe(true);
   623	      if (!ok) continue;
   624	      expect(
   625	        contractReciprocatesArtifact(frontmatter, artifact.id),
   626	        `${artifact.id}: contract "${artifact.contract}" does not reciprocate — its artifact_ids frontmatter does not contain "${artifact.id}"`,
   627	      ).toBe(true);
   628	    }
   629	  });
   630	});
   631	
   632	describe('contractReciprocatesArtifact — constructed-violation guard (Slice 23)', () => {
   633	  it('returns true when artifact_ids contains the id', () => {
   634	    expect(contractReciprocatesArtifact({ artifact_ids: ['foo.bar'] }, 'foo.bar')).toBe(true);
   635	  });
   636	
   637	  it('returns true when artifact_ids lists several ids including the target', () => {
   638	    expect(
   639	      contractReciprocatesArtifact({ artifact_ids: ['a.one', 'b.two', 'c.three'] }, 'b.two'),
   640	    ).toBe(true);
   641	  });
   642	
   643	  it('returns false when artifact_ids omits the id', () => {
   644	    expect(contractReciprocatesArtifact({ artifact_ids: ['a.one'] }, 'b.two')).toBe(false);
   645	  });
   646	
   647	  it('returns false when artifact_ids is missing', () => {
   648	    expect(contractReciprocatesArtifact({}, 'foo.bar')).toBe(false);
   649	  });
   650	
   651	  it('returns false when artifact_ids is not an array', () => {
   652	    expect(contractReciprocatesArtifact({ artifact_ids: 'foo.bar' }, 'foo.bar')).toBe(false);
   653	  });
   654	
   655	  it('returns false when frontmatter is null or undefined', () => {
   656	    expect(contractReciprocatesArtifact(null, 'foo.bar')).toBe(false);
   657	    expect(contractReciprocatesArtifact(undefined, 'foo.bar')).toBe(false);
   658	  });
   659	});

   661	// Slice 23 — schema-export coverage ledger (HIGH #4, arc-phase-1-close-codex.md).
   662	// Every non-index file in src/schemas/ must be either bound to an artifact.schema_file
   663	// OR explicitly allowlisted in SCHEMA_FILE_ALLOWLIST with a category + reason. Every
   664	// `export const <Name>` in a bound file must appear in the owning artifact's
   665	// schema_exports. This is the ledger that would have flagged config.ts schemas being
   666	// absorbed by adapter.registry.schema_exports without a first-class config.* artifact
   667	// (MED #18, named Knight-Leveson correlated miss tracked to Slice 26).
   668	describe('src/schemas/ — schema-export coverage ledger (ADR-0003, HIGH #4)', () => {
   669	  const file = loadArtifacts();
   670	  const schemasDir = join(REPO_ROOT, 'src', 'schemas');
   671	  const schemaFiles = readdirSync(schemasDir)
   672	    .filter((f) => f.endsWith('.ts') && f !== 'index.ts')
   673	    .map((f) => `src/schemas/${f}`)
   674	    .sort();
   675	  const artifactSchemaFiles = new Set(
   676	    file.artifacts.map((a) => a.schema_file).filter((v): v is string => typeof v === 'string'),
   677	  );

     1	import { mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, symlinkSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     2	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
     3	import { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path';
     4	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
     5	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     6	import { z } from 'zod';
     7	
     8	import { checkPluginCommandClosure } from '../../scripts/audit.mjs';
     9	
    10	const HERE = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
    11	const REPO_ROOT = resolve(HERE, '..', '..');
    12	
    13	// Minimal plugin-manifest schema (Codex MED 6 fold-in). Kept inline so the
    14	// test exercises an INDEPENDENT shape definition — if helper/schema diverge,
    15	// the test catches the disagreement. Schema is intentionally conservative:
    16	// it matches the current P2.2 scaffold shape and will need amending when
    17	// manifest semantics broaden (e.g. per-command rigor hints at P2.8+).
    18	const PluginCommandEntry = z
    19	  .object({
    20	    name: z.string().min(1),
    21	    file: z.string().regex(/^commands\/[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.md$/, {
    22	      message: 'file must match commands/<basename>.md (flat grammar)',
    23	    }),
    24	    description: z.string().min(1),
    25	  })
    26	  .strict();
    27	const PluginManifest = z
    28	  .object({
    29	    name: z.string().min(1),
    30	    version: z.string().min(1),
    31	    description: z.string().min(1),
    32	    keywords: z.array(z.string()),
    33	    commands: z.array(PluginCommandEntry).min(2),
    34	  })
    35	  .strict();
    36	
    37	// These tests exercise checkPluginCommandClosure (audit.mjs Check 23,
    38	// introduced by slice P2.2) against constructed temp-dir fixtures. They
    39	// encode ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 binding requirements: plugin.json carries a
    40	// `commands` array whose entries correspond to `.claude-plugin/commands/*.md`
    41	// files with non-empty YAML frontmatter `name` + `description` and a
    42	// non-empty body; required anchor commands `circuit:run` and
    43	// `circuit:explore` are present; no orphan command files exist on disk.
    44	
    45	function withTempRepo(fn: (root: string) => void) {
    46	  const root = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'circuit-next-plugin-surface-'));
    47	  try {
    48	    fn(root);
    49	  } finally {
    50	    rmSync(root, { recursive: true, force: true });
    51	  }
    52	}
    53	
    54	function writeRel(root: string, rel: string, body: string) {
    55	  const abs = join(root, rel);
    56	  mkdirSync(dirname(abs), { recursive: true });
    57	  writeFileSync(abs, body);
    58	}
    59	
    60	const VALID_COMMAND_FILE_BODY = (name: string, desc: string) => `---
    61	name: ${name}
    62	description: ${desc}
    63	---
    64	
    65	# /${name} scaffold
    66	
    67	Non-empty body for plugin-command-closure audit check validation.
    68	`;
    69	
    70	function writeValidScaffold(root: string) {
    71	  writeRel(
    72	    root,
    73	    '.claude-plugin/plugin.json',
    74	    JSON.stringify(
    75	      {
    76	        name: 'test-plugin',
    77	        version: '0.0.1',
    78	        description: 'test fixture',
    79	        keywords: ['test'],
    80	        commands: [
    81	          {
    82	            name: 'circuit:run',
    83	            file: 'commands/circuit-run.md',
    84	            description: 'Router placeholder description (ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 anchor).',
    85	          },
    86	          {
    87	            name: 'circuit:explore',
    88	            file: 'commands/circuit-explore.md',
    89	            description: 'Explore placeholder description (ADR-0007 CC#P2-1 target).',
    90	          },
    91	        ],
    92	      },
    93	      null,
    94	      2,
    95	    ),
    96	  );
    97	  writeRel(
    98	    root,
    99	    '.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-run.md',
   100	    VALID_COMMAND_FILE_BODY('circuit:run', 'Router placeholder'),
   101	  );
   102	  writeRel(
   103	    root,
   104	    '.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-explore.md',
   105	    VALID_COMMAND_FILE_BODY('circuit:explore', 'Explore placeholder'),
   106	  );
   107	}
   108	
   109	describe('checkPluginCommandClosure (ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 enforcement, P2.2)', () => {
   110	  it('passes on a valid scaffold with both anchor commands', () => {
   111	    withTempRepo((root) => {
   112	      writeValidScaffold(root);
   113	      const result = checkPluginCommandClosure(root);
   114	      expect(result.level).toBe('green');
   115	      expect(result.detail).toMatch(/anchors circuit:run \+ circuit:explore present/);
   116	    });
   117	  });
   118	
   119	  it('reds when plugin.json is missing', () => {
   120	    withTempRepo((root) => {
   121	      const result = checkPluginCommandClosure(root);
   122	      expect(result.level).toBe('red');
   123	      expect(result.detail).toMatch(/plugin\.json missing/);
   124	    });
   125	  });
   126	
   127	  it('reds when plugin.json fails to parse as JSON', () => {
   128	    withTempRepo((root) => {
   129	      writeRel(root, '.claude-plugin/plugin.json', '{ invalid json :::');
   130	      const result = checkPluginCommandClosure(root);
   131	      expect(result.level).toBe('red');
   132	      expect(result.detail).toMatch(/failed to parse/);
   133	    });
   134	  });
   135	
   136	  it('reds when plugin.json omits the commands array', () => {
   137	    withTempRepo((root) => {
   138	      writeRel(
   139	        root,
   140	        '.claude-plugin/plugin.json',
   141	        JSON.stringify({ name: 't', version: '0', description: 'd', keywords: [] }),
   142	      );
   143	      const result = checkPluginCommandClosure(root);
   144	      expect(result.level).toBe('red');
   145	      expect(result.detail).toMatch(/missing `commands` array/);
   146	    });
   147	  });
   148	
   149	  it('reds when commands entry references a missing file', () => {
   150	    withTempRepo((root) => {
   151	      writeValidScaffold(root);
   152	      // Remove the circuit-run.md file but leave the manifest entry.
   153	      rmSync(join(root, '.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-run.md'));

Author:     Pete Petrash <pete.petrash@gmail.com>

    slice-32: ADR-0007 — Phase 2 close criteria locked (P2.1 governance)
    
    Lane: Ratchet-Advance (governance ratchet — close-criteria authority
    ratchet; advances independently from Slice 31a's phase-graph authority
    ratchet per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #8; additionally advances the
    audit-coverage ratchet via `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` and the
    no-aggregate-scoring firewall surface via ADR-0007 §6 inline
    restatement).
    
    Trajectory: this slice (P2.1 in the plan) serves the Phase 2 close-
    criteria-lock arc, which serves the Phase 2 entry goal; no earlier
    slice has made it smaller or mis-sequenced — Slice 31a opened Phase 2,
    plan at 58b53a5/3bf4868 framed the draft list, this slice locks it
    authoritatively.
    
    Failure mode: draft close criteria in
    specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md marked "draft — candidate, not
    locked" could not govern near-term slices (P2.2 plugin scaffold, P2.3
    explore contract, P2.4 agent adapter, P2.5 end-to-end fixture); the
    first code slice to cite a criterion would either invent a partial
    close definition or drift, producing ambiguous Phase 2 close later.
    
    Acceptance evidence:
    - specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md — the locked-
      governance ADR (ACCEPTED post-fold-in)
    - specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md — Codex cross-model challenger
      pass (opening: REJECT PENDING FOLD-INS; 7 HIGH / 5 MED / 1 LOW /
      1 META; closing: ACCEPT-WITH-FOLD-INS; all HIGH + MED + LOW + META
      folded in via ADR-0007 revision in same authoring session; fold-in
      table at ADR-0007 Appendix B)
    - specs/reviews/adversarial-yield-ledger.md — row appended:
      class=governance, rigor_profile=standard, pass 1
    - specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §Phase 2 close criteria —
      amended in place with full locked summary table (mirror of ADR-0007
      §Decision.2, not a pointer); Open Questions #3 (container isolation)
      and #5 (explore spine policy) marked RESOLVED via ADR-0007
    - scripts/audit.mjs Check 22 added:
      checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation — for every Phase 2 slice commit
      (past discipline floor + at or after Phase 2 open + ADR-0007 in
      commit tree) that touches isolation-protected paths, requires
      explicit posture in commit body or Break-Glass lane; prior verify-
      gate check shifted to Check 23
    - 754 tests green; 20 green / 1 yellow / 1 red audit (yellow is
      pre-existing framing warning on old docs commits, unrelated; red
      is "Status docs current" which flips green the moment this
      commit's subject "slice-32:" lands as HEAD)
    
    Alternate framing: author ADR-0007 after P2.2–P2.5 land, once real
    surface pressure emerges on the close criteria. Rejected because
    lane discipline is weaker without an authoritative anchor — P2.2+
    slices would have no ADR to bind to and would either drift or
    fabricate their own partial close definitions.
    
    Authority: specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md (this
    slice's primary artifact) + CLAUDE.md §Core methodology + CLAUDE.md
    §Hard invariants #6 (cross-model challenger required for ratchet
    change) + #8 (no aggregate scoring across ratchets).
    
    Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required) —
    this slice is LLM drafting in the operator session under operator
    full-autonomy authorization for overnight Phase 2 progress, not an
    agent/tool process with filesystem authority. Per ADR-0007
    §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 interim-enforcement scope and the LLM-drafting-
    vs-agent-tool-process distinction made operative there.
    
    P2.1 lands ADR-0007, locking Phase 2 close criteria with concrete
    executable enforcement bindings (test paths, audit function names,
    fixture/golden paths, non-substitutable failure conditions — not
    slice-name-only bindings). CC#P2-7 container isolation is re-deferred
    at the close-criterion layer with nine named trigger conditions and
    an interim per-slice citation audit check. This ADR does not move
    Phase 2 closer to done; it defines what done means so that P2.2
    through P2.11 + P2-MODEL-EFFORT can bind against a fixed target.
    Policy-level CLAUDE.md hard invariants #1–#4 remain in force at the
    policy layer; the close-criterion-layer weakening is acknowledged
    openly in ADR-0007 §Consequences.Accepted and §6 Precedent firewall.

Author:     Pete Petrash <pete.petrash@gmail.com>

    slice-33: P2.2 — plugin manifest + command-surface scaffold (+ audit Check 23)
    
    Lane: Ratchet-Advance (plugin-surface ratchet first step; audit-coverage
    ratchet; contract-test-count ratchet — three independently tracked
    ratchet advances per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #8).
    
    Trajectory: this slice (P2.2 in the plan) serves the ADR-0007 CC#P2-3
    plugin-command-registration arc (scaffold at P2.2, full invokability at
    P2.11), which serves the Phase 2 one-workflow-parity arc by giving
    P2.3+ slices a real command surface to attach to rather than orphaned
    CLI entries. No earlier slice makes this one smaller — slice-32 locked
    the close criteria; P2.2 is the first code slice binding to them.
    
    Failure mode: without this scaffold plus an enforcement check, P2.3's
    `explore` contract + fixture would have no plugin-surface binding, and
    the first invocation-evidence slice (P2.11) would either discover the
    gap late or fabricate an ad-hoc surface. A presence-only audit check
    would not catch silent-rename, anchor swap, symlink-escape, or
    YAML-empty-value regressions — each of which would still satisfy
    "files exist" but break CC#P2-3 semantically.
    
    Acceptance evidence:
    - .claude-plugin/plugin.json expanded with commands[] (two anchors:
      circuit:run + circuit:explore); matching
      .claude-plugin/commands/circuit-run.md + circuit-explore.md scaffold
      bodies explicitly marked "not implemented yet — P2.8/P2.3+P2.5
      implementing slices."
    - scripts/audit.mjs Check 23 `checkPluginCommandClosure` enforces:
      manifest/commands closure; unique names + files; grammar
      `commands/<basename>.md` flat-only; symlink rejection (lstat-based);
      anchor-to-canonical-file binding (HIGH 1 fold-in); YAML-aware empty
      detection via comment-stripping + folded-scalar + quoted-string
      normalization (MED 5 fold-in); recursive orphan walk + nested-dir
      rejection (MED 4 fold-in). Verify-gate shifted Check 23 → Check 24.
    - scripts/audit.d.mts adds type declarations for
      `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` (slice-32 export, previously
      missing) and `checkPluginCommandClosure`.
    - tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts adds 26 tests: happy-path
      self-check + 24 negative fixtures (missing manifest, JSON parse
      error, missing commands array, missing file, orphan, anchor missing,
      anchor-at-non-canonical-file, swapped anchors, duplicate names,
      grammar violations, non-.md ext, symlink escape, nested dir, non-.md
      in commands/, YAML-empty name/description variants, inline-comment
      only name, empty folded scalar, path escape) + 1 Zod-schema-level
      live-manifest validation (MED 6 fold-in for independent
      helper/schema disagreement detection).
    - specs/ratchet-floor.json contract_test_count advanced 574 → 727
      (MED 7 fold-in — closes the moving-window hole for the +26 new
      tests); last_advanced_in_slice flipped 26b → 33.
    - tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts updated: the
      `last_advanced_in_slice` live-assertion pins the current slice id
      (33) so any future floor advancement forgetting to update the
      marker fails the test. Test comment explains the semantics.
    - specs/reviews/arc-slice-33-plugin-surface-codex.md — Codex challenger
      pass (opening verdict REJECT PENDING FOLD-INS, 3 HIGH / 4 MED / 0
      LOW / 1 META; closing verdict ACCEPT-WITH-FOLD-INS after all HIGH +
      MED + META folded in). Arc-review shape with commands_run,
      opened_scope, skipped_scope.
    - specs/reviews/adversarial-yield-ledger.md row appended
      (class=reversible, rigor_profile=standard, pass 1).
    - 780 tests green (+26 from slice-32's 754); 22 green / 1 yellow / 0
      red audit (yellow is pre-existing framing warning on old docs
      commits, unrelated).
    
    Alternate framing: defer P2.2 until after P2.3/P2.4/P2.5 land, and
    only then author the plugin manifest from the target workflow's
    pressure. Rejected because the scaffold is cheap, the audit floor
    grows strictly better with it in place before P2.3+ lands, and the
    absence of a locked plugin-surface ratchet would let P2.3+ slices
    drift on command-registration semantics. Codex challenger explicitly
    confirmed the present-anchor-before-invokability sequence via HIGH 1
    (anchor-to-file binding is the correct enforcement surface at this
    slice, not P2.11).
    
    Authority: specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md §Decision.1
    CC#P2-3 (plugin command registration close criterion with concrete
    executable enforcement binding); specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md
    §P2.2 (slice framing); CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #6 (cross-model
    challenger for ratchet change) + #8 (no aggregate scoring); Codex
    pass at specs/reviews/arc-slice-33-plugin-surface-codex.md (3 HIGH /
    4 MED / 0 LOW / 1 META folded in, closing ACCEPT-WITH-FOLD-INS).
    
    Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required) —
    this slice is LLM drafting in the operator session under overnight
    full-autonomy authorization, not an agent/tool process with
    filesystem authority.
    
    P2.2 advances the plugin-surface ratchet to `active — partial` for
    CC#P2-3; reaching `active — satisfied` requires P2.11 invocation
    evidence at specs/reviews/p2-11-invoke-evidence.md (deferred).
    CC#P2-3 is still `active — red` overall because full satisfaction
    needs P2.11.

    slice-34: P2.3 — explore workflow contract + fixture + Check 24
    
    Lane: Ratchet-Advance (contract-coverage ratchet first Phase 2 workflow
    contract; audit-coverage ratchet Check 24 for spine-coverage;
    artifact-registry ratchet 5 new explore.* artifacts).
    
    Trajectory: slice-34 (P2.3 in the plan) serves the one-workflow-parity
    arc by authoring the first-parity target workflow's contract + fixture.
    P2.4 (agent adapter) and P2.5 (end-to-end fixture run) bind downstream.
    No earlier slice makes this smaller — slice-32 locked CC#P2-1 target
    = `explore`; slice-33 scaffolded the plugin command surface; slice-34
    is the first slice authoring actual workflow semantics.
    
    Failure mode: without a locked contract the `explore` fixture would
    drift into ad-hoc semantics during P2.4/P2.5 implementation, or the
    spine coverage question (Open Question #5) would re-surface
    unresolved. The `Synthesize → act` title-to-canonical translation
    would be lost in plan prose rather than recorded authoritatively in
    the contract.
    
    Acceptance evidence:
    - specs/contracts/explore.md v0.1 — 310-line workflow-specific
      contract with one runtime-enforced invariant EXPLORE-I1 (canonical
      phase set {frame, analyze, act, review, close} + partial spine-
      policy omits {plan, verify}) + four deferred properties tracked
      in specs/invariants.json (explore.prop.* at target_slice 38+).
      Includes: explicit title-to-canonical translation table with
      rejected-alternative note for `Synthesize → plan`; weaker-
      substitute framing for verify-omission (review is synthesis-
      kinded, not an adversarial dispatch); workflow-kind seam
      acknowledgment; schema-placeholder note for gate `required`
      arrays; 8 reopen conditions covering target retarget / canonical-
      set change / artifact-schema refactor / workflow-kind concept /
      P2.5 deferral non-delivery / reader-graph drift / P2.10
      reconciliation / explore.result dedicated schema need.
    - .claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json — full-spine fixture
      parseable under Workflow.safeParse with 5 phases (frame →
      analyze → synthesize → review → close with Synthesize canonical=
      act) and 5 steps with gates.
    - specs/artifacts.json — 5 new greenfield/data-plane artifact
      entries (explore.brief, .analysis, .synthesis, .review-verdict,
      .result) with model-authored or engine-computed origin tokens,
      reader/writer graph aligned with contract §Artifact reader/
      writer graph table.
    - specs/invariants.json — 1 invariant EXPLORE-I1 (test-enforced,
      binding to tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts describe title)
      + 5 properties (explore.prop.canonical_phase_set_is_correct test-
      enforced; 4 others phase2-property target_slice 38+).
    - scripts/audit.mjs Check 24 `checkSpineCoverage` — rejects kind-
      canonical-set violations for known workflow kinds (explore);
      exempts dogfood-run-0 (Phase 1.5 partial fixture); information-
      only pass-through for unknown kinds. Verify-gate Check bumped
      24 → 25.
    - scripts/audit.d.mts — new export declaration for checkSpineCoverage.
    - tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts — 13 tests exercising
      green/red paths including missing canonical, extra canonical,
      wrong mode, wrong omits, dogfood exemption, unknown-kind pass-
      through, JSON parse failure, no-id fixture, live-repo fixture
      parse under Workflow.safeParse.
    - specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md — Codex contract-review
      challenger pass. Opening verdict: REJECT pending HIGH fold-ins
      (4 HIGH / 7 MED / 2 LOW / 1 META). Closing verdict: REJECT →
      incorporated → ACCEPT (after fold-in). Fold-ins:
        * HIGH 1: EXPLORE-I2..I5 prose-only on contract-surface violates
          ledger rule → demoted to deferred properties (invariant_ids
          now just [EXPLORE-I1]; explore.prop.* rows track the
          guarantees).
        * HIGH 2: fixture writes unregistered explore.result → registered
          in artifacts.json + contract artifact_ids.
        * HIGH 3: EXPLORE-I1 claimed more than checkSpineCoverage
          enforces → narrowed invariant scope with explicit enforcement-
          gap list (rationale content / id-vs-filename heuristic / full
          Workflow.safeParse deferred to P2.5).
        * HIGH 4: `Synthesize → act` undeclared ADR amendment → added
          explicit title-to-canonical translation subsection with
          rationale + rejected `Synthesize → plan` alternative.
        * MED 5: reader/writer graph divergence → authoritative table
          in contract + artifacts.json reader lists aligned + fixture
          step `reads` arrays already aligned.
        * MED 6: property id not in any test title → describe title now
          contains `explore.prop.canonical_phase_set_is_correct`.
        * MED 7: target_slice=29 conflict with P2.5 roadmap → property
          rows now target_slice=38 (P2.5 projected).
        * MED 8: workflow-kind implicit `id` convention → §Workflow-kind
          seam note added with reopen triggers.
        * MED 9: "review covers verify" overclaim → rephrased as weaker
          substitute with explicit v0.1 limitation acknowledgment.
        * MED 10: schema_sections gates are placeholders → §schema_sections
          gate placeholder note added with P2.10 reconciliation trigger.
        * MED 11: spine-coverage tests miss rationale/mapping mutations
          → accepted as ratchet-stage (the basic canonical set / mode /
          omits coverage is the minimum for P2.3; additional mutation
          coverage folded into reopen condition #5 trigger for P2.5).
        * LOW 12: reopen conditions miss realistic drift cases → added
          4 new reopen conditions (5-8) covering deferred invariant
          non-delivery, artifact-graph drift, gate/schema reconciliation,
          explore.result schema need.
        * LOW 13: plan text `explore-md-codex.md` stale → amended to
          `specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md`.
        * META 14: isolation posture cannot be validated from files
          alone → this commit body carries the citation below.
    - specs/reviews/adversarial-yield-ledger.md row appended (class=
      reversible, pass 1).
    - specs/ratchet-floor.json advanced 727 → 740; last_advanced_in_slice
      33 → 34.
    - tests/contracts/status-epoch-ratchet-floor.test.ts
      last_advanced_in_slice live-assertion updated 33 → 34.
    - specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md P2.3 deliverable path
      corrected to specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md (LOW 13).
    - 793 tests green; 23 green / 1 yellow / 0 red audit (yellow is
      pre-existing framing warning on old docs commits, unrelated).
    
    Alternate framing: defer contract authoring until P2.5 pressure
    emerges — rejected because lane discipline is weaker without an
    authoritative anchor; P2.4 adapter slice would have no contract to
    bind artifact shapes against. Codex HIGH 1 / HIGH 3 fold-ins
    specifically confirmed the correct current-layer enforcement shape
    is "one runtime invariant + deferred properties," not "five MUST-
    reject invariants."
    
    Authority: specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md §Decision.1
    CC#P2-1 (one-workflow parity — target=`explore`); CC#P2-6 (spine
    policy coverage; Open Question #5 full-spine Standard rigor
    resolution); specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §P2.3 (slice
    framing); CLAUDE.md §Core methodology pillar 1 (Contract-First);
    CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #6 (cross-model challenger required for
    ratchet change) + #8 (no aggregate scoring); Codex pass at
    specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md.
    
    Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required) —
    this slice is LLM drafting in the operator session under overnight
    full-autonomy authorization, not an agent/tool process with
    filesystem authority.
    
    P2.3 advances the contract-coverage ratchet from 9 domain contracts
    to 9 domain + 1 workflow-specific contract. CC#P2-1 remains `active
    — red` (full satisfaction requires P2.5 end-to-end run with real
    agent dispatch); CC#P2-6 remains `active — red` at close-criterion
    layer (contract declares the canonical set; full satisfaction via
    `checkSpineCoverage` green on the committed fixture which this slice
    achieves — but CC#P2-6 close requires the fixture to actually RUN
    end-to-end, which is P2.5).

 succeeded in 0ms:
{
  "name": "circuit-next",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "description": "Claude Code plugin that automates common developer workflows with per-step configurability (model, effort, skills), grounded in evidence-based prompting research and contract-first + architecture-first + tiny-step-ratcheting methodology.",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.0.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "check": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "lint": "biome check .",
    "format": "biome format --write .",
    "verify": "npm run check && npm run lint && npm run test",
    "audit": "node scripts/audit.mjs",
    "inventory": "node scripts/inventory.mjs",
    "slice:doctor": "node scripts/doctor.mjs",
    "circuit:run": "tsx src/cli/dogfood.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "zod": "^3.23.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@biomejs/biome": "^1.9.4",
    "@types/node": "^22.9.0",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "^5.6.3",
    "vitest": "^2.1.4"
  }
}

   248	/**
   249	 * Slice 23 — collect top-level runtime identifiers from a schema source.
   250	 * Matches `export const|type|function|class <Name>` (Codex HIGH #6 fold-in:
   251	 * the prior `export const`-only regex missed type-only exports like
   252	 * `AppliedEntry`, `JsonObject`). `_`-prefixed names are excluded only if
   253	 * they match `COMPILE_TIME_GUARD_PATTERN` — a stricter filter than the
   254	 * previous broad `_` check (Codex MED #8). Re-exports and `export default`
   255	 * are out of scope for v0.1; full TS AST coverage is tracked v0.2.
   256	 */
   257	export function collectSchemaExports(schemaSrc) {
   258	  const names = new Set();
   259	  const pattern = /^export (?:const|type|function|class) ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\b/gm;
   260	  let match = pattern.exec(schemaSrc);
   261	  while (match !== null) {
   262	    const name = match[1];
   263	    const isKnownGuard = name.startsWith('_') && COMPILE_TIME_GUARD_PATTERN.test(name);
   264	    if (!isKnownGuard) names.add(name);
   265	    match = pattern.exec(schemaSrc);
   266	  }
   267	  return names;
   268	}

   272	 * Returns true iff the contract's `artifact_ids` frontmatter list contains
   273	 * `artifactId`. Used both by audit.mjs and by artifact-authority.test.ts to
   274	 * assert the reverse direction of the authority graph: an artifact row's
   275	 * `contract` pointer must be reciprocated by the contract naming it back.
   276	 */
   277	export function contractReciprocatesArtifact(contractFrontmatter, artifactId) {
   278	  const ids = contractFrontmatter?.artifact_ids;
   279	  if (!Array.isArray(ids)) return false;
   280	  return ids.includes(artifactId);
   281	}
   282	
   283	/**
   284	 * Slice 11 — schema_exports existence check. Returns true iff `name` appears
   285	 * as a top-level defining export in the source (word-boundary exact match
   286	 * on the identifier). Matches `export const|type|function|class <name>`
   287	 * (Codex HIGH #6 fold-in: prior `const`-only regex missed type-only
   288	 * exports like `AppliedEntry`, `JsonObject`). Does not match
   289	 * `export { <name> }` re-exports or `export default` — artifacts.json
   290	 * names the DEFINING module, and defaults have no stable identifier to
   291	 * pin. Full TypeScript AST-based coverage is tracked v0.2.
   292	 */
   293	export function schemaExportPresent(schemaSrc, name) {
   294	  const escaped = name.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
   295	  const pattern = new RegExp(`^export (?:const|type|function|class) ${escaped}\\b`, 'm');
   296	  return pattern.test(schemaSrc);
   297	}
   298	
   299	/**
   300	 * Slice 12 — plane classifier validity check (ADR-0004). Returns true iff
   301	 * `plane` is one of the closed two-element set `{control-plane, data-plane}`.
   302	 */
   303	export function planeIsValid(plane) {
   304	  return PLANES.has(plane);
   305	}
   306	
   307	/**
   308	 * Slice 12 — data-plane trust-boundary-detail check (ADR-0004). Returns
   309	 * true iff the prose `trust_boundary` names at least one **unnegated**
   310	 * origin token from the closed set {operator-local, engine-computed,

     1	import { z } from 'zod';
     2	import { WorkflowId } from './ids.js';
     3	import { DispatchRole } from './step.js';
     4	
     5	export const BuiltInAdapter = z.enum(['agent', 'codex', 'codex-isolated']);
     6	export type BuiltInAdapter = z.infer<typeof BuiltInAdapter>;
     7	
     8	// ADAPTER-I2: the `'auto'` literal is a reserved sentinel for
     9	// `DispatchConfig.default`; adapter names must not collide with it.
    10	export const RESERVED_ADAPTER_NAMES: readonly string[] = [
    11	  ...BuiltInAdapter.options,
    12	  'auto',
    13	] as const;
    14	
    15	export const AdapterName = z.string().regex(/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/);
    16	export type AdapterName = z.infer<typeof AdapterName>;
    17	
    18	// ADAPTER-I3: element-level `.min(1)` forbids empty argv elements
    19	// (e.g. `['']` or `['codex', '']`) which either signal a bug or are a
    20	// silent gotcha — shells drop them; `execve(2)` does not.
    21	export const CustomAdapterDescriptor = z
    22	  .object({
    23	    kind: z.literal('custom'),
    24	    name: AdapterName,
    25	    command: z.array(z.string().min(1)).min(1),
    26	  })
    27	  .strict();
    28	export type CustomAdapterDescriptor = z.infer<typeof CustomAdapterDescriptor>;
    29	
    30	export const BuiltInAdapterRef = z
    31	  .object({
    32	    kind: z.literal('builtin'),
    33	    name: BuiltInAdapter,
    34	  })
    35	  .strict();
    36	export type BuiltInAdapterRef = z.infer<typeof BuiltInAdapterRef>;
    37	
    38	export const NamedAdapterRef = z
    39	  .object({
    40	    kind: z.literal('named'),
    41	    name: AdapterName,
    42	  })
    43	  .strict();
    44	export type NamedAdapterRef = z.infer<typeof NamedAdapterRef>;
    45	
    46	export const AdapterRef = z.discriminatedUnion('kind', [
    47	  BuiltInAdapterRef,
    48	  NamedAdapterRef,
    49	  CustomAdapterDescriptor,
    50	]);
    51	export type AdapterRef = z.infer<typeof AdapterRef>;
    52	
    53	// Codex HIGH #1 fold-in — a resolved adapter MUST NOT still be a named
    54	// reference. Named references are pre-resolution pointers at the registry;
    55	// the dispatcher must dereference them before emitting DispatchStartedEvent.
    56	// `ResolvedAdapter` is the 2-variant discriminated union used at the event
    57	// layer; `AdapterRef` remains the 3-variant pre-resolution union used in
    58	// config and CLI parsing.
    59	export const ResolvedAdapter = z.discriminatedUnion('kind', [
    60	  BuiltInAdapterRef,
    61	  CustomAdapterDescriptor,
    62	]);
    63	export type ResolvedAdapter = z.infer<typeof ResolvedAdapter>;
    64	
    65	// ADAPTER-I7: dispatch resolution source with category + disambiguator.
    66	// Closes the category-only-provenance gap pre-emptively (same shape as
    67	// SEL-I7 applied[] entries for selection). An audit reading
    68	// `DispatchStartedEvent.resolved_from` can identify the exact config
    69	// entry that chose the adapter.
    70	const ExplicitResolutionSource = z.object({ source: z.literal('explicit') }).strict();
    71	const RoleResolutionSource = z.object({ source: z.literal('role'), role: DispatchRole }).strict();
    72	const CircuitResolutionSource = z
    73	  .object({ source: z.literal('circuit'), workflow_id: WorkflowId })
    74	  .strict();
    75	const DefaultResolutionSource = z.object({ source: z.literal('default') }).strict();
    76	const AutoResolutionSource = z.object({ source: z.literal('auto') }).strict();
    77	
    78	export const DispatchResolutionSource = z.discriminatedUnion('source', [
    79	  ExplicitResolutionSource,
    80	  RoleResolutionSource,
    81	  CircuitResolutionSource,
    82	  DefaultResolutionSource,
    83	  AutoResolutionSource,
    84	]);
    85	export type DispatchResolutionSource = z.infer<typeof DispatchResolutionSource>;

    41	export type StepArtifactWrittenEvent = z.infer<typeof StepArtifactWrittenEvent>;

    52	export type GateEvaluatedEvent = z.infer<typeof GateEvaluatedEvent>;
    53	
    54	export const CheckpointRequestedEvent = EventBase.extend({

    60	export type CheckpointRequestedEvent = z.infer<typeof CheckpointRequestedEvent>;
    61	
    62	export const CheckpointResolvedEvent = EventBase.extend({

    69	export type CheckpointResolvedEvent = z.infer<typeof CheckpointResolvedEvent>;
    70	
    71	// ADAPTER-I7: `resolved_from` is a `DispatchResolutionSource` discriminated
    72	// union that names the winning precedence category AND carries the
    73	// disambiguator (`role` on role-match, `workflow_id` on circuit-match).
    74	// An audit reading this event can reconstruct the exact merged-config entry
    75	// that chose the adapter — closes the category-only-provenance gap that the
    76	// flat-enum drafting left open.
    77	//
    78	// Codex HIGH #1 fold-in — `adapter: ResolvedAdapter` (2-variant: built-in or
    79	// custom descriptor). Named references are pre-resolution pointers and MUST
    80	// NOT appear in the event log; the dispatcher dereferences them against the

    82	//
    83	// The role ↔ resolved_from.role binding (Codex HIGH #4) is enforced at the
    84	// Event-union level, not here, because `z.discriminatedUnion` cannot admit
    85	// ZodEffects variants (wrapped via superRefine). Mirrors the `Step` pattern.
    86	export const DispatchStartedEvent = EventBase.extend({
    87	  kind: z.literal('dispatch.started'),
    88	  step_id: StepId,
    89	  attempt: z.number().int().positive(),
    90	  adapter: ResolvedAdapter,
    91	  role: DispatchRole,
    92	  resolved_selection: ResolvedSelection,
    93	  resolved_from: DispatchResolutionSource,
    94	}).strict();
    95	export type DispatchStartedEvent = z.infer<typeof DispatchStartedEvent>;
    96	
    97	export const DispatchCompletedEvent = EventBase.extend({
    98	  kind: z.literal('dispatch.completed'),
    99	  step_id: StepId,
   100	  attempt: z.number().int().positive(),
   101	  verdict: z.string().min(1),
   102	  duration_ms: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
   103	  result_path: z.string().min(1),
   104	  receipt_path: z.string().min(1),
   105	}).strict();
   106	export type DispatchCompletedEvent = z.infer<typeof DispatchCompletedEvent>;
   107	
   108	export const StepCompletedEvent = EventBase.extend({
   109	  kind: z.literal('step.completed'),
   110	  step_id: StepId,
   111	  attempt: z.number().int().positive(),
   112	  route_taken: z.string().min(1),

   114	export type StepCompletedEvent = z.infer<typeof StepCompletedEvent>;
   115	
   116	export const StepAbortedEvent = EventBase.extend({

   118	  step_id: StepId,

   120	  reason: z.string().min(1),
   121	}).strict();
   122	export type StepAbortedEvent = z.infer<typeof StepAbortedEvent>;
   123	
   124	export const RunClosedOutcome = z.enum(['complete', 'aborted', 'handoff', 'stopped', 'escalated']);
   125	export type RunClosedOutcome = z.infer<typeof RunClosedOutcome>;
   126	
   127	export const RunClosedEvent = EventBase.extend({
   128	  kind: z.literal('run.closed'),
   129	  outcome: RunClosedOutcome,

   132	export type RunClosedEvent = z.infer<typeof RunClosedEvent>;

   134	// Codex HIGH #4 fold-in — cross-variant superRefine enforces the

   138	// and hoist cross-field refinements to the union level.
   139	export const Event = z
   140	  .discriminatedUnion('kind', [

   143	    StepArtifactWrittenEvent,
   144	    GateEvaluatedEvent,

   153	  .superRefine((ev, ctx) => {

   161	    }
   162	  });
   163	export type Event = z.infer<typeof Event>;

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba specs/contracts/run.md | sed -n '230,345p'" in /Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next
 succeeded in 0ms:
   230	### Reducer-level (Phase 2 scope)
   231	
   232	- `run.prop.deterministic_replay` — For any valid `RunLog` plus its
   233	  corresponding `Workflow` manifest, two independent reducer runs produce
   234	  bit-identical `Snapshot`s. This is the load-bearing property of the
   235	  event-sourced architecture (`specs/evidence.md` hard invariant 1).
   236	
   237	- `run.prop.attempt_monotonicity_per_step` — For every step_id that appears
   238	  in the log, the sequence of `attempt` values observed on that step_id's
   239	  events is weakly monotonic (each attempt value is ≥ the previous, strictly
   240	  greater when a retry is observed, never decreasing).
   241	
   242	- `run.prop.step_event_causal_ordering` — For every `(step_id, attempt)`
   243	  pair in the log, the event kinds on that pair follow a legal protocol:
   244	  `step.entered` precedes any sub-event on that pair, which precedes exactly
   245	  one of `step.completed` or `step.aborted`. No sub-event may appear without
   246	  a matching `step.entered`; no second terminal event may appear on the
   247	  same pair.
   248	
   249	- `run.prop.checkpoint_event_pairing` — For every `checkpoint.requested`
   250	  event on a `(step_id, attempt)` pair, there is exactly one subsequent
   251	  `checkpoint.resolved` event on the same pair before any terminal step
   252	  event. Unresolved checkpoints are a runtime stall, not a log invariant,
   253	  but a log that contains an unresolved `checkpoint.requested` followed by
   254	  a `step.completed` represents an impossible state.
   255	
   256	- `run.prop.dispatch_event_pairing` — For every `dispatch.started` event on
   257	  a `(step_id, attempt)` pair, there is exactly one subsequent
   258	  `dispatch.completed` event on the same pair before any terminal step
   259	  event. A `dispatch.started` with no matching `dispatch.completed` is
   260	  a reducer inconsistency.
   261	
   262	- `run.prop.artifact_written_before_gate` — For any synthesis step, every
   263	  `gate.evaluated` event with `outcome: 'pass'` on that step is preceded by
   264	  at least one `step.artifact_written` event on the same `(step_id,
   265	  attempt)` pair. (Failing gates can precede any write.)
   266	
   267	- `run.prop.projection_is_a_function` — For any valid `RunLog`, `reducer(log,
   268	  manifest)` is a total function: it produces exactly one `Snapshot`, and
   269	  that Snapshot satisfies the `RunProjection` binding. Combined with
   270	  `deterministic_replay`, this is the full event-sourcing contract.
   271	
   272	## Cross-contract dependencies
   273	
   274	- **event** (`src/schemas/event.ts`) — `RunLog` embeds `Event[]`. Every
   275	  event variant is already `.strict()` (RUN-I8) and declares its own
   276	  per-kind required fields; `RunLog` adds log-level structural invariants
   277	  on top.
   278	- **snapshot** (`src/schemas/snapshot.ts`) — `RunProjection` pairs
   279	  `RunLog` with `Snapshot`. `Snapshot` and `StepState` are both `.strict()`
   280	  (RUN-I8). The `SnapshotStatus` enum is intentionally a superset of
   281	  `RunClosedOutcome` by exactly one value (`'in_progress'`), which is how
   282	  RUN-I7's mapping from log-closure to snapshot-status is total without
   283	  information loss.
   284	- **workflow** (`src/schemas/workflow.ts`) — `RunBootstrappedEvent.workflow_id`
   285	  must refer to a known `Workflow.id` at the given `manifest_hash`. Not
   286	  enforced at the schema layer; enforced at runtime by the workflow
   287	  catalog.
   288	- **lane** (`src/schemas/lane.ts`) — `RunBootstrappedEvent.lane` is a
   289	  required `LaneDeclaration`. RUN-I6 binds it into the Snapshot; evidence
   290	  invariant 3 (every Run carries lane) is load-bearing.
   291	- **rigor** (`src/schemas/rigor.ts`) — frozen at bootstrap (RUN-I6).
   292	- **ids** (`src/schemas/ids.ts`) — `RunId`, `WorkflowId`, `InvocationId`,
   293	  `StepId` branded slugs.
   294	
   295	## Failure modes (carried from evidence)
   296	
   297	- `carry-forward:event-log-insufficient-to-replay` — Existing Circuit's
   298	  `RunBootstrappedEvent` was missing lane; `Snapshot` did not carry
   299	  `manifest_hash`; richer `step.completed`/`step.aborted` events were
   300	  missing (adversarial-review HIGH #3 — `specs/evidence.md` §Adversarial).
   301	  **Closed in Tier 0 skeleton** (`lane` + `manifest_hash` on both;
   302	  `step.*_completed`/`step.aborted` added). Re-ratified here: `RunLog`
   303	  enforces the log-level invariants those changes were meant to support.
   304	
   305	- `carry-forward:snapshot-divergence` — A reducer bug that produces a
   306	  Snapshot inconsistent with its source log was historically silent; the
   307	  Snapshot would simply disagree and nobody would notice until a downstream
   308	  consumer saw wrong data. Closed by RUN-I6/I7: any projection that
   309	  disagrees on bootstrap-frozen fields or closure state is rejected at
   310	  `RunProjection.safeParse`. This is a *consistency* check, not a reducer-
   311	  correctness proof (see `run.prop.deterministic_replay`).
   312	
   313	- `carry-forward:surplus-key-silent-strip` — Prior to this contract,
   314	  `Event` variants and `Snapshot` were not `.strict()`, so a typo in an
   315	  event writer (`artifact_pahh` instead of `artifact_path`) parsed as a
   316	  legal event with the misspelled key silently stripped. Closed by RUN-I8.
   317	
   318	- `carry-forward:cross-run-smuggle` — A log produced by concatenating two
   319	  runs' events would parse under the flat `Event` schema — individual
   320	  events are valid; only the `run_id` inconsistency reveals the error.
   321	  Closed by RUN-I3, with defense-in-depth via the identity-field own-
   322	  property guard (prototype-chain attack class).
   323	
   324	- `carry-forward:nested-surplus-key-silent-strip` — Prior to this slice,
   325	  `.strict()` was applied only at the top level of `Event`/`Snapshot`.
   326	  Surplus keys inside `lane`, `adapter`, `resolved_selection`, or
   327	  `resolved_selection.model` were silently stripped, which meant a
   328	  snapshot-vs-bootstrap lane comparison could wrongly accept a polluted
   329	  payload. Closed by RUN-I8's transitive-strict discipline across
   330	  `LaneDeclaration`, `AdapterRef`, `ProviderScopedModel`, `SkillOverride`,
   331	  `SelectionOverride`, `ResolvedSelection`, `SelectionResolution.applied[]`.
   332	
   333	## Evolution
   334	
   335	- **v0.1 (this draft)** — RUN-I1..I8 enforced at the schema layer:
   336	  `RunLog` aggregate with bootstrap/first-event, sequence monotonicity,
   337	  run_id consistency, bootstrap singleton, closure singleton with
   338	  no-post-closure-events. `RunProjection` aggregate binding log and
   339	  snapshot with bootstrap-frozen field parity, exact `events_consumed`
   340	  equality (no stale prefix), and closure-to-status mapping as a
   341	  compile-time total function (`OutcomeStatusEquality`). `.strict()`
   342	  extended transitively from every event variant + `Snapshot`/`StepState`
   343	  through `LaneDeclaration`, `AdapterRef`, `ProviderScopedModel`,
   344	  `SkillOverride`, `SelectionOverride`, `ResolvedSelection`, and
   345	  `SelectionResolution.applied[]` entries. Identity-field own-property

     1	---
     2	name: phase-2-implementation
     3	description: Phase 2 implementation plan — from Alpha Proof fixture to one-workflow parity. Opens with target-workflow choice, sequences near-term slices, names mid-term skeleton, and records Phase 2 close-criteria candidates.
     4	type: plan
     5	date: 2026-04-21
     6	authored_by: claude-opus-4-7
     7	base_commit: 0223d1162b35458c22c4b8680859f872a83897c0
     8	supersedes_scope:
     9	  - (none — first Phase 2 plan authoring; complements specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md which owns Phase 1.5 close semantics only)

    11	  - specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md §Slice P2-MODEL-EFFORT (pulled in by reference)
