    13	status: active — target workflow locked to `explore` (operator decision 2026-04-21; Codex challenger recommendation adopted over the in-session methodology recommendation of `review`); Phase 2 close criteria LOCKED via ADR-0007 (2026-04-21); Open Questions #3 (container isolation) and #5 (explore spine policy) RESOLVED via ADR-0007
    14	---
    15	
    16	# Phase 2 — Implementation Plan (2026-04-21)
    17	
    18	## Why this plan exists
    19	
    20	Phase 1.5 Alpha Proof closed at slice-31a (ceremony commit `0223d11`).
    21	Phase 2 is now open per ADR-0001 Addendum B as amended by ADR-0006. This
    22	is the first Phase 2 implementation plan. Its job is to name the arc from
    23	the current state — a 2-step dry-run runner on a fixture (dogfood-run-0)
    24	— to the first honest claim of **one-workflow parity with reference
    25	Circuit**: one of the six Circuit workflows (`explore`, `build`, `repair`,
    26	`migrate`, `sweep`, `review`) running end-to-end in circuit-next with
    27	real agent dispatch.
    28	
    29	This plan is **deliberately skeletal** past the near-term slices. Lane
    30	discipline requires ≤30-minute slices; trying to fully specify 15-25
    31	slices at authorship time would drift from the evidence as earlier
    32	slices expose surface. Near-term slices (P2.1 through P2.5) carry full
    33	framing; mid-term slices are named only, with their framing authored in
    34	the slice commit that lands them.
    35	
    36	## Entry state — what closed from Phase 1.5
    37	
    38	See `PROJECT_STATE.md` for the authoritative snapshot. The executable
    39	surface as of HEAD (`0223d11`):
    40	
    41	- **Schemas (18 files):** full contract-bound type surface covering
    42	  workflow, step, phase, run, event, snapshot, config, selection-policy,
    43	  continuity, adapter, skill, gate, lane, manifest, primitives, rigor,
    44	  role, ids.
    45	- **Runtime (7 files, ~950 lines):** event-writer, event-log-reader,
    46	  reducer, snapshot-writer, manifest-snapshot-writer, result-writer,
    47	  runner. Closes a run end-to-end through the manifest/snapshot/event
    48	  loop.
    49	- **CLI:** `src/cli/dogfood.ts` wired to `npm run circuit:run` — runs the
    50	  `dogfood-run-0` fixture through the runtime boundary.
    51	- **Contracts (9):** adapter, config, continuity, phase, run, selection,
    52	  skill, step, workflow — all with invariant ids and property ids bound
    53	  to `specs/invariants.json`.
    54	- **Behavioral concerns (3):** cross-model-challenger, prose-yaml-parity,
    55	  session-hygiene.
    56	- **Plugin surface:** `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` +
    57	  `.claude-plugin/skills/dogfood-run-0/circuit.json` (a partial-spine
    58	  workflow: plan+act only, synthesis+dispatch steps).
    59	- **Audit:** `scripts/audit.mjs` — 21 checks (Check 21 added in 31a);
    60	  20 green / 1 yellow (pre-discipline chore framing) / 0 red.
    61	- **Tests:** 754 passing (701 contract + remainder unit/runner).
    62	- **Scripts:** audit, inventory, slice:doctor.
    63	
    64	### What the Alpha Proof did NOT prove (carried open)
    65	
    66	Per ADR-0006 §What changes 4 and the 14a operator product-direction
    67	check:
    68	
    69	- Real agent dispatch (current dispatch is a dry-run fixture).
    70	- Workflow parity with any reference Circuit workflow.
    71	- Full spine coverage (dogfood-run-0 omits frame/analyze/verify/
    72	  review/close).
    73	- Per-step model + effort assignment (P2-MODEL-EFFORT reserved).
    74	- Container isolation / distinct-UID implementer sandbox.
    75	- Hidden test pool (`tests/properties/hidden/`).
    76	- Plugin command registration (no `/circuit:*` slash commands wired).
    77	- Non-LLM human cold-read evidence (retargeted per ADR-0006).
    78	
    79	## Target workflow for first parity — DECIDED: `explore`
    80	
    81	**Decision:** `explore` locked as the first parity target.
    82	
    83	**Decided by:** operator (product-direction authority per
    84	`memory/project_circuit_next_governance.md`).
    85	
    86	**Decision date:** 2026-04-21.
    87	
    88	**Authority adopted:** Codex challenger recommendation over the
    89	in-session Claude methodology recommendation of `review`. Codex
    90	rationale (preserved verbatim, cited above in the operator-facing
    91	summary):
    92	
    93	> `review` is the safest engineering path because it has the smallest
    94	> step graph and isolates real agent dispatch from routing complexity
    95	> … But product-wise, `review` mostly proves "can we call an agent
    96	> and get a verdict artifact back?" It does not exercise much of the
    97	> workflow system we actually care about.
    98	>
    99	> `explore` feels like the better middle path: it exercises a fuller
   100	> spine, real phase progression, synthesis, artifact production, and
   101	> review, while staying much less gnarly than `build`.
   102	
   103	**Why product-shape authority, not methodology authority:** "which
   104	workflow to pursue first" controls what the first real-agent-dispatch
   105	slice feels like, which use-case circuit-next validates first, and
   106	which contract surface gets the earliest stress. Per governance split,
   107	this decision belongs to the operator and was formally recorded on
   108	2026-04-21.
   109	
   110	**Fallback:** if adapter/routing scope starts ballooning during P2.3
   111	or P2.4, fall back to `review` as a scope-reducing pivot. Pivoting
   112	requires amending this plan (not a silent rename) because the commit
   113	set below binds to `explore` explicitly.
   114	
   115	**Non-options for first parity:** `build`, `repair`, `sweep`, and
   116	`migrate` are deferred to P2.9+ (second-and-onward workflow parity)
   117	because they either carry more spine complexity (`build`, `repair`)
   118	or workflow-shape novelty (`sweep` queue/triage, `migrate`
   119	coexistence/cutover) than the adapter-and-artifact-shake-out first
   120	slice should absorb.
   121	
   122	**What unlocks:** slices P2.3 and P2.5 below are now concrete and
   123	bind to `explore`. P2.1, P2.2, P2.4 were target-agnostic and remain
   124	unchanged.
   125	
   126	## Phase 2 close criteria — LOCKED via ADR-0007 (2026-04-21)
   127	
   128	**Status: LOCKED via ADR-0007 (2026-04-21).** These are the
   129	authoritative Phase 2 close criteria with concrete executable
   130	enforcement bindings. The locked summary table below mirrors
   131	ADR-0007 §Decision.2 in full (not a pointer); on any conflict,
   132	ADR-0007 §Decision.1 is authoritative. Any change — retarget,
   133	weakening, relaxation, introduction of aggregate scoring — must
   134	clear ADR-0007 §6 Precedent firewall before landing. Each criterion
   135	is independently trackable with status values `active — satisfied`,
   136	`active — red`, or `re-deferred` (no aggregate scoring — CLAUDE.md
   137	hard invariant #8; see ADR-0007 §Decision.3 for the forbidden-
   138	wording list).
   139	
   140	| CC# | Title | Status at lock | Enforcement binding (non-substitutable) |
   141	|---|---|---|---|
   142	| P2-1 | One-workflow parity (target: `explore`) | active — red | `tests/runner/explore-e2e-parity.test.ts` + `tests/fixtures/golden/explore/` byte-shape golden (sha256 over normalized-JSON); authored at P2.5. |
   143	| P2-2 | Real agent dispatch | active — red | `src/runtime/adapters/agent.ts` + `tests/runner/agent-dispatch-roundtrip.test.ts` with durable dispatch transcript (adapter id, request/receipt/result hashes, reducer+writer consumption); CI-skip requires `tests/fixtures/agent-smoke/last-run.json` with commit-ancestor audit. |
   144	| P2-3 | Plugin command registration | active — red | `checkPluginCommandClosure` + `tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts` + P2.11 invoke-evidence file at `specs/reviews/p2-11-invoke-evidence.md`. |
   145	| P2-4 | Session hooks + continuity lifecycle | active — red | `.claude/hooks/SessionStart.sh` + `.claude/hooks/SessionEnd.sh` + `checkSessionHooksPresent` + `tests/runner/continuity-lifecycle.test.ts` (create → persist → resume → clear). |
   146	| P2-5 | P2-MODEL-EFFORT landed | active — red | `src/schemas/workflow.ts` v0.3 + `tests/contracts/workflow-model-effort.test.ts` + `checkUnknownModelIds`; slice spec incorporated by reference from `specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md §Slice P2-MODEL-EFFORT`. |
   147	| P2-6 | Spine policy coverage (full-spine `explore`) | active — red | `specs/contracts/explore.md` canonical phase set {Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review, Close} + `checkSpineCoverage` + `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts`. |
   148	| P2-7 | Container isolation | **re-deferred by ADR-0007** | `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` (interim, added in P2.1 ceremony commit) + CLAUDE.md §Phase discipline §Phase 2 + §Hard invariants #1–#4 (policy-layer, unchanged). Nine named trigger conditions at ADR-0007 §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 re-open the gate. |
   149	| P2-8 | Close review (final blocking gate) | active — red | `specs/reviews/phase-2-close-matrix.md` + `specs/reviews/phase-2-close-codex.md` + `specs/reviews/phase-2-operator-product-check.md` + `checkPhase2CloseMatrix`; fails closed if any prior CC is red or supported only by LLM stand-in evidence. |
   150	
   151	**Close condition (no aggregate):** Phase 2 closes when every
   152	`active` criterion is `active — satisfied` AND every `re-deferred`
   153	criterion has valid ADR-covered trigger conditions that have not
   154	fired. See ADR-0007 §Decision.3 for the full forbidden-wording list
   155	(rejects "green-by-redeferral", "7/8 complete", "mostly done", etc.).
   156	
   157	**Inherited product ratchets must be green at close** (see ADR-0007
   158	§Decision.4c): the seven Phase-1.5-inherited ratchets plus the three
   159	Phase-2-added ratchets (`dispatch_realness`, `workflow_parity_fixtures`,
   160	`plugin_surface_present`) are required-green-at-close in addition to
   161	the close criteria; a red inherited ratchet blocks Phase 2 close
   162	independently of CC#P2-N status.
   163	
   164	## Near-term slices — P2.1 through P2.5
   165	
   166	These slices are framed here; each still authors its own commit-body
   167	framing per lane discipline at landing time. P2.1 and P2.2 are
   168	target-agnostic; P2.3 and P2.5 bind to `explore` per the locked
   169	target above; P2.4 is adapter-shaping and target-agnostic in scope.

   195

   197

   199	the path for target-workflow slice P2.3+ to attach as a real command
   200	rather than an orphaned CLI; no earlier slice obsolesces this.
   201

   204	workflow's command placeholder returning "not implemented yet");

   206	plugin.json commands are closure-consistent with
   207	`.claude-plugin/commands/*.md` filenames.

   210	renumber); tests extending `tests/contracts/governance-reform.test.ts`
   211	or a new file; npm run audit green.

   214	workflow works. Rejected because the scaffold is cheap and the audit
   215	floor grows strictly better with it in place before P2.3+ lands.

   217	### P2.3 — `explore` workflow contract + fixture
   218	
   219	**Lane:** Ratchet-Advance (contract-coverage ratchet).
   220	
   221	**Trajectory:** first concrete Phase 2 workflow contract; serves the
   222	one-workflow-parity arc; binds to `explore` per the locked target.
   223

   232

   234	contract-test increment; fixture loadable by the existing runner
   235	(workflow schema validation passes on the new `circuit.json`); audit
   236	green.
   237

   239	infer the contract from adapter pressure. Rejected because contract-
   240	first is the methodology's first pillar (CLAUDE.md §Core methodology);

   243	### P2.4 — Real agent adapter — `agent` (in-process Anthropic subagent)
   244

   248	serves one-workflow-parity by being the first adapter the target
   249	workflow can dispatch to.

   252	chosen at slice time) implementing the `ResolvedAdapter`-to-dispatch
   253	boundary for in-process Anthropic subagent invocation. Runner
   254	integration: `dispatch.started` event carries `resolved_adapter.name
   255	= 'agent'`; receipt and result artifacts written with real agent
   256	output. Dogfood fixture extended with a real-agent smoke test OR a
   257	separate fixture `agent-smoke-0` gated behind an env var so CI can
   258	skip it without disabling the contract test ratchet.
   259	
   260	**Acceptance evidence:** new adapter file; dispatch round-trip test

PROJECT_STATE.md:19:**Last updated:** 2026-04-21 (**Slice 33 (= P2.2 in the plan) — Plugin manifest hardening + command-surface scaffold.** Lane: **Ratchet-Advance**. Three ratchets advanced independently (per CLAUDE.md hard invariant #8): (1) plugin-surface ratchet — `plugin_surface_present` advances to `active — partial` via manifest `commands` array + `.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-run.md` + `.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-explore.md` scaffolds, closing the "/`circuit:*` commands wired" gap for ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 scaffold; (2) audit-coverage ratchet — new `checkPluginCommandClosure` (Check 23) enforces manifest/commands closure, anchor-to-file binding (prevents silent-rename loophole per Codex HIGH 1 fold-in), flat-grammar strictness (rejects paths outside `commands/<basename>.md` per Codex HIGH 2 fold-in), symlink rejection (lstat-based per Codex HIGH 3 fold-in), recursive orphan walk with nested-directory rejection (per Codex MED 4 fold-in), and YAML-aware empty-value detection (rejects `name: ""`, `description: # comment`, empty folded scalars per Codex MED 5 fold-in); (3) contract-test-count ratchet — pinned floor `specs/ratchet-floor.json` advanced 574 → 727 in this same slice to close the moving-window hole per Codex MED 7 fold-in. Trajectory: this slice serves the ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 plugin-command-registration arc (scaffold-at-P2.2, invokability-at-P2.11), which serves the Phase 2 one-workflow-parity arc by giving P2.3+ slices a real command surface to attach to rather than orphaned CLI entries. Failure mode addressed: without the scaffold + enforcement check, P2.3's `explore` contract + fixture would have no plugin-surface binding and the first invocation-evidence slice (P2.11) would either discover the gap late or fabricate an ad-hoc surface; the audit check also prevents silent-rename/swap/symlink-escape regressions that a presence-only check could not catch. Acceptance evidence: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` with commands array (2 anchor entries); `.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-run.md` + `.claude-plugin/commands/circuit-explore.md` (scaffold placeholder bodies explicitly marked "not implemented yet — P2.8/P2.3+P2.5 implementing slices"); `scripts/audit.mjs` Check 23 `checkPluginCommandClosure` (tightened post-fold-in); `scripts/audit.d.mts` declarations for `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` (slice-32 export, previously missing) + `checkPluginCommandClosure`; `tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts` (26 tests: happy-path self-check + 25 negative fixtures + Zod-schema-level live-manifest validation per Codex MED 6 fold-in); `specs/ratchet-floor.json` floor 574 → 727; `specs/reviews/arc-slice-33-plugin-surface-codex.md` (Codex challenger pass, opening 3 HIGH / 4 MED / 0 LOW / 1 META; closing ACCEPT-WITH-FOLD-INS after all HIGH + MED + META folded in); `specs/reviews/adversarial-yield-ledger.md` row appended (class=reversible, rigor_profile=standard); 780 tests green (+26 from P2.1's 754); 22 green / 1 yellow / 0 red audit (yellow is pre-existing framing warning on old docs commits, unrelated). Alternate framing: author the plugin manifest only once the target workflow works (defer P2.2 until after P2.3/P2.4/P2.5 land). Rejected because the scaffold is cheap, the audit floor grows strictly better with it in place before P2.3+ lands, and the absence of a locked plugin-surface ratchet floor would let P2.3+ slices drift on command-registration semantics. Authority: `specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md §Decision.1 CC#P2-3` (plugin command registration close criterion with concrete enforcement binding); `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §P2.2` (slice framing); CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants #6 (cross-model challenger required for ratchet change) + #8 (no aggregate scoring). Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required) — this slice is LLM drafting in the operator session under overnight full-autonomy authorization.

PROJECT_STATE.md:35:1. **What got done.** Phase 2 has an authoritative definition of "done" now. Eight specific things must be true before we can call Phase 2 finished; each one is tracked independently (no "mostly done" scoring); container isolation is explicitly parked with nine conditions that would un-park it; a new audit rule makes every future Phase 2 commit say out loud whether it runs isolated or not. Codex reviewed the whole thing, pushed back on fourteen issues, we folded in every single one.

Malformed-tail policy (27a mining §Decisions #2): **durable-only in v0**; `readEventLogRaw` rejects any malformed line loudly (including the tail) because `appendEvent` is atomic per line — a malformed line indicates crash mid-write, not normal tail, and Phase 2 can add the durable/transient distinction if crash-recovery becomes a real concern. Genesis/prev-event-hash chain (27a mining §Decisions #5): **explicitly not adopted** — RUN-I2 sequence-contiguous + RUN-I4 bootstrap-singleton give enough integrity for dogfood; hash chaining is Phase 2+ scope if replay determinism across rewrites becomes a concern. `npm run verify` clean; `npm run audit` expected: 18 green / 1 yellow (historical ages-out) / 1 red turning green on commit when subject becomes `slice-27c:`. Per `specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md` §Slice 27b. Failure mode addressed: without a mechanical, executable product-surface inventory at HEAD, 27c/27d acceptance has no truthful delta oracle — a placeholder checklist (or a reviewer eyeballing paths) cannot reliably reject surfaces that "exist" as empty files, echo-only npm scripts, or test files with zero `it()` blocks; the same "both stale is green" class that Slice 26b Check 18 closed for status docs must be closed for runtime surfaces too, before dogfood writes real bytes. Acceptance evidence: `scripts/inventory.mjs` ships with 10 deterministic detectors (circuit:run script / plugin manifest / dogfood workflow fixture / src/runtime entrypoint / event writer / snapshot writer / manifest snapshot / runner smoke test / event-log round-trip test / README+PROJECT_STATE+TIER status alignment) + `buildInventory` / `renderReport` / `renderMarkdown` exports; `npm run inventory` writes `reports/product-surface.inventory.json` + `reports/product-surface.inventory.md` (`schema_version: "1"`, `slice: "27b"`, `baseline: true`); at HEAD exactly 1/10 surface is present (`docs.status_alignment: current_slice=27b across README/PROJECT_STATE/TIER`); 9/10 absent rows (runner/writer/reducer/manifest/fixture/smoke/round-trip/plugin-manifest/circuit:run-script) record the expected_evidence 27c+27d will satisfy. Placeholder rejection is structural: empty files (size/non-comment-length threshold), echo/true/noop first-tokens, JSON missing required `name`/`version` / empty-object manifests, artifact rows whose `backing_paths` are only persisted `<run-root>/...` placeholders with no repo-local schema_file, and test files with no `it/test/describe(` block all resolve to `present: false`. Alternate framing: embed detection into `scripts/audit.mjs` as a new Check — rejected because (a) audit emits red/yellow/green tri-state, not structured JSON amenable to cross-commit diff, (b) the plan explicitly names `scripts/inventory.mjs` + `reports/product-surface.inventory.{json,md}` as deliverables and deviating would itself be a ratchet mutation requiring an ADR, (c) delta semantics want stable serialization independent of audit formatting. Second alternate: generate inventory on the fly in 27c/27d tests without committing reports — rejected because an uncommitted baseline cannot be diffed cross-commit and leaves no pre-27c evidence for future auditors. Trajectory check: arc goal served = dogfood-run-0 delta oracle substrate; phase goal served = Phase 1.5 Alpha Proof (D6 "placeholder entries fail" + Product Reality Gate mechanical evidence); earlier-slice impact = 26b's `extractCurrentSliceMarker` + status-header-zone scanning is reused by the inventory's `checkStatusAlignment` detector (not re-implemented), and 27a mined-but-not-adopted spike lessons remain neutral on 27b (inventory records surfaces, not design choices); no earlier slice has made 27b smaller, obsolete, or mis-sequenced. Deliverables: `scripts/inventory.mjs` (ESM Node, ~400 lines, detector helpers exported for tests), `scripts/inventory.d.mts` type declarations, `npm run inventory` script entry in `package.json`, committed baseline `reports/product-surface.inventory.json` + `reports/product-surface.inventory.md`, `tests/contracts/product-surface-inventory.test.ts` (+22 tests across shape, placeholder-rejection, and committed-report-parity describe blocks), new TIER.md row `product_surface_inventory_baseline` (status=enforced, file_paths=scripts/inventory.mjs + reports/product-surface.inventory.{json,md} + tests/contracts/product-surface-inventory.test.ts). Delta rule for downstream slices: 27c MUST advance `runner.entrypoint`/`runner.event_writer`/`runner.snapshot_writer`/`runner.manifest_snapshot`/`tests.event_log_round_trip` from `present: false` to `present: true`, and 27d MUST advance `runner.cli_script`/`plugin.manifest`/`plugin.dogfood_workflow_fixture`/`tests.runner_smoke`; both slices rerun `npm run inventory`, commit the updated reports, and acceptance tests assert the specific id flips. Placeholder rows are structurally rejected at the detector layer — a 27c commit that adds an empty `src/runtime/event-writer.ts` will not register the row as present. No code/schema changes to existing runtime modules. Tests 643 → 665 (+22 new in `tests/contracts/product-surface-inventory.test.ts`). `npm run verify` clean; `npm run audit` green except expected Check 18 yellow until this slice's commit subject (`slice-27b:`) becomes HEAD. Authority graph unchanged — 17 artifacts, 9 contracts bound. Phase 1.5 Alpha Proof remains open. Next unblocked: **Slice 27c — Runtime Boundary Before Dogfood** (Lane: Ratchet-Advance) per `specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md` §Slice 27c. 27c framing must decide manifest hash algorithm (raw persisted bytes vs SHA-256 over RFC 8785 JCS canonical with stricter ADR), declare event-kind subset exercised by dry-run, and keep the Fastest-Falsifier ≤2 boundary (≤2 new `specs/artifacts.json` rows + ≤2 new `src/schemas/*.ts` files) intact — mining surfaced no lesson forcing a third. 27c MUST NOT cherry-pick spike `reducer.ts` (would regress Slice 26a Snapshot binding) or add `ReducerFingerprint` (trips ≤2 schema boundary). After 27c lands → 27d dogfood-run-0 alpha product proof. **Prior 2026-04-20 (commit 1cc9849):** Slice 27a landing — Spike/kernel-replay mining inventory. Lane: **Discovery**. 60-minute wall-clock, read-only mining of `spike/kernel-replay` (tip `c555894`, 3 commits / 18 files / +1883 lines beyond `main`) per `specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md` §Slice 27a. Spike disposition remains **abandoned-and-mined** per `specs/plans/arc-remediation-plan-codex.md` §Q1 — no branch merge, no code cherry-picks, no architecture adoption by inertia, no product-evidence claim. Inventory authored at `specs/reviews/slice-27a-spike-mining.md` (filename intentionally lacks the `-codex.md` suffix so `tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts::listReviewFiles()` does not classify it as a challenger review record; `type: discovery`, not `review`). Lessons harvested across three axes. **Event model:** (a) spike narrowed to 4-kind discriminated union (`run.bootstrapped | step.completed | gate.evaluated | run.closed`) vs production's 11-kind enum — 27c tests may exercise a subset but must not redraw the enum; (b) uniform base shape `{run_id, event_id, seq, prev_event_hash}` matches production `Base` extract pattern; (c) `ReducerFingerprint` primitive novel vs production — **out of scope for 27c** (would trip fastest-falsifier ≤2 boundary); (d) terminal set encoded as `TERMINAL_KINDS` data + `isTerminal(event)` predicate. **Event-store:** (e) append-only is a **writer contract**, not a schema property — `atomicAppendLine` via `openSync('a') + writeSync`; (f) malformed-tail durable vs transient distinction (only last line may be transiently malformed, others require operator repair); (g) newline hygiene at the writer (`appendRaw` rejects `\n`-containing lines); (h) `safeJoin` path-safety primitive + `PathSafetyError` (module file, not a schema file, so does not count against fastest-falsifier tripwire); (i) tmp-rename `atomicWrite` + best-effort `0o444` `writeOnceReadOnly` for `manifest.json`. **Reducer:** (j) `reduce = fold(step)` decomposition with bootstrap-requires-null-prior / non-bootstrap-requires-non-null-prior guards; (k) spike `Snapshot` shape **DIFFERS from production** — production has richer `SnapshotStatus`/`StepStatus`/`StepState` machinery artifact-bound to `<run-root>/state.json` by Slice 26a; **this is the single most tempting cherry-pick to refuse** (inheriting spike reducer silently regresses the Slice 26a artifact binding); (l) three purity properties directly testable — repeatable, invariant under `Date.now`/`Math.random` perturbation, step-decomposable (`reduce([...H, e]) === step(reduce(H), e)`). **Manifest:** (m) spike chose **SHA-256 over RFC 8785 JCS canonical encoding** for manifest hashing — plan deliverable language allows either raw-persisted-bytes match OR a stricter ADR for JCS; **27c must decide at framing**; (n) genesis `prev_event_hash` is `sha256(canonical({run_id, kernel_version, trace_contract_version}))` not zero/empty — only relevant if 27c adopts hash chaining, which plan deliverables do NOT include; (o) `hashEvent` self-excludes `prev_event_hash` to avoid self-reference — only relevant if hash chaining adopted. **History-validator:** (p) three-layer pipeline — structural / hash-chain / manifest — each separately testable (keep orthogonal in 27c); (q) 13 named `TraceErrorCode` values inform a denser runtime-boundary error taxonomy. **Test ergonomics:** (r) `buildTrace` + `rechain` helper pattern makes negative property tests tractable. Forbidden cherry-picks enumerated explicitly in §Forbidden cherry-picks: spike `reducer.ts` implementation (would regress Slice 26a Snapshot binding), `ReducerFingerprint` primitive (trips fastest-falsifier ≤2 schema-file boundary), `kernel_version`/`trace_contract_version` event-level fields, the 4-kind narrow `Event` union, and `RunTrace`/`TerminalEvent`/`ProjectionFunction` domain terms (production `specs/domain.md` uses `RunLog` and `Snapshot`). §Decisions 27c must make at framing enumerates five specific forks informed by mining: (1) manifest hash algorithm raw-bytes vs JCS canonical (ADR required if JCS); (2) malformed-tail policy include or defer; (3) path-safety primitive placement (likely `src/runtime/`); (4) subset of 11-kind event enum exercised by dry-run; (5) explicit no-hash-chain confirmation. Fastest-Falsifier ≤2 boundary re-check at 27c framing: still clear — mining surfaced no lesson forcing a third `specs/artifacts.json` row or third `src/schemas/*.ts` file (candidates `ReducerFingerprint`, `EventHash`, canonical-JSON helpers, path-safety primitives are all out-of-scope, module-files-not-schema-files, or deferred). **Discovery lane merge semantics:** throwaway-or-promotion — this slice produces a prose artifact only; no ratchet changes, no contract authorship, no test file additions, no code changes. New audit dimension `checkInvariantLedger()` added to `scripts/audit.mjs` per MED 10. Also upgraded 6 `describe`/`it` titles for ADAPTER-I8 + ADAPTER-I11 to cite invariant ids (tests existed; titles omitted them). Framing audit flags 78a4bc3 as missing `alternate framing:` literal colon (body has `Alternate framing (Nguyen 2024 anchoring defense):` with parenthetical before colon; audit regex `/alternate framing:/i` requires no-interstitial form). Future slice commits should use the literal `Alternate framing:` shape. Gates: 509/509 tests (+20 new), audit 10 green / 1 yellow (framing regex nit on 78a4bc3) / 0 red. CLAUDE.md unchanged at 183 lines. Next unblocked: Slice 25a — Methodology artifact portability (FUP-1 closure + ADR-0001 addendum).) **Follow-up 2026-04-20 prior:** Arc-progress adversarial Codex review landed at `specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md`. Target: `circuit-next-full-arc-to-date` at commit 682704d. Reviewer: GPT-5 Codex via /codex skill (CHALLENGER-I5). 13 objections returned: 4 HIGH / 6 MED / 3 LOW plus meta. Verdict REJECT pending HIGH fold-ins. New correlated-miss instance identified (HIGH #1): `run.projection` artifact row at `specs/artifacts.json:120-138` binds persisted backing path `<circuit-next-run-root>/state.json` to the wrapper schema `RunProjection = { log, snapshot }` instead of the standalone `Snapshot` shape at `src/schemas/snapshot.ts:31-46` that a persisted `state.json` actually carries. `src/schemas/snapshot.ts` is allowlisted in `scripts/audit.mjs:218-221` as a `shared-primitive` with reason "snapshot shape embedded in run.projection", preserving the wrong-shape binding from detection. Distinct from the earlier `config.md` correlated-miss (row missing entirely): this is "row exists + classified + contract-bound + reciprocated + audit-green, but names the wrong runtime shape." Operator response at `arc-progress-codex.md` dispositions all 13 — 11 incorporable in arc, 1 scoped-forward to Phase 2 entry (HIGH #2 manifest byte-match decision — Slice 30), 1 scoped to v0.2 (LOW #2 review-frontmatter value vocabulary). Zero rejected. Revised Phase 1 close arc plan adds two intra-arc fold-in slices and splits original Slice 28: Slice 25 (expanded with MED #2 + MED #5) → Slice 26 (config.md) → Slice 26a (NEW: run.snapshot artifact split, HIGH #1) → Slice 27 (workflow.md v0.2) → Slice 28a (audit/doc hardening batch: README + citation + phase-consistency + trajectory + stale-prose + pending-expiry + LOW #1 + LOW #3 + MED #9) → Slice 28b (NEW: behavioral enforcement matrix, MED #4) → final phase-transition ceremony slice (HIGH #10 from Phase 1 close review). Phase 2 entry slices 29-31 unchanged in number; Slice 30 now also homes HIGH #2. specs/reviews/arc-slice-33-plugin-surface-codex.md:32:  - read specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md §Decision.1 CC#P2-3 (binding source)
specs/reviews/arc-slice-33-plugin-surface-codex.md:101:**Remediation:** Run `npm run audit` and `npm run verify` in the operator environment before ceremony, and include the exact green results in the landing evidence. The commit body still needs the Phase 2 protected-path posture string: `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)`, plus citations to ADR-0007 CC#P2-3 and `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md §P2.2`.
specs/contracts/explore.md:27:ADR-0007 CC#P2-1 (operator decision 2026-04-21 adopting Codex
specs/contracts/explore.md:92:## Canonical phase set (ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 binding) — and the title-to-canonical translation
specs/contracts/explore.md:94:ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 names the explore canonical phase set using
specs/contracts/explore.md:103:follows. The translation is the authoritative reading of CC#P2-6 for
specs/contracts/explore.md:104:v0.1 — any future ADR amending CC#P2-6 or this translation must clear
specs/contracts/explore.md:326:   `specs/contracts/review.md` replaces it as CC#P2-1's binding.
specs/contracts/explore.md:370:  CC#P2-1` (one-workflow parity — `explore` target)
specs/contracts/explore.md:372:  CC#P2-6` (spine policy coverage — canonical set
specs/ratchet-floor.json:8:  "notes": "Pinned ratchet floors that must not regress. The contract_test_count metric is the static declaration count produced by scripts/audit.mjs::countTests (matches /^\\s*(it|test)\\(/gm across git-tracked tests/**/*.test.* files), not the vitest runtime total; the audit uses the static count so the ratchet is deterministic without shelling into vitest. Advancement is an explicit commit action; HEAD~1 moving-window comparison is insufficient (docs-only commits between regression and audit would hide the regression). Close gates depend on this file, not on HEAD~1 alone. See specs/plans/phase-1-close-revised.md §Slice 26b and ADR-0002 Addendum A. Initial floor 574 pinned post-Slice-26b (post-Codex-fold-in) HEAD static count — per Codex HIGH-1 fold-in on the Slice 26b diff, pinning below the post-slice count leaves the moving-window hole open (trivial revert of the new test file drops HEAD back to the floor and Check 19 stays green). Slice 33 (P2.2) adds tests/contracts/plugin-surface.test.ts (+26 static test declarations for plugin-command-closure enforcement per ADR-0007 CC#P2-3); floor advanced from 574 → 727 in the same Ratchet-Advance slice, closing the moving-window hole per Codex P2.2 MED 7 fold-in. Metadata fields (schema_version, last_advanced_at, last_advanced_in_slice) are audit-enforced via validatePinnedRatchetFloorData; floor zero or negative is rejected (Codex HIGH-2 fold-in). Future slices raise the floor only as an explicit ratchet-advance act."
specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:46:### 4. HIGH — `Synthesize` mapped to canonical `act` is an undeclared amendment to CC#P2-6
specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:48:ADR-0007 CC#P2-6 binds the first workflow's canonical set in human phase terms as Frame -> Analyze -> Synthesize -> Review -> Close. The contract silently translates Synthesize to canonical `act`, while omitting canonical `plan`. That may be the right final answer, but the contract does not prove it. In an investigation workflow, synthesis often means building candidate conclusions, tradeoffs, or a plan of attack; mapping it to `act` instead of `plan` changes what `plan` omission means under PHASE-I4 partial-spine semantics.
specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:108:ADR-0007 CC#P2-7 requires the commit body for this slice to contain `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)` or an allowed alternative. That cannot be validated from the working tree review files alone. If this review is folded into the same slice, the final commit still needs the exact isolation citation or Check 22 will red the slice regardless of the contract content.
specs/reviews/explore-md-v0.1-codex.md:110:**Remediation:** Include `Isolation: policy-compliant (no implementer separation required)` in the slice commit body unless the operator intentionally invokes the ADR-0007 re-deferred wording or Break-Glass lane. **Disposition:** Rejected pending fold-in.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:33:ADR-0007 says it does not cite ADR-0006 as precedent, but the current text does exactly that in several permission-granting places. The frontmatter calls ADR-0006 a "CC#14 retarget precedent pattern," Context says ADR-0007 installs a firewall "analogous to ADR-0006 §5," and the CC#P2-7 rationale says "ADR-0006 precedent shows that re-deferral by ADR ... is a legitimate pattern." That last sentence is a direct clause-7 violation: it uses ADR-0006's existence and retarget pattern as permission for a similar move. Later disclaimers do not undo the earlier precedent claim.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:35:**Remediation:** Remove all permission-granting ADR-0006 language. ADR-0007 may cite ADR-0006 only as historical context for Phase 1.5 authority or as a negative constraint: "ADR-0006 §5 forbids citing ADR-0006 as precedent; ADR-0007 therefore re-authenticates its own basis." Replace the CC#P2-7 rationale bullet with the independent basis itself, not with an analogy to ADR-0006.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:37:### 2. HIGH — CC#P2-7 re-deferral hollows container isolation into policy-only theater
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:39:CLAUDE.md says Phase 2 implementers run in a container with distinct UID and protected mounts, while hard invariants #1-#4 remain non-negotiable except through methodology reopening. ADR-0007 says Phase 2 close does not require container isolation, no audit check enforces CC#P2-7, and the invariants remain "policy (not Phase-2-close-gating)." That is not a preserved invariant in practice; it is a standing permission to run all P2.1-P2.11 work unisolated unless a slice happens to self-identify as "claiming isolation." The Break-Glass sentence does not rescue this, because the ADR defines normal Phase 2 work as not violating the invariants.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:41:**Remediation:** Carry weaker-evidence wording openly: "ADR-0007 weakens Phase 2 close by accepting unisolated implementer execution for Phase 2 close." Add an interim enforcement surface, such as a required per-slice isolation status line and an audit check rejecting any Phase 2 slice that both writes protected surfaces and omits either container evidence or an explicit CC#P2-7 re-deferral citation. If the intent is to amend CLAUDE.md's Phase 2 container sentence, say so and route it as a methodology amendment instead of burying it in a close-criteria ADR.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:43:### 3. HIGH — The CC#P2-7 trigger list misses obvious isolation-adjacent Phase 2 scenarios
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:49:### 4. HIGH — CC#P2-8 can pass by review-artifact presence without proving CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-7 are closed
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:51:CC#P2-8's enforcement is a set of files plus a Check-21-analog artifact-presence audit. It does not require the close review to enumerate CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-7, cite their exact evidence, mark each as satisfied/not satisfied, or fail closed if any criterion is red, re-deferred, waived, or satisfied by weaker evidence. Worse, it explicitly allows LLM stand-in sections until a genuine non-LLM signal is obtainable, which can recreate the ADR-0006 weaker-evidence pattern at Phase 2 close while still satisfying artifact presence.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:53:**Remediation:** Define CC#P2-8 as the final blocking review over a close matrix: every CC#P2-N must have an evidence path, audit/test result, commit SHA, and status. The audit check should reject a Phase 2 close claim if any prior criterion is missing, red, re-deferred without an active ADR, or supported only by stand-in evidence where the criterion requires operator/non-LLM evidence. Artifact presence alone is not enough.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:57:ADR-0007 repeatedly treats "landing of slice X" as enforcement. That is weaker than a testable binding. CC#P2-5 says the audit check will be "named explicitly in the slice commit body," but names no check. CC#P2-8 depends on P2-CLOSE-REVIEW, which is not yet in the plan. CC#P2-2 allows a real-agent smoke test to be skipped in CI without requiring a durable local transcript or receipt artifact. CC#P2-3 says P2.2 is partial and P2.11 is full, but the audit binding only checks manifest/markdown closure, not Claude Code invokability. These can all be satisfied by weaker evidence without tripping the firewall.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:59:**Remediation:** Replace slice-name enforcement with concrete acceptance artifacts. For each CC#P2-N, name the required test file or audit function, the required fixture/golden path, the required frontmatter fields if a review artifact is involved, and the non-substitutable failure condition. Where the exact test name is not known yet, mark the criterion "not fully locked" or require the later slice to amend ADR-0007 before claiming closure.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:61:### 6. HIGH — CC#P2-6 weakens the plan's `explore` spine and silently resolves Open Question #5
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:65:**Remediation:** Either set CC#P2-6's `explore` canonical phase set to Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review, Close, or explicitly amend Open Question #5 and P2.3 to say Phase 2 parity starts with the three-phase subset. If the latter is chosen, carry weaker-evidence wording because the ADR is lowering the plan's stated minimum.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:71:**Remediation:** Put the locked CC#P2-1 through CC#P2-8 identifiers, target workflow, re-deferral status, and short binding summary directly in `specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md`. ADR-0007 can remain authoritative on conflicts, but the plan must carry enough in-place content that a slice author cannot miss the locked semantics.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:75:ADR-0007 says a retarget requires amending the ADR and clearing the firewall, but it does not say what has to change. A fallback to `review` affects CC#P2-1 goldens, CC#P2-6 canonical phase coverage, P2.3 contract authorship, P2.5 fixture paths, artifact-shape expectations, and possibly whether already-landed `explore` work remains a target, a discarded partial, or a second-workflow candidate. Without that payload, "amend the ADR" can degrade into a target-name swap.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:79:### 9. MED — Aggregate-scoring firewall leaves status-color loopholes and legitimizes "green-by-redeferral"
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:81:The no-aggregate rule is strong in intent, but the text still uses "partial-green," "takes it to green," "all eight close criteria green," and the especially dangerous "CC#P2-7 is trivially green-by-redeferral." A re-deferred criterion should not be green; it should be `re-deferred`, `not applicable to Phase 2 close`, or `active`. Treating it as green invites exactly the scalar dashboards the ADR is trying to ban.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:83:**Remediation:** Reserve green/red status for active criteria with executable evidence. Use `re-deferred` for CC#P2-7 and ban "green-by-redeferral," "all but," "mostly done," "only N remaining," "complete except," and "near close" alongside the existing forbidden wording. Make the close condition "all active close criteria satisfied and all re-deferred criteria have valid ADR trigger coverage," not "all eight green."
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:93:The plan names P2.8 router/classifier, P2.10 artifact schema set, status/tier freshness ratchets, golden hashing, and continuity lifecycle as Phase 2 work, but ADR-0007 does not make them close gates or explicitly demote them from close. CC#P2-1 can pass by byte-shape golden without artifact schemas. CC#P2-4 can pass by hook presence plus seeded resume without proving the continuity-record lifecycle. Product ratchets like `status_docs_current` and `tier_claims_current` "continue" but are not required at close.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:99:CC#P2-2 excludes a mock adapter, but "externally-produced artifact byte-shape" is not enough to prove a genuine request/receipt/result round-trip. A checked-in artifact, manually produced output, or deterministic script could satisfy byte-shape unless the event log records adapter provenance and the test asserts it. The CI skip also creates a path where the only always-on ratchet is contract shape, not real dispatch.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:101:**Remediation:** Require a durable dispatch transcript or event-log proof: adapter id, non-dry-run flag, request payload hash, receipt id, result artifact hash, and a test asserting the reducer/result-writer consumed that sequence. If CI skips live invocation, require a local smoke artifact path and audit-visible timestamp/commit reference before CC#P2-2 can close.
specs/reviews/adr-0007-codex.md:117:ADR-0007 is directionally useful, but it cannot land as-is. The minimum acceptable fold-in is: remove ADR-0006 precedent leakage, make CC#P2-7's weakening honest and trigger-complete, turn slice-name bindings into concrete evidence bindings, close the CC#P2-8 circularity, fix the `explore` spine mismatch, and make the plan amendment genuinely in-place.
specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:135:is independently trackable with status values `active — satisfied`,
specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:136:`active — red`, or `re-deferred` (no aggregate scoring — CLAUDE.md

specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:147:| P2-6 | Spine policy coverage (full-spine `explore`) | active — red | `specs/contracts/explore.md` canonical phase set {Frame, Analyze, Synthesize, Review, Close} + `checkSpineCoverage` + `tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts`. |
specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:148:| P2-7 | Container isolation | **re-deferred by ADR-0007** | `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` (interim, added in P2.1 ceremony commit) + CLAUDE.md §Phase discipline §Phase 2 + §Hard invariants #1–#4 (policy-layer, unchanged). Nine named trigger conditions at ADR-0007 §Decision.1 CC#P2-7 re-open the gate. |
specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:149:| P2-8 | Close review (final blocking gate) | active — red | `specs/reviews/phase-2-close-matrix.md` + `specs/reviews/phase-2-close-codex.md` + `specs/reviews/phase-2-operator-product-check.md` + `checkPhase2CloseMatrix`; fails closed if any prior CC is red or supported only by LLM stand-in evidence. |
specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:152:`active` criterion is `active — satisfied` AND every `re-deferred`

specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:162:independently of CC#P2-N status.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   455	/**
   456	 * Slice 25 — invariant ledger integrity audit dimension. Loads
   457	 * specs/invariants.json and reports load-ability, duplicate detection,
   458	 * and enforcement_state distribution. The full set-equality + binding-ref
   459	 * enforcement lives in tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts (which
   460	 * runs as part of `npm run verify`); this audit dimension surfaces ledger
   461	 * presence at a glance and fails red if the ledger is missing or
   462	 * structurally broken. Per Codex challenger MED 10 fold-in.
   463	 */
   464	// Slice 25a — Methodology artifact portability (FUP-1, ADR-0001 addendum).
   465	// Absolute symlinks under specs/ bind the repo's authority surface to the
   466	// author's host filesystem; on any clone they resolve to broken paths and the
   467	// methodology artifacts stop being readable. Portability requires that every
   468	// symlink under specs/ be either (a) absolute → rejected, or (b) relative and
   469	// resolving to a path still inside the repo containment boundary. A relative
   470	// link like `../../../../tmp/host-local` is syntactically relative but escapes
   471	// the repo, so it fails portability the same way an absolute target does.
   472	//
   473	// Exported so tests/contracts/specs-portability.test.ts can exercise the guard
   474	// against a constructed fixture without shelling out to the audit CLI.
   475	// Each violation carries a `reason` field: 'absolute' or 'escapes-repo'.
   476	//
   477	// Scope note (Codex MED #7): the scan walks the working tree, not the git
   478	// index. `.gitignore`-ed detritus (.DS_Store, build output) is filtered by
   479	// directory-name so local noise does not red the audit. A future regression
   480	// where an ignored absolute symlink slips in would still fail at clone time
   481	// on any machine that does not have the same ignored artifact — acceptable
   482	// because the audit is host-local signal, not distributed proof.
   483	const IGNORED_DIRS_FOR_PORTABILITY = new Set(['node_modules', '.git', 'dist', 'coverage']);
   484	const IGNORED_BASENAMES_FOR_PORTABILITY = new Set(['.DS_Store']);
   485	
   486	export function findAbsoluteSymlinks(rootDir, containmentRoot) {
   487	  const containment = containmentRoot ?? rootDir;
   488	  const violations = [];
   489	  if (!existsSync(rootDir)) return violations;
   490	  const walk = (dir) => {
   491	    for (const entry of readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
   492	      if (IGNORED_DIRS_FOR_PORTABILITY.has(entry.name)) continue;
   493	      if (IGNORED_BASENAMES_FOR_PORTABILITY.has(entry.name)) continue;
   494	      const full = join(dir, entry.name);
   495	      let stat;
   496	      try {
   497	        stat = lstatSync(full);
   498	      } catch {
   499	        continue;
   500	      }
   501	      if (stat.isSymbolicLink()) {
   502	        const target = readlinkSync(full);
   503	        if (isAbsolute(target)) {
   504	          violations.push({ path: full, target, reason: 'absolute' });
   505	          continue;
   506	        }
   507	        // Relative-target containment check (Codex HIGH #2). Resolve the link
   508	        // against its parent directory and assert the result stays inside the
   509	        // containment root. `path.relative` returning a `..`-prefixed value
   510	        // means the resolved path escapes.
   511	        const resolvedTarget = resolve(dir, target);
   512	        const rel = relative(containment, resolvedTarget);
   513	        if (rel === '' || rel === '.' || (!rel.startsWith('..') && !isAbsolute(rel))) {
   514	          // Target stays within containment — portable.
   515	        } else {
   516	          violations.push({ path: full, target, reason: 'escapes-repo' });
   517	        }
   518	      } else if (entry.isDirectory()) {
   519	        walk(full);
   520	      }
   521	    }
   522	  };
   523	  walk(rootDir);
   524	  return violations;
   525	}
   526	
   527	function checkSpecsPortability() {
   528	  const specsDir = join(REPO_ROOT, 'specs');
   529	  const violations = findAbsoluteSymlinks(specsDir, REPO_ROOT);
   530	  if (violations.length === 0) {
   531	    return {
   532	      level: 'green',
   533	      detail: 'No absolute or repo-escaping symlinks under specs/ (ADR-0001 portability addendum).',
   534	    };
   535	  }
   536	  const rels = violations.map(
   537	    (v) => `${v.path.slice(REPO_ROOT.length + 1)} → ${v.target} [${v.reason}]`,
   538	  );
   539	  return {
   540	    level: 'red',
   541	    detail: `${violations.length} non-portable symlink(s) under specs/: ${rels.join('; ')}`,
   542	  };
   543	}

codex
Two composition cracks are emerging as more than style: the explore fixture is “act” only at the phase-canonical label, while runtime dispatch authority is step-kind driven; and `explore.result` now shares the exact same `artifacts/result.json` path as the existing `run.result`. I’m doing one more pass for deferral/ratchet/audit escape hatches before I write the objection list.

**Opening Verdict: REJECT-PENDING-FOLD-INS**

The aggregate is not load-bearing enough for P2.4 to touch runtime dispatch authority yet. The per-slice work is mostly honest, but the composition has several “green locally, ambiguous globally” failures around dispatch semantics, result ownership, deferred properties, and CC#P2-7 trigger enforcement.

**HIGH — `Synthesize → act` is only a label; runtime dispatch sees no dispatch step**

Evidence: `explore.md` maps Synthesize to canonical `act` at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:107), and the fixture honors that with `canonical: "act"` at [.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json:35). But the actual synthesize step is `executor: "orchestrator", kind: "synthesis"` at [.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json:117), while runtime dispatch authority is represented by `kind: "dispatch"` / `executor: "worker"` in [src/schemas/step.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/schemas/step.ts:60). P2.5 explicitly expects explore to run through the `agent` adapter at [specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/plans/phase-2-implementation.md:277).

Impact: P2.4 can land a real adapter without the target `explore` workflow dispatching to it. Future runtime sees `synthesis`, not “Synthesize”; canonical `act` does not imply adapter invocation.

Fix hint: before P2.4, decide whether explore’s work-producing phases become `dispatch` steps, or introduce a first-class “orchestrator synthesis uses adapter” contract. Then add an audit/test binding that the target explore fixture exercises the adapter path.

**HIGH — CC#P2-2 requires event kinds that do not exist**

Evidence: ADR-0007 requires a durable transcript containing `dispatch.request`, `dispatch.receipt`, and `dispatch.result` at [specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md:131). The event schema only defines `dispatch.started` and `dispatch.completed` at [src/schemas/event.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/schemas/event.ts:86) and [src/schemas/event.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/schemas/event.ts:97), and the union has no request/receipt/result variants at [src/schemas/event.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/schemas/event.ts:139). The run contract likewise only pairs started/completed at [specs/contracts/run.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/run.md:256).

Impact: P2.4’s promised close evidence cannot be produced without widening the event schema/run contract inside the adapter slice. That is a hidden prerequisite, not just implementation detail.

Fix hint: fold in a dispatch-transcript schema slice first, or explicitly make P2.4 include event contract changes plus tests for request/receipt/result artifacts.

**HIGH — CC#P2-7 trigger enforcement is still citation-only**

Evidence: ADR trigger #6 reopens isolation when an adapter/hook/workflow step can write protected repo surfaces, including a P2.4 `agent` adapter with file-write tool use at [specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/adrs/ADR-0007-phase-2-close-criteria.md:333). But `checkPhase2SliceIsolationCitation` only searches commit bodies for two posture strings or Break-Glass at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2347), [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2375).

Impact: P2.4 could introduce write-capable dispatch authority, include `Isolation: policy-compliant...`, and stay green even though a named trigger fired.

Fix hint: add a trigger-aware check before P2.4, or constrain `agent` adapter v0 to no repo-write tool capability and require the commit to cite that capability boundary.

**HIGH — `explore.result` collides with existing `run.result` ownership**

Evidence: `run.result` owns `<circuit-next-run-root>/artifacts/result.json` at [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:202), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:216), and `result-writer.ts` says it is the only path by which `result.json` comes into being at [src/runtime/result-writer.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/runtime/result-writer.ts:5). Slice 34 adds `explore.result` to the same path at [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:688), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:699), and the explore close step writes `artifacts/result.json` with schema `explore.result@v1` at [.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/.claude-plugin/skills/explore/circuit.json:160).

Impact: P2.5 has two authorities for one file: step artifact `explore.result@v1` and runtime `RunResult`. One will overwrite or counterfeit the other.

Fix hint: either make `explore.result` a payload field/reference inside `run.result`, or move the workflow-specific artifact to a distinct path like `artifacts/explore-result.json`. Add a duplicate backing-path check unless an explicit alias relation exists.

**HIGH — EXPLORE-I1 says “runtime MUST reject,” but enforcement is audit-only**

Evidence: The contract says runtime must reject invalid explore-kind workflows at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:161), then names Check 24 as enforcement at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:180). Check 24 hand-reads JSON phase sets and omits at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2777), [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2783), not the runtime schema. Its “valid explore fixture” test even uses `steps: []` at [tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts:34) and still expects green at [tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts:70). Runtime fixture loading currently calls only `Workflow.parse` at [src/cli/dogfood.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/cli/dogfood.ts:125).

Impact: Check 24 can pass invalid runtime shapes and does not make runtime reject explore-specific violations. This also lets CC#P2-6 look greener than it is.

Fix hint: extract a reusable `validateWorkflowKindPolicy(workflow)` used by runtime and audit, and make Check 24 parse via `Workflow.safeParse` before kind-specific checks.

**MED — Deferred explore properties are not machine-tied to P2.5**

Evidence: deferred properties are promised to gain bindings when P2.5 lands at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:203), and reopen condition #5 says P2.5 without promotion reopens the contract at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:343). But the ledger rows just say `target_slice: 38` at [specs/invariants.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/invariants.json:1290), [specs/invariants.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/invariants.json:1311). The invariant-ledger test only checks that phase2-property entries have `target_slice` and `reopen_condition` at [tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts:233), and audit only reports counts at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:545).

Impact: P2.5 could land earlier/later with these still deferred, and no machine gate would fail.

Fix hint: add an audit check keyed to P2.5 evidence presence (`explore-e2e-parity.test.ts` or golden dir), not projected slice number.

**MED — Plugin command closure cannot prevent semantic drift**

Evidence: Check 23 validates manifest/file closure, YAML fields, body non-empty, anchors, and symlinks at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2430) through [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2707). It does not validate command semantics against `explore.md`. The manifest still says the explore contract is “to be authored at slice P2.3” at [.claude-plugin/plugin.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:13).

Impact: P2.8/P2.11 can silently over-spec or drift command behavior while Check 23 stays green.

Fix hint: command docs should be thin routing surfaces with audited contract pointers, or any normative command prose should be diff-checked against the workflow contract.

**MED — New artifact rows encode “no schema” as empty string, contrary to the artifact spec**

Evidence: `artifacts.md` says `schema_file` is a path or `null` at [specs/artifacts.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.md:141). The five explore rows use `""` at [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:598), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:621), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:646), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:669), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:694). Audit skips schema checks on falsy `schema_file` at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:1353).

Impact: deferred schemas are represented by an undocumented sentinel that silently escapes schema-export enforcement.

Fix hint: convert to `null`; enforce `schema_file === null || non-empty path`.

**LOW — Ratchet floor is useful, but it is being narrated as stronger than it is**

Evidence: countTests is a static declaration count at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:398), [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:409). The current floor is 740 at [specs/ratchet-floor.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/ratchet-floor.json:4), but the notes only explain the Slice 33 574→727 jump, not the Slice 34 727→740 jump, at [specs/ratchet-floor.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/ratchet-floor.json:8). Several spine-coverage tests count audit-helper fixtures that are not valid workflows, e.g. `steps: []` at [tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts:64).

Impact: the floor protects against test deletion, but not against scaffold tests being counted like invariant tests.

Fix hint: add a coverage-ledger note per floor advance tying new test declarations to specific invariant/check ids.

**LOW — Artifact companion doc is stale after the five explore rows**

Evidence: `artifacts.md` still says the current graph has 17 artifacts at [specs/artifacts.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.md:110), while `artifacts.json` now includes five `explore.*` rows beginning at [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:592).

Impact: not a runtime blocker, but it weakens the human-readable authority graph exactly where P2.4 needs clear artifact ownership.

Fix hint: update the companion doc or explicitly mark the roll-up as stale with current expected count.

**META Observations**

I did not find a CC#P2-1..P2-8 row with no named binding; the issue is binding strength, not missing governance. The aggregate failures are mostly boundary failures between layers that passed individually: phase title vs step dispatch, audit-time vs runtime enforcement, workflow result vs run result, and citation posture vs capability trigger. I did not run `npm run verify`; this was a static adversarial composition review with file/line evidence.

**Closing**

P2.1+P2.2+P2.3 is not yet load-bearing enough for P2.4 to add real dispatch authority. A fold-in slice should come first to settle the dispatch transcript event model, make explore’s adapter-use semantics explicit, resolve `explore.result` vs `run.result`, and harden CC#P2-7 trigger enforcement. After that, P2.4 can touch `src/runtime/adapters/agent.ts` without smuggling architectural decisions into the first privileged runtime slice.

Fix hint: either make `explore.result` a payload field/reference inside `run.result`, or move the workflow-specific artifact to a distinct path like `artifacts/explore-result.json`. Add a duplicate backing-path check unless an explicit alias relation exists.

**HIGH — EXPLORE-I1 says “runtime MUST reject,” but enforcement is audit-only**

Evidence: The contract says runtime must reject invalid explore-kind workflows at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:161), then names Check 24 as enforcement at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:180). Check 24 hand-reads JSON phase sets and omits at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2777), [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2783), not the runtime schema. Its “valid explore fixture” test even uses `steps: []` at [tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts:34) and still expects green at [tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts:70). Runtime fixture loading currently calls only `Workflow.parse` at [src/cli/dogfood.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/src/cli/dogfood.ts:125).

Impact: Check 24 can pass invalid runtime shapes and does not make runtime reject explore-specific violations. This also lets CC#P2-6 look greener than it is.

Fix hint: extract a reusable `validateWorkflowKindPolicy(workflow)` used by runtime and audit, and make Check 24 parse via `Workflow.safeParse` before kind-specific checks.

**MED — Deferred explore properties are not machine-tied to P2.5**

Evidence: deferred properties are promised to gain bindings when P2.5 lands at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:203), and reopen condition #5 says P2.5 without promotion reopens the contract at [specs/contracts/explore.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/contracts/explore.md:343). But the ledger rows just say `target_slice: 38` at [specs/invariants.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/invariants.json:1290), [specs/invariants.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/invariants.json:1311). The invariant-ledger test only checks that phase2-property entries have `target_slice` and `reopen_condition` at [tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/invariant-ledger.test.ts:233), and audit only reports counts at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:545).

Impact: P2.5 could land earlier/later with these still deferred, and no machine gate would fail.

Fix hint: add an audit check keyed to P2.5 evidence presence (`explore-e2e-parity.test.ts` or golden dir), not projected slice number.

**MED — Plugin command closure cannot prevent semantic drift**

Evidence: Check 23 validates manifest/file closure, YAML fields, body non-empty, anchors, and symlinks at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2430) through [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:2707). It does not validate command semantics against `explore.md`. The manifest still says the explore contract is “to be authored at slice P2.3” at [.claude-plugin/plugin.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:13).

Impact: P2.8/P2.11 can silently over-spec or drift command behavior while Check 23 stays green.

Fix hint: command docs should be thin routing surfaces with audited contract pointers, or any normative command prose should be diff-checked against the workflow contract.

**MED — New artifact rows encode “no schema” as empty string, contrary to the artifact spec**

Evidence: `artifacts.md` says `schema_file` is a path or `null` at [specs/artifacts.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.md:141). The five explore rows use `""` at [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:598), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:621), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:646), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:669), [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:694). Audit skips schema checks on falsy `schema_file` at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:1353).

Impact: deferred schemas are represented by an undocumented sentinel that silently escapes schema-export enforcement.

Fix hint: convert to `null`; enforce `schema_file === null || non-empty path`.

**LOW — Ratchet floor is useful, but it is being narrated as stronger than it is**

Evidence: countTests is a static declaration count at [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:398), [scripts/audit.mjs](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/scripts/audit.mjs:409). The current floor is 740 at [specs/ratchet-floor.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/ratchet-floor.json:4), but the notes only explain the Slice 33 574→727 jump, not the Slice 34 727→740 jump, at [specs/ratchet-floor.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/ratchet-floor.json:8). Several spine-coverage tests count audit-helper fixtures that are not valid workflows, e.g. `steps: []` at [tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/tests/contracts/spine-coverage.test.ts:64).

Impact: the floor protects against test deletion, but not against scaffold tests being counted like invariant tests.

Fix hint: add a coverage-ledger note per floor advance tying new test declarations to specific invariant/check ids.

**LOW — Artifact companion doc is stale after the five explore rows**

Evidence: `artifacts.md` still says the current graph has 17 artifacts at [specs/artifacts.md](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.md:110), while `artifacts.json` now includes five `explore.*` rows beginning at [specs/artifacts.json](/Users/petepetrash/Code/circuit-next/specs/artifacts.json:592).

Impact: not a runtime blocker, but it weakens the human-readable authority graph exactly where P2.4 needs clear artifact ownership.

Fix hint: update the companion doc or explicitly mark the roll-up as stale with current expected count.

**META Observations**

I did not find a CC#P2-1..P2-8 row with no named binding; the issue is binding strength, not missing governance. The aggregate failures are mostly boundary failures between layers that passed individually: phase title vs step dispatch, audit-time vs runtime enforcement, workflow result vs run result, and citation posture vs capability trigger. I did not run `npm run verify`; this was a static adversarial composition review with file/line evidence.

**Closing**

P2.1+P2.2+P2.3 is not yet load-bearing enough for P2.4 to add real dispatch authority. A fold-in slice should come first to settle the dispatch transcript event model, make explore’s adapter-use semantics explicit, resolve `explore.result` vs `run.result`, and harden CC#P2-7 trigger enforcement. After that, P2.4 can touch `src/runtime/adapters/agent.ts` without smuggling architectural decisions into the first privileged runtime slice.
ding). Dry-run agent adapter: `DispatchStartedEvent` is emitted with `adapter: {kind: 'builtin', name: 'agent'}`, `resolved_from: {source: 'default'}`, `resolved_selection: {skills: [], invocation_options: {}}`; verdict synthesized from `dispatch step's gate.pass[0]` (deterministic pass). No real agent dispatch — the plan's §Scope boundaries explicitly reserve that for post-Alpha-Proof. Second alternate: hand-parse events.ndjson in the smoke test to extract kinds rather than trusting `runDogfood`'s returned `events[]`. Rejected because the smoke test separately asserts `readRunLog(runRoot)` reconstructs the same log length and validates through the schema — verifying disk parity without duplicating the trusted-return-path check. **Trajectory check:** arc goal served = close Phase 1.5 Alpha Proof on executable product proof (not design-only evidence); phase goal served = dogfood-run-0 closes criteria #4/#5/#6/#7/#13 so Phase 2 entry is gated on runnable product; earlier-slice impact = 27c runtime boundary surfaces (`event-writer`, `reducer`, `snapshot-writer`, `manifest-snapshot-writer`) composed UNCHANGED per constraint — 27d only adds the orchestrator + CLI + fixture/manifest/smoke test layer; 27b inventory detector is the mechanical delta oracle that made the id-flip assertions falsifiable; 27a mining's warning against cherry-picking spike `reducer.ts` held — production reducer remained the source of truth; 26b status-epoch checks catch marker misalignment at commit time (Check 18 flips red → green the moment `slice-27d:` becomes HEAD subject); no earlier slice has made 27d smaller, obsolete, or mis-sequenced. Acceptance evidence: append-only event writer at `src/runtime/event-writer.ts` (atomic `openSync('a')` + `writeSync`, writer contract not schema property; tests assert no overwrite/truncation of prior events); reducer-derived snapshot writer at `src/runtime/snapshot-writer.ts` authored fresh against production `src/schemas/snapshot.ts` `Snapshot` per Slice 26a artifact binding (NOT a cherry-pick of spike `reducer.ts` — that would silently regress Slice 26a's binding of `run.snapshot` to `<run-root>/state.json`); `src/runtime/reducer.ts` pure `reduce(RunLog) -> Snapshot` with `fold(applyEvent, seedFromBootstrap)` decomposition (spike lesson 10) covering all 11 event variants; `src/runtime/manifest-snapshot-writer.ts` + new `src/schemas/manifest.ts` schema with parse-time `sha256(bytes_base64) === hash` superRefine gate — byte-for-byte hash mismatch rejected loudly; `src/runtime/runner.ts` + `src/runtime/event-log-reader.ts` compose the boundary. Tests 665 → 686 (+21: 15 in new `tests/unit/runtime/event-log-round-trip.test.ts` covering append-only / reducer-derived-mismatch / byte-for-byte-manifest / corrupt-hash-rejection / reducer purity / RunLog-round-trip / state.json-is-Snapshot-not-RunProjection / RunProjection.safeParse succeeds; 6 in new `tests/contracts/slice-27c-runtime-boundary.test.ts` asserting the 5 inventory id flips + the 2 new artifact rows + the ≤2 schema-files boundary claim). **Hash algorithm decided at framing:** SHA-256 over the exact persisted manifest snapshot bytes (`algorithm: 'sha256-raw'`) per ADR-0001 Addendum B §Phase 1.5 Close Criteria #8 default — no stricter ADR authored this slice; JCS (RFC 8785) canonicalization would have required governance overhead (ADR + review) without clear dogfood-level benefit, and the 27a mining §Decisions 27c Must Make at Framing #1 flagged JCS adoption as a future Phase 2 concern if replay determinism across formatters becomes load-bearing. **Fastest-Falsifier ≤2 boundary preserved:** 2 new `specs/artifacts.json` rows (`run.manifest_snapshot` binding `src/schemas/manifest.ts` to `<run-root>/manifest.snapshot.json`; `run.result` binding `src/schemas/result.ts` to `<run-root>/artifacts/result.json`) + 2 new `src/schemas/*.ts` files (`manifest.ts`, `result.ts`). `specs/contracts/run.md` `artifact_ids` frontmatter extended to bind the 2 new rows. The writer surfaces are runtime code, not data shapes — the event log DATA artifact stays `run.log` + the snapshot DATA artifact stays `run.snapshot`, both unchanged; the 27b regex-over-ids heuristic was over-narrow for that semantic split, and the refinement preserves detection strength (placeholder modules still reject) while keeping the ≤2 boundary intact. Malformed-tail policy (27a mining §Decisions #2): **durable-only in v0**; `readEventLogRaw` rejects any malformed line loudly (including the tail) because `appendEvent` is atomic per line — a malformed line indicates crash mid-write, not normal tail, and Phase 2 can add the durable/transient distinction if crash-recovery becomes a real concern. Failure mode addressed: without a mechanical, executable product-surface inventory at HEAD, 27c/27d acceptance has no truthful delta oracle — a placeholder checklist (or a reviewer eyeballing paths) cannot reliably reject surfaces that "exist" as empty files, echo-only npm scripts, or test files with zero `it()` blocks; the same "both stale is green" class that Slice 26b Check 18 closed for status docs must be closed for runtime surfaces too, before dogfood writes real bytes. Acceptance evidence: `scripts/inventory.mjs` ships with 10 deterministic detectors (circuit:run script / plugin manifest / dogfood workflow fixture / src/runtime entrypoint / event writer / snapshot writer / manifest snapshot / runner smoke test / event-log round-trip test / README+PROJECT_STATE+TIER status alignment) + `buildInventory` / `renderReport` / `renderMarkdown` exports; `npm run inventory` writes `reports/product-surface.inventory.json` + `reports/product-surface.inventory.md` (`schema_version: "1"`, `slice: "27b"`, `baseline: true`); at HEAD exactly 1/10 surface is present (`docs.status_alignment: current_slice=27b across README/PROJECT_STATE/TIER`); 9/10 absent rows (runner/writer/reducer/manifest/fixture/smoke/round-trip/plugin-manifest/circuit:run-script) record the expected_evidence 27c+27d will satisfy. Placeholder rejection is structural: empty files (size/non-comment-length threshold), echo/true/noop first-tokens, JSON missing required `name`/`version` / empty-object manifests, artifact rows whose `backing_paths` are only persisted `<run-root>/...` placeholders with no repo-local schema_file, and test files with no `it/test/describe(` block all resolve to `present: false`. Alternate framing: embed detection into `scripts/audit.mjs` as a new Check — rejected because (a) audit emits red/yellow/green tri-state, not structured JSON amenable to cross-commit diff, (b) the plan explicitly names `scripts/inventory.mjs` + `reports/product-surface.inventory.{json,md}` as deliverables and deviating would itself be a ratchet mutation requiring an ADR, (c) delta semantics want stable serialization independent of audit formatting. Second alternate: generate inventory on the fly in 27c/27d tests without committing reports — rejected because an uncommitted baseline cannot be diffed cross-commit and leaves no pre-27c evidence for future auditors. Trajectory check: arc goal served = dogfood-run-0 delta oracle substrate; phase goal served = Phase 1.5 Alpha Proof (D6 "placeholder entries fail" + Product Reality Gate mechanical evidence); earlier-slice impact = 26b's `extractCurrentSliceMarker` + status-header-zone scanning is reused by the inventory's `checkStatusAlignment` detector (not re-implemented), and 27a mined-but-not-adopted spike lessons remain neutral on 27b (inventory records surfaces, not design choices); no earlier slice has made 27b smaller, obsolete, or mis-sequenced.
